EDCS-05502276.translation: To Vienislivilla, freedwoman of Ntaies Ambatus, aged 50.
EDCS-05502276.summary: This inscription records Vienislivilla, freedwoman of Ntaies Ambatus, who died at 50.

EDCS-05502280.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Valerius Firmanus, veteran of the Legio VII Gemina Pia Felix, and Lucilia Patera, wife of Valeria Kara, daughter, most innocent, aged 8 years and 11 days.
EDCS-05502280.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Gaius Valerius Firmanus, a veteran, and his wife Lucilia Patera, commemorating their daughter Valeria Kara who died at age 8.

EDCS-05502283.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Memmius Probus, a Latin grammarian, to whom the Republic of the Tritians established a salary of 1100 denarii for having them for 25 years. May the earth lie lightly on you here.
EDCS-05502283.summary: This inscription records the endowment of a salary for Lucius Memmius Probus, a Latin grammarian who served the community of Tritium for 25 years.

EDCS-05502301.translation: To Titus Magilius Rectugeni, son of Uxama Argaela, aged 30. He is placed here.
EDCS-05502301.summary: This tomb inscription commemorates Titus Magilius Rectugeni, son of Uxama Argaela, who died at 30.

EDCS-05502302.translation: Cornelius Paternus had this made for his most devoted wife Cornelia Ambada, aged 50.
EDCS-05502302.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Ambada, erected by her husband Cornelius Paternus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502304.translation: Calpurnius had this made for his grandfather Gaius Calpurnius Lupus.
EDCS-05502304.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Calpurnius Lupus, erected by his grandson Calpurnius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502323.translation: Antionius Flavius of Neviensis, a carpenter, made a vow for the health of Estiter (while working) on a beam.
EDCS-05502323.summary: A votive inscription by Antonius Flavius, a carpenter, for the health of Estiter, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502356.translation: Turaesamus, son of Cantaber, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-05502356.summary: A funerary epitaph for Turaesamus, son of Cantaber, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502358.translation: Gaius Calpurnius Lupus, chief magistrate of Pompeii, greetings. You have the power to enforce your magistracy against stubborn individuals, and furthermore, those who fail to take securities will know that they will not be safe on this account, for the risk of unaccepted securities will fall upon them, and whatever actions are taken by those present will be a common burden. Farewell. Given on the Ides of October, during the third consulship of Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus.
EDCS-05502358.summary: A legal decree or ruling issued by Claudius Quartinus, IIvir of Pompeii, regarding the enforcement of magistracy and securities, dated to 133 AD.

EDCS-05502372.text_edition: \n Caio Atilio Luci filio Quirina Geniali Atilia Festa avo Lucio Atilio Cai filio Quirina Festo Atilia Festa patri optimo Atilia Luci filia Festa et sibi se viva fecit\n
EDCS-05502372.translation: Atilia Festa and herself, while alive, made this for Gaius Atilius Lucius son, Quirina, named Genialis; for his grandfather Lucius Atilius Gaius son, Quirina, named Festus; and for her father Atilius Lucius son, Festa.
EDCS-05502372.summary: A funerary epitaph likely commissioned by Atilia Festa for her family members, including her father and grandfather, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502373.translation: Cornelia Placida laid to rest here Gaius Atilius Aquilo, son of [---].
EDCS-05502373.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Atilius Aquilo, set up by Cornelia Placida, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502375.text_edition: \n Caius Plotius Cai filius Siaco hic situs est Fabia Ederettatia C Irurciradin\n
EDCS-05502375.translation: Gaius Plotius son of Gaius, from Siaco, lies here. Fabia Ederettatia, daughter of [---], from Irurciradin.
EDCS-05502375.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Plotius and Fabia Ederettatia, likely from Roman Hispania.

EDCS-05502377.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antigonus et Homulla Semproniae Anti<g=C>onae matri posuerunt \n
EDCS-05502377.translation: Antigonus and Homulla placed this monument to the Divine Shades for their mother Sempronia Antigona.
EDCS-05502377.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Shades for Sempronia Antigona, erected by her children Antigonus and Homulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502378.text_edition: \n trahen tantos lus Patruinus s Sulia s Albus Abcellilac\n
EDCS-05502378.translation: Trahen tantos, son oflus, Patruinus, son of Sulia, Albus, son of Abcellila.
EDCS-05502378.summary: A fragmented inscription listing names and patronymics, possibly an epitaph or a record of individuals, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502380.text_edition: \n - Postumio Flacco Quirina Segiensi Lucius Sempronius Festus [fecit ------\n
EDCS-05502380.translation: Lucius Sempronius Festus of the Quirina tribe from Segienses made [this] for Postumius Flaccus [---].
EDCS-05502380.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Sempronius Festus for Postumius Flaccus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502383.text_edition: \n Iulius Longinus Doles Biticenti filius Bessus eques alae Taurianae torquatae victricis civium Romanorum annorum XL aerum XXII hic situs est Sulpicius Susulla et Fuscus Bitius heredes ex testamento faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05502383.translation: Julius Longinus Bessus, son of Doles from Biticenti, a cavalryman of the victorious Tauriana Torquata wing, aged 40 years, 22 years of service, is buried here. Sulpicius Susulla and Fuscus Bitius, the heirs, saw to its construction according to the will.
EDCS-05502383.summary: This is a tombstone for Julius Longinus Bessus, a Roman cavalryman who served for 22 years and died at 40, erected by his heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502384.translation: Marcus Marius Fidus is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Marcus Marius Faustus, his brother, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-05502384.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Marius Fidus, with a common epitaph and a dedication by his brother, Marcus Marius Faustus, for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502385.translation: Terentia Vitalis, daughter of Gaius, wife of Publius Caecilius Vaelo, aged 32 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05502385.summary: This is a tombstone for Terentia Vitalis, wife of Publius Caecilius Vaelo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502386.translation: Sulpicius fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the household gods.
EDCS-05502386.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Laribus Vialibus (household gods), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502394.translation: To the departed spirits, for Martialis, my brother. [Lacunae indicating illegible text.]
EDCS-05502394.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Martialis, the brother of the deceased, with significant lacunae, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502395.translation: To the departed spirits. Hermopilus placed this for Octavia Aeroticena, his most rare wife.
EDCS-05502395.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Hermopilus for his wife Octavia Aeroticena, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502406.summary: This is a simple tombstone for Terentia Munica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502407.translation: For Marcus Clodius Flaccus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, flamen, military tribune of the IV Flavia Legion, a most outstanding man, the best citizen, for his many services to his public affairs, the citizens and inhabitants of Labitolosa.
EDCS-05502407.summary: This is a public inscription honoring Marcus Clodius Flaccus for his distinguished civic and military career, erected by the citizens and inhabitants of Labitolosa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502409.text_edition: \n Caio Licinio Cai filio Galeria Saturnino aedili IIviro flamini Porcia Publi filia Nigrina uxor\n
EDCS-05502409.translation: To Gaius Licinius Saturninus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen. Porcia Nigrina, daughter of Publius, his wife.
EDCS-05502409.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Licinius Saturninus, a local magistrate, by his wife Porcia Nigrina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502410.text_edition: \n Afrania Luci liberta Crhocale sic sita\n
EDCS-05502410.translation: Crocale, freedwoman of Lucius Afranius, is laid here.
EDCS-05502410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the freedwoman Crocale, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502411.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcia Tempestiva, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502413.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Servilia Severiana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502415.translation: Tertius, slave of Caennius Niger, seven years old, is laid here. Lucius Caennius Niger and Optata, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-05502415.summary: This is a tombstone for Tertius, a young slave, erected by his master and mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502416.translation: --- son of Lucius Arsac[---]inni and M[
EDCS-05502416.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription with partial names, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502421.translation: Lucius Cornelius Samius Aquensis, freedman of Philomus, is laid here.
EDCS-05502421.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Samius Aquensis, a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502426.translation: To Mars. Arruntius Initialis fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05502426.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a shrine or offering to Mars, made by Arruntius Initialis.

EDCS-05502427.translation: Sacred to Mars. Grattius Pyramus fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-05502427.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a shrine or offering to Mars, made by Grattius Pyramus.

EDCS-05502428.translation: To the Nymphs, thus Attalus of the Cornelii.
EDCS-05502428.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs, likely a private offering by Attalus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502432.translation: To Gnaeus Nonius, son of Gaius Nonius, from the tribe Quirina, Crescentius, as magistrate and priest of Rome and the Emperors, by decree of the town council. Gaius Nonius Sincerus [erected this] to his father.
EDCS-05502432.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Nonius Sincerus for his father, Gnaeus Nonius Crescentius, who held significant civic and religious offices, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502441.translation: To the gods of the underworld. To Nonius Suavetius Apuleianus, aged 14 years and 7 months. His father and mother, and his sister, had this made for their most devoted son and dearest brother.
EDCS-05502441.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named Nonius Suavetius Apuleianus, erected by his parents and sister.

EDCS-05502459.translation: Cantaber, son of Elguismicus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Mars the Great.
EDCS-05502459.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Cantaber to the god Mars.

EDCS-05502460.translation: Amia for Elariq(um) to the Lares.
EDCS-05502460.summary: A dedication to the Lares by Amia for Elariq(um).

EDCS-05502467.translation: Acilia Anneza made and dedicated this while alive for herself, for her husband Lucius Aemilius Maternus, and for her daughter Aemilia Materna; this place will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-05502467.summary: A funerary monument set up by Acilia Anneza for herself and her family, specifying that the plot is not inheritable.

EDCS-05502468.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Aemilius Faustus had this made while alive for his exceedingly indulgent wife Aemilia Pia and for himself.
EDCS-05502468.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Pia, erected by her husband Aemilius Faustus who also prepared a burial place for himself.

EDCS-05502472.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, endowed with tribunician power, father of the country, consul. The inhabitants of Toletum, most devoted to his divine majesty and his greatness, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502472.summary: A dedication from the city of Toletum to Emperor Philip the Arab, honoring his titles and authority.

EDCS-05502473.translation: Bbedo son of Canbar, aged 60 years, lies here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-05502473.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bbedo, son of Canbar, wishing him a light burial.

EDCS-05502474.text_edition: \n Caecilia Urca cara suis hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502474.translation: Caecilia Urca, dear to her family, lies here.
EDCS-05502474.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Caecilia Urca.

EDCS-05502475.translation: Of Pompeius Peregrinus, who died abroad, aged 30. His colleagues made this.
EDCS-05502475.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeius Peregrinus, erected by his colleagues after his death abroad.

EDCS-05502479.translation: Annia Diodora, dear to her family, aged 60. Marcus Geminius Mamma[tus(?)] (or Mamma[rius?]), dear to his family, aged 20. They lie here. May the earth lie light upon you.
EDCS-05502479.summary: A funerary epitaph for Annia Diodora and Marcus Geminius Mammatus (or Mammaius), wishing them a light burial.

EDCS-05502480.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Levinus, son, erected this for his father, Gaius Valerius Pompeianus.
EDCS-05502480.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his father, Gaius Valerius Pompeianus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502484.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Symmacho Quirina annorum LXXXX sit tibi terra levis Aeliae Ponpei sic ucsori sic annorum XX\n
EDCS-05502484.translation: To Lucius Aelius Symmachus, of the Quirina tribe, aged 90. May the earth lie lightly upon you. To Aelia Pompeia, his wife, aged 20.
EDCS-05502484.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the deaths of a man, Lucius Aelius Symmachus, and his wife, Aelia Pompeia, with their ages and tribal affiliations, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502485.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vitalis, husband, and his son Minicius Chrestus had this made for Minic<i=E>a Chreste<ni>, aged 59. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502485.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a husband and son for Minic<i=E>a Chreste<ni>, detailing her age and including the common wish for a light grave, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502487.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Rufina made this for her dearest and most devoted husband Myrismus, who lived 75 years. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502487.summary: A funerary epitaph by a wife, Rufina, for her husband, Myrismus, stating his age and including the traditional wish for a light grave, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502489.text_edition: \n Pro salute ordinis Valerius Natalis Maternus III VISISMEV ponoSTEOTA VII VIII M VAIE Torquato et Magio consulibus convivant semper feliciter\n
EDCS-05502489.translation: For the well-being of the council, Valerius Natalis Maternus [erected this inscription during the consulship of Torquatus and Magius]. May they always live happily.
EDCS-05502489.summary: An inscription dedicating something for the welfare of a council (ordo) during the consulship of Torquatus and Magius, likely from the late Republic or early imperial period.

EDCS-05502491.text_edition: \n Dianae Quintia Mani Valeriani serva ex voto\n
EDCS-05502491.translation: To Diana, by Quintia Mani, a servant of Valerianus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-05502491.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Diana by a freedwoman or slave named Quintia Mani, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502492.translation: To Diana, by Cassia Margarita. She fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502492.summary: A votive inscription to Diana by Cassia Margarita, who fulfilled her vow willingly, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502500.translation: [Erected by] C() Bessuca for her son. She fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502500.summary: A votive inscription by C() Bessuca for her son, where she willingly fulfilled her vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502501.translation: To Lumiis, from a vow. Primi<g=C>enius Liti<q=C>(um) [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-05502501.summary: A votive inscription potentially to a deity named Lumiis, by Primi<g=C>enius Liti<q=C>(um), likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502502.translation: To Mercury, by Tiberius Claudius Prosodus. He fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05502502.summary: A votive inscription to Mercury by Tiberius Claudius Prosodus, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-05502503.translation: Albanus... willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05502503.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely a votive offering, by an individual named Albanus, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-05502505.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Livia, referred to as 'grandmother'.

EDCS-05502506.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, grandson of Augustus, great-grandson of the deified [Augustus]. Lucius Turellius Geminus, son of Lucius, the aedile, [erected this] at his own expense.
EDCS-05502506.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Drusus Caesar, erected at his own expense by Lucius Turellius Geminus, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-05502507.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius, grandson of Augustus, great-grandson of the deified [Augustus], consul. Lucius Turellius Geminus, son of Lucius, the aedile, [erected this] at his own expense.
EDCS-05502507.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Germanicus Caesar, erected at his own expense by Lucius Turellius Geminus, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-05502508.translation: ...to Germanicus... with tribunician power for the third time... father of the fatherland.
EDCS-05502508.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription honoring Germanicus, mentioning his tribunician power and title 'father of the fatherland'.

EDCS-05502517.translation: ...for his daughter and for himself... alive he took care to have it made. The sodales Claudiani contributed 200 sesterces for the funeral. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502517.summary: This funerary inscription records a man providing for his daughter and himself, with a contribution from the sodales Claudiani for the funeral expenses and a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-05502518.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a name possibly starting with 'Laur' and referring to 'companions' (sodales).

EDCS-05502519.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription that appears to mention 'companions' (sodales).

EDCS-05502521.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Rufus, his son, took care to have this made for Philumenus, the physician. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502521.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Rufus to his father Philumenus, a physician.

EDCS-05502522.translation: To Quintianus, notary of the Caecili Porciani, aged 30 years. Manlius Victorinus and Fabia, his mother, [erected this] for their son.
EDCS-05502522.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Manlius Victorinus and his mother Fabia for their 30-year-old son Quintianus, who was a notary.

EDCS-05502523.translation: Julius Hilario and Montana Contucianco are buried here. Julius Hilario.
EDCS-05502523.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Julius Hilario and Montana Contucianco, with Julius Hilario mentioned again, likely as the primary individual or the one who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05502524.translation: Bruttius Satullus and Bruttia Festa [are buried here]. Fuscus [had this made] for his father and mother and for himself.
EDCS-05502524.summary: This is a tombstone indicating that Bruttius Satullus and Bruttia Festa are buried here, and Fuscus arranged for it for his parents and himself.

EDCS-05502526.translation: To Attius Saturninus of Valeriana, aged 26. Attia Ianuaria arranged for [this] for her dearest son; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502526.summary: This is a tombstone for Attius Saturninus, aged 26, erected by his mother Attia Ianuaria, dating likely to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502527.text_edition: \n Titus Valerius Kai sic Apti filius Galeria Valeriensis hic situs est Baebianae annorum \n
EDCS-05502527.translation: Titus Valerius Kai, son of Aptus, of the Galeria tribe, Valerian, aged [unknown], is buried here in Baebiana.
EDCS-05502527.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Valerius Kai, son of Aptus, a member of the Galeria tribe and from Valeriana, buried in Baebiana.

EDCS-05502530.translation: Aemilius Secundus arranged for [this] for his father, and his mother, and for himself, at his own expense.
EDCS-05502530.summary: This is a tombstone commissioned by Aemilius Secundus for his father, mother, and himself.

EDCS-05502531.translation: Iulia Peregrina arranged for [this monument] to be made for her husband Antonio Festivo and [her daughter] Iulia Candida, and for herself.
EDCS-05502531.summary: This is a tombstone commissioned by Iulia Peregrina for her husband Antonio Festivo, her daughter Iulia Candida, and herself.

EDCS-05502533.translation: To Caecilia Pamphila, aged 32 years, 10 months, and 16 days. Caecilius Barsamis [erected this] for his wife.
EDCS-05502533.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilia Pamphila, aged 32 years, 10 months, and 16 days, erected by her husband Caecilius Barsamis.

EDCS-05502535.translation: Celtibera, daughter of Gaius Julius Clumenus, aged 12, and Iulia Quieta, daughter of Gaius Julius Cornutus, are buried here. Gaius Julius Clumenus [had this made] for his wife and daughter; may the earth lie lightly on them.
EDCS-05502535.summary: This is a tombstone for Celtibera (daughter of C. Julius Clumenus) and Iulia Quieta (daughter of C. Julius Cornutus), erected by C. Julius Clumenus for his wife and daughter.

EDCS-05502536.translation: Hispanus Avelicus is buried here. For Fronto, his son, aged 16; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502536.summary: This is a tombstone for Fronto, aged 16, son of Hispanus Avelicus, with a wish for light earth.

EDCS-05502538.translation: [For] Mesicus, son of Cantabrus. Festus, Quieta, Optatus, the heirs, and for themselves.
EDCS-05502538.summary: This is a funerary dedication by heirs (Festus, Quieta, Optatus) for Mesicus, son of Cantabrus, and for themselves.

EDCS-05502540.translation: To Octavia Plias, daughter of Octavius. [Restored text]
EDCS-05502540.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia Plias, daughter of Octavius, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05502541.translation: To Nugio, son of Numerius Porcius Crastus, aged 3 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502541.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Nugio, son of Numerius Porcius Crastus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502542.translation: [Restored text]. Securus and Festa [erected this] for Hermione, their father and patron. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502542.summary: This funerary inscription records Securus and Festa dedicating a monument to their father and patron, Hermione, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502545.translation: [Restored text]. [He] lies here, aged 12 years. Sempronius Ce[restius? Restored text]
EDCS-05502545.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of an individual aged 12, possibly named Sempronius Cerestius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502547.translation: Gaius Valerius Hilarus, son of Nigrinus. [Restored text]
EDCS-05502547.summary: This inscription likely records Gaius Valerius Hilarus, son of Nigrinus, possibly a funerary or dedicatory text from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502548.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely containing Roman names such as Valerius and Saturninus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502555.translation: Videlis, daughter of Lucius Caecilius Achoristus, lies here, aged 6 years. [Restored text]
EDCS-05502555.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Videlis, daughter of Lucius Caecilius Achoristus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502556.translation: [Restored text], son of [Restored text]mius. May the earth lie lightly on you. [Restored text]
EDCS-05502556.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely containing a patronymic and a wish for the deceased, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502561.translation: [Restored text]. [He] lies here. [Restored text]va Vi[ Restored text]. May the earth lie lightly on you. [Restored text]
EDCS-05502561.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription containing a statement of burial and a common epitaph formula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502566.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that mentions the name Rectinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502583.text_edition: \n Ex reditu pecuniae quam C(aius) Iulius Celsus rei publicae legavit decreto ordinis municipii Ercavicensium via facta HS Ccentum milibus nummum milia passuum VIII\n
EDCS-05502583.translation: From the return of the money which Gaius Julius Celsus bequeathed to the state, by decree of the council of the municipality of Ercavica, a road was made at a cost of 100,000 sesterces for 8 miles.
EDCS-05502583.summary: A municipal decree records the construction of an 8-mile road funded by a bequest from Gaius Julius Celsus.

EDCS-05502588.translation: To Lucius Terentius Crescens, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe; [erected by] Maria Ammia, [and] Lucius Terentius Firmus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, [and] Lucius Terentius Firmanus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 22. [Erected by] Valeria Severa, his mother-in-law, to her husband and son.
EDCS-05502588.summary: A funerary epitaph honors Lucius Terentius Firmanus, erected by his mother-in-law, Valeria Severa, and other family members.

EDCS-05502590.translation: To Gaius Marius Marianus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, and twice duumvir. [Erected by] Valeria Severa, daughter of Gaius, to her most devoted son.
EDCS-05502590.summary: A funerary epitaph honors Gaius Marius Marianus, a local magistrate, erected by his mother Valeria Severa.

EDCS-05502592.translation: Quintus Fabius Vegetus, who served in the 10th Praetorian cohort for 18 years, died at the age of 80. [Erected by] Lucius Fabius Vindex to his best friend. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502592.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorates Quintus Fabius Vegetus, a Praetorian guardsman, who died at 80, erected by his friend Lucius Fabius Vindex.

EDCS-05502593.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Aelius Hermerotes, charioteer, deceased at 23 years. Hermia, a municipal slave of Valerius, [erected this] to her incomparable son. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Frequent traveler, whoever passes by, read: I was born for you.
EDCS-05502593.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Hermerotes, a 23-year-old charioteer, erected by his mother Hermia, who encourages passersby to reflect on their own mortality.

EDCS-05502594.translation: Lucius Abilius Sabinus, from Limicus, aged 14. Fronto made this.
EDCS-05502594.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Abilius Sabinus, aged 14, who came from Limicus, erected by Fronto.

EDCS-05502595.translation: To Lucius Aelius Crescens, aged 27. Aelius Arabus ordered this to be made.
EDCS-05502595.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Crescens, aged 27, commissioned by Aelius Arabus.

EDCS-05502596.translation: Sextus Aelius Saturninus, son of Sextus, aged 25, lies here.
EDCS-05502596.summary: A simple tombstone marking the burial place of Sextus Aelius Saturninus, aged 25.

EDCS-05502597.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Annius Tertius.

EDCS-05502599.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Antonia Pithusa.

EDCS-05502600.translation: Caecilia Pyralis, 29 years old. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502600.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Caecilia Pyralis, with a common wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05502601.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Lucius Cornelius Secundus is buried here.

EDCS-05502603.translation: Eutychus, servant of Octavia, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502603.summary: A funerary epitaph recording that Eutychus, a freedman of Octavia, is buried here, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05502604.summary: A funerary epitaph likely naming the deceased Fabius Scipio.

EDCS-05502605.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Fabia Urbana is buried here.

EDCS-05502606.translation: Fabia Urbica, 45 years old, lies here.
EDCS-05502606.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Fabia Urbica.

EDCS-05502607.translation: Felicia Iunia, 23 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502607.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased, Felicia Iunia, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05502608.translation: Lucius Florentius. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502608.summary: A funerary epitaph naming Lucius Florentius, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-05502609.translation: Here lies Martilla, freedwoman of Caius Valerius Placidus.
EDCS-05502609.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying the deceased as Martilla, a freedwoman of Caius Valerius Placidus.

EDCS-05502610.translation: Octavia Ammica, daughter of Contucius, lies here.
EDCS-05502610.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying the deceased as Octavia Ammica, daughter of Contucius.

EDCS-05502611.translation: To Marcus Pompeius Cantabrus, aged 29. Pompeia placed his remains here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502611.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pompeius Cantabrus, aged 29, likely placed by Pompeia, with the standard closing wish.

EDCS-05502612.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for Pompeius Fortunatus, initiated by Pompeia.

EDCS-05502613.translation: Pomponia Melissa, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502613.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Melissa, aged 30, recording her burial and including the standard closing wish.

EDCS-05502614.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Popilius Gratus is buried here.

EDCS-05502615.translation: [...] Maria Recta, a servant, lies here.
EDCS-05502615.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Maria Recta, identified as a servant, recording her burial.

EDCS-05502616.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Lucius Sempronius Florus is buried here.

EDCS-05502617.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Terentius Martialis, aged 45, [...]
EDCS-05502617.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' (Spirits of the Underworld) for Terentius Martialis, aged 45.

EDCS-05502619.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. To Valerius Fuscus, aged 18. Valerius Festus placed this for his most dutiful grandson. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502619.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the 'Dis Manibus' for Valerius Fuscus, aged 18, set up by his grandfather Valerius Festus.

EDCS-05502620.summary: This funerary epitaph likely records the burial of Licinia, possibly associated with or for Vendalus.

EDCS-05502623.translation: [...] to Marius Valerius Valentinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe. Marius Porcianus, father, [...]
EDCS-05502623.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Marius Valerius Valentinus, son of Marcus, erected by his father Marius Porcianus.

EDCS-05502624.translation: Siceni, aged 30. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502624.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Siceni, who died at the age of 30, and includes the common epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502627.translation: For the safety and return of Luta, son of Festus, Phainus and Her[...], freely and deservedly, paid their vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-05502627.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, where Phainus and Her[...] offer a vow to Jupiter for the safety and return of Luta, son of Festus.

EDCS-05502636.translation: To Manlia Dorida, aged 35. Gaius Cornelius Primus for his most excellent wife and himself.
EDCS-05502636.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Cornelius Primus to his wife Manlia Dorida, who died at the age of 35, and also for himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502637.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Aelatius Auccarus, son of Aulus, aged 29, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05502637.summary: This funerary inscription records that Quintus Aelatius Auccarus, son of Aulus, aged 29, had this monument erected, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502640.translation: Publius Baebius Venustus, son of Publius Baebius Venetus, grandson of Publius Baebius Baesisceris, from Oretani, at the request of the order and the people, in honor of the divine house, built a bridge with 20,000 sesterces and dedicated it with public games.
EDCS-05502640.summary: This public works inscription records that Publius Baebius Venustus built a bridge for 20,000 sesterces, funded games, and dedicated it in honor of the imperial family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502642.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Porcia Tertiola, who died at the age of 37, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502643.translation: Publius Cot[3] Principis, son of [3]larcurie[3]/[3]us, lies here. May [3]VO LX [lie lightly]. Freedmen and heirs, from his will, had this made at their own expense through Cornelius [3]RAA and [3]REFIILVT.
EDCS-05502643.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Cot[3] Principis and states that his freedmen and heirs arranged for the monument to be built according to his will, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502644.translation: Lucius Julius Victor, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Victor, who died at the age of 25, and includes the common epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-05502647.translation: Lucius Livius Lupus, with his own money, made and dedicated a silver statue, with its base, to the genius of the municipality of Laminitani, on land granted by decree of the town council.
EDCS-05502647.summary: This public dedication records that Lucius Livius Lupus funded and dedicated a silver statue to the genius of the municipality of Laminitani, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05502648.translation: For Allia Candida, daughter of Marcus. Licinia Macedonica, her mother, supervised the collection. Clients and freedmen of the patroness placed [this monument].
EDCS-05502648.summary: This funerary inscription records that Licinia Macedonica, mother of Allia Candida, supervised the erection of a monument by her clients and freedmen.

EDCS-05502649.translation: To Publius Licinius Licinianus, brother, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, for his brother Publius Licinius Maximus, who was prefect of the 2nd Cohort of Gauls (cavalry) in Dacia and tribune of soldiers of the 7th Claudian Legion Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-05502649.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Publius Licinius Maximus, a military officer, and was erected by his brother Publius Licinius Licinianus.

EDCS-05502651.translation: Fabulla, daughter of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, and daughter-in-law, to Licinia Avita, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-05502651.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased Licinia Avita, honored by her daughter-in-law Fabulla.

EDCS-05502653.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, and great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 20th year, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, by the colony of the Libisosanorum.
EDCS-05502653.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, with a long titulature, and is issued by the colony of Libisosanorum.

EDCS-05502654.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpia Pia lovingly had made for her husband, Marcus Ulpius Gresianus, 45 years old, who was a clerk of inheritances, a clerk of the province of Lugdunensis and Aquitania, and a clerk of the province of Lusitania; may the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-05502654.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Ulpius Gresianus, a provincial clerk, and was set up by his wife Ulpia Pia.

EDCS-05502655.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 6th year, emperor for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05502655.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Antoninus Pius with his imperial titles and was made by decree of the local council (decurions).

EDCS-05502656.translation: To Publius Licinius Licinianus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, who was prefect of the 7th Cohort of Raeti (cavalry) in Germania, tribune of soldiers of the 22nd Legion Primigenia Pia Fidelis, and prefect.
EDCS-05502656.summary: This damaged funerary inscription honors Publius Licinius Licinianus, a military officer who served in multiple commands, likely erected by family members.

EDCS-05502657.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Norbana Maxima, 40 years old. Acinippius.
EDCS-05502657.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the deceased Norbana Maxima and was likely erected by Acinippius.

EDCS-05502659.translation: Annia Victorina, daughter of Lucius, for the memory of her husband Marcus Fulvius Moderatus and her son Marcus Fulvius Victorinus, brought water with all her expense, having constructed bridges and pipes and a reservoir with its ornaments, and having provided a feast, she dedicated it.
EDCS-05502659.summary: Annia Victorina funded the construction of a water supply system with bridges, pipes, and a reservoir in memory of her husband and son.

EDCS-05502660.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-05502660.summary: This fragment records Marcus Fulvius Victor, likely a funerary dedication with further details now lost.

EDCS-05502662.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Filon, 25 years old, pious towards his family. His most devoted parents erected this in his memory. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502662.summary: This funerary inscription records Filon, aged 25, and was set up by his devoted parents.

EDCS-05502663.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Here lies Laetus, aged 25, pious to his family. The urban guild erected this monument to him. Here he lies buried. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502663.summary: A funerary inscription for Laetus, erected by his urban guild, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502664.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Dis Manibus sacrum Pravie annorum XXXV sit terra levis\n
EDCS-05502664.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pravia, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502664.summary: A funerary inscription for Pravia, stating her age and a wish for a light burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502665.translation: aged 37. Here he lies buried. May the earth lie lightly on you. Serenus VIR BLVS B[... erected] this monument.
EDCS-05502665.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for Serenus, mentioning his age and possibly his profession or status, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502676.translation: Quartulus, aged 9. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502676.summary: A funerary inscription for Quartulus, a child aged 9, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502690.translation: of the treasury and curator of the divine Titus in Baetica, prefect of Gallia, prefect of the treasury of the Caesars of the Emperors of Germany, tribune of the 8th legion, Augustal priest in Baetica, first [served]...
EDCS-05502690.summary: A fragmented inscription detailing a career of an imperial official with various administrative and military roles, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502693.translation: Lucius Septimius Sempronianus, son of Quintus, chief centurion of the 14th Gemina Victrix legion, for himself and for his most beloved wife Fulvia Fuscilla, daughter of Gaius, with whom he lived for 12 years, 2 months, and 6 days, without complaint.
EDCS-05502693.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Septimius Sempronianus and his wife Fulvia Fuscilla, detailing their marriage duration and his military rank, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502694.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Annio Simplex, soldier of the 7th Gemina Felix legion. Valerius Festus, his heir, erected [this].
EDCS-05502694.summary: A funerary inscription for Annio Simplex, a soldier of the 7th Legion, erected by his heir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502706.translation: lived 71 years, 5 months, and 12 days. Aurelius Priscus for his most dutiful father, and Valeria Quintilla his wife, and Zosimus and Onysianus, freedmen, erected this altar with its ornaments. Here he lies buried. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502706.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelius Priscus' father, erected by his son, wife, and freedmen, recording his age and burial details, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05502708.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you. Canula lies buried here.
EDCS-05502708.summary: A simple funerary inscription for Canula, stating her burial and a wish for a light resting place, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502709.translation: Chrisis, aged 16, pious to her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05502709.summary: A funerary inscription for a young woman named Chrisis, aged 16, highlighting her piety, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502779.text_edition: \n Caio Valerio Cai filio Pupinia Restituto cui splendidissimus ordo locum et statuam decrevit Valeria Hygia mater honore accepto impensam remisit\n
EDCS-05502779.translation: To Gaius Valerius Restitutus, son of Gaius, of the Pupinia tribe, to whom the most distinguished council decreed a place and a statue, Valeria Hygia, his mother, having received the honor, remitted the expense.
EDCS-05502779.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription recording the posthumous honors granted to Gaius Valerius Restitutus by the local council, with his mother covering the costs.

EDCS-05502791.text_edition: \n Lucius Baebius Marci filius Lucius Catius Marci filius Lucius Taurius Luci filius Servius Aefolanus filius Genio oppidi columnam pompam ludosque coiraverunt\n
EDCS-05502791.translation: Lucius Baebius, son of Marcus, Lucius Catius, son of Marcus, Lucius Taurius, son of Lucius, and Servius Aefolanus, son of [unnamed father], arranged for the column, procession, and games for the Genius of the town.
EDCS-05502791.summary: This inscription records a communal religious and civic event, detailing the contributions of four individuals towards a column, procession, and games to honor the town's Genius.

EDCS-05502792.text_edition: \n Herculei Gaditano Lucius Avius Eclectus votum solvit libentes merito\n
EDCS-05502792.translation: To Gaditan Hercules, Lucius Avius Eclectus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05502792.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Gaditanus by Lucius Avius Eclectus.

EDCS-05502793.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae Caius Valerius Felix ex voto dono dedit\n
EDCS-05502793.translation: To Augusta Victoria, Gaius Valerius Felix gave this gift according to his vow.
EDCS-05502793.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a gift to Augusta Victoria by Gaius Valerius Felix.

EDCS-05502795.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae consuli IIII pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate conventus Carthaginensis curante Postumio Clariano flamine\n
EDCS-05502795.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, consul for the fourth time, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, from the district of Carthago, under the supervision of Postumius Clarianus, the flamen.
EDCS-05502795.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely related to the Roman district of Carthago, indicating his various titles and offices.

EDCS-05502796.text_edition: \n Iuliae Avitae Mammeae Augustae matri domini nostri sanctissimi Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae et universi generis humani conventus Karthaginensis\n
EDCS-05502796.translation: To Iulia Avita Mammaea Augusta, mother of our most sacred lord Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and of the camp, and of the senate, and of the fatherland, and of all humankind, from the district of Carthago.
EDCS-05502796.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Iulia Avita Mammaea, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, by the district of Carthago, recognizing her titles and relationship to the emperor.

EDCS-05502797.text_edition: \n Publio Silio legato pro praetore patrono colonei\n
EDCS-05502797.translation: To Publius Silvius, legate pro praetore, patron of the colony.
EDCS-05502797.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Publius Silvius, recognizing his role as legate pro praetore and patron of the colony.

EDCS-05502798.text_edition: \n clausula a q s s p us quibus V reditum a Iuni Homulli legati Augusti pro praetore se praestare\n
EDCS-05502798.translation: Clause from a document concerning the income that Junius Homullus, imperial legate pro praetore, guarantees.
EDCS-05502798.summary: This is a legal or administrative inscription fragment referencing an agreement about income guaranteed by Junius Homullus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-05502799.translation: [Fragmentary] To Gaius Appuleius, son of Marcus, from the district...
EDCS-05502799.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely honorary, mentioning Gaius Appuleius, son of Marcus, and the district.

EDCS-05502800.text_edition: \n Regi Iubae regis Iubae filio regis Iempsalis nepoti regis Caudae pronepoti regis Masinissae pronepotis nepoti IIviro quinquennali patrono coloni\n
EDCS-05502800.translation: To Juba, son of King Juba, grandson of King Iempsal, great-grandson of King Gauda, descendant of King Masinissa, twice great-grandson, IIvir quinquennalis, patron of the colonists.
EDCS-05502800.summary: This is a dedication to a royal figure, Juba, who held the office of IIvir quinquennalis and was a patron of the colonists, likely from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-05502801.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Marci filio Quirina Vindiciano flamini conventus Carthaginensis statuam decrevit conventus Carthaginensis\n
EDCS-05502801.translation: The conventus Carthaginensis decreed a statue to Marcus Valerius Vindicianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, flamen of the conventus Carthaginensis.
EDCS-05502801.summary: This inscription records the decree by the conventus Carthaginensis to erect a statue for Marcus Valerius Vindicianus, a local magistrate.

EDCS-05502806.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Marci filius Marci nepos Quirina Rectus domo Roma qui et Carthaginensis et Sicellitanus et Assotanus et Lacedaemonius et Argivus et Bastetanus scriba quaestorius scriba aedilicius civis adlectus ob honorem aedilitatis hoc opus testamento suo fieri iussit\n
EDCS-05502806.translation: Lucius Aemilius Rectus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Rome, who was also a Carthaginian, Sicilian, Assotan, Lacedaemonian, and Argive citizen, and a citizen of Bastetani, a quaestorian scribe, an aedile scribe, and admitted as a citizen because of the honor of the aedileship, ordered this work to be made by his will.
EDCS-05502806.summary: This funerary inscription details the extensive civic and honorary titles of Lucius Aemilius Rectus, who commissioned his tomb by will.

EDCS-05502807.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Marci filius Marci nepos Quirina Rectus domo Roma qui et Carthaginensis et Sicellitanus et Assotanus et Lacedaemonius et Argius et Bastitanus et scriba quaestorius scriba aedilicius civis adlectus ob honorem aedilitatis concordiae decurionum testamento suo fieri iussit Lucius Aemilius Senex heres sine deductione XX vel tributorum ex CCL libris argenti fecit\n
EDCS-05502807.translation: Lucius Aemilius Rectus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Rome, who was also a Carthaginian, Sicilian, Assotan, Lacedaemonian, Argive, and Bastitan citizen, and a quaestorian scribe, an aedile scribe, and admitted as a citizen because of the honor of the aedileship, ordered this work of concord among decurions to be made by his will. Lucius Aemilius Senex, the heir, made it from 250 pounds of silver without deduction of the twentieth tax or other taxes.
EDCS-05502807.summary: This funerary inscription, similar to EDCS-05502806, describes Lucius Aemilius Rectus's titles and the construction of a monument for the 'concord of decurions' by his heir.

EDCS-05502808.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Cornelius Luci filius Galeria Cinna IIvir murum longum pedes CII ex decreto decurionum faciendum curavit idemque probavit\n
EDCS-05502808.translation: Gnaeus Cornelius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Cinna, IIvir, arranged for a wall 102 feet long to be built by decree of the decurions, and he himself approved it.
EDCS-05502808.summary: This inscription records that Gnaeus Cornelius Cinna, serving as IIvir, oversaw and approved the construction of a 102-foot wall based on a decision by the decurions.

EDCS-05502809.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Marci filius Galeria Marcellus augur quinquennalis murum a porta Popilia ad turrim proximam pedes CXLVI et ultra turrim pedes XI decreto decurionum faciendum curavit idemque probavit\n
EDCS-05502809.translation: Marcus Cornelius Marcellus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, augur, quinquennalis, arranged for a wall 146 feet long from the Popilia gate to the next tower, and 11 feet beyond the tower, to be built by decree of the decurions, and he himself approved it.
EDCS-05502809.summary: Marcus Cornelius Marcellus, holding offices including augur and quinquennalis, commissioned and approved the construction of a specific section of wall according to a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-05502812.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Pollio as a IIvir quinquennalis.

EDCS-05502816.text_edition: \n Heisce magistris coirarunt sic Caius Poplicius Cai filius Lucius Cervius Luci filius Marcus Caeicius N Cai libertus Lucius Talepius Auli libertus Cnaeus Tongilius Publi libertus Lucius Paquius Noni libertus Silo Quinctio Verati Cai servus Philemo Pontilienorum Marci (et) Cai servus Quinctio Claudi Postumi Cai servus\n
EDCS-05502816.translation: Under these magistrates, Caius Poplicius, son of Caius; Lucius Cervius, son of Lucius; Marcus Caeicius, freedman of N. [Caelius?]; Lucius Talepius, freedman of Aulus; Cnaeus Tongilius, freedman of Publius; Lucius Paquius, freedman of Noni Silo; Marcus, slave of Quinctius Veratius; Caius, slave of Philemo; Caius, slave of Quinctius Claudius Postumus, supervised.
EDCS-05502816.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, including freedmen and slaves, who oversaw public works under specific magistrates.

EDCS-05502819.text_edition: \n Lucius Sulpicius Hel libertus Heliodorus VIvir Augustalis hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502819.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Heliodorus, freedman of Helis, VIvir Augustalis, is buried here.
EDCS-05502819.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sulpicius Heliodorus, a freedman and a member of the college of VIviri Augustales.

EDCS-05502820.text_edition: \n Octaviae Marci filiae Lucanae dominae optimae Marcus Fulvius Gillo Scribonius Fidus procurator eius loco dato ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05502820.translation: Marcus Fulvius Gillo and Scribonius Fidus, his procurator, [erected this] in the place given by decree of the decurions, to Octavia Lucana, daughter of Marcus, his excellent lady.
EDCS-05502820.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Fulvius Gillo and his procurator erected a monument in honor of Octavia Lucana, funded by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-05502821.text_edition: \n Marco Servilio Quinti filio Aniensi Sulpicio Victori Crispo Considiano \n
EDCS-05502821.translation: To Marcus Servilius Crispus Considius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe.
EDCS-05502821.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Marcus Servilius Crispus Considius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe.

EDCS-05502824.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Manilius filius Cornelianus operat \n
EDCS-05502824.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Shades. Manilius, son of Cornelianus, made this.
EDCS-05502824.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits by Manilius, son of Cornelianus.

EDCS-05502827.text_edition: \n Marcus Aimilius? --- Conobariensis qui fuit Vmis filius hoc monumentum conditivom se vivo fecit loco suo in fronte pedes CXX in agro pedes XC? --- sibei et sueis\n
EDCS-05502827.translation: Marcus Aemilius Conobariensis, son of Vmis(?), who was from(?) [---], made this monument for himself and his family while still alive, in his own place, 120 feet in front and 90 feet in area(?).
EDCS-05502827.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and tomb commission by Marcus Aemilius Conobariensis for himself and his family, specifying dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-05502828.text_edition: \n Marci Aemili Marci liberti Zenonis Atelliani\n
EDCS-05502828.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Zeno, freedman of Marcus, from Atella.
EDCS-05502828.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Marcus Aemilius Zeno, a freedman from Atella.

EDCS-05502829.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Mellusa, a freedwoman of Marcus.

EDCS-05502830.text_edition: \n Caius Apuleius Cai libertus Fronto hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502830.translation: Gaius Apuleius Fronto, freedman of Gaius, lies here.
EDCS-05502830.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Gaius Apuleius Fronto, a freedman of Gaius, is buried here.

EDCS-05502831.summary: This is a greeting inscribed on a monument, likely funerary, for Aquina, daughter of Stratonicius(?).

EDCS-05502832.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Atellius Cnaei libertus Theophrastus vixit cum fide\n
EDCS-05502832.translation: Gnaeus Atellius Theophrastus, freedman of Gnaeus, lived with faith.
EDCS-05502832.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Gnaeus Atellius Theophrastus, a freedman of Gnaeus, noting his faithful life.

EDCS-05502833.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Atellius Cnaei (libertus) Toloco hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502833.translation: Gnaeus Atellius Toloco, freedman of Gnaeus, lies here.
EDCS-05502833.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Gnaeus Atellius Toloco, a freedman of Gnaeus, is buried here.

EDCS-05502834.text_edition: \n Atellia Cnaei liberta Cleunica heic sita est\n
EDCS-05502834.translation: Atellia Cleunica, freedwoman of Gnaeus, lies here.
EDCS-05502834.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Atellia Cleunica, a freedwoman of Gnaeus, is buried here.

EDCS-05502836.text_edition: \n Mater si possem lubens fili vice morti succumberem nunc iaces hoc tumulo sit tibi terra levis Aucta Zenonis liberta mater Philocalo Fadenia soror de suo\n
EDCS-05502836.translation: If I could, mother, I would gladly die in your place; now you lie in this tomb. May the earth lie lightly on you. Aucta, freedwoman of Zenon, mother of Philocalus, and Fadenia, sister, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-05502836.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aucta, the freedwoman of Zenon and mother of Philocalus, and is dedicated by her sister Fadenia, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502837.text_edition: \n Lucius Baebius Luci libertus Saturio Rana hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502837.translation: Lucius Baebius Saturio, freedman of Lucius Rana, rests here.
EDCS-05502837.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Baebius Saturio, freedman of Lucius Rana, dating to the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502838.translation: Caius Caedius, son of Publius, of the Scaptia tribe.
EDCS-05502838.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, for Caius Caedius, son of Publius, of the Scaptia tribe, probably from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502839.text_edition: \n Caesii Cosmus et Iucunda Marco Caesio Cosmioni pio filio nnorum XX\n
EDCS-05502839.translation: Cosmus and Iucunda for their dear son Marcus Caesius Cosmius, aged 20.
EDCS-05502839.summary: This epitaph commemorates Marcus Caesius Cosmius, who died at the age of 20, and was dedicated by his parents, Cosmus and Iucunda, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502840.translation: Lucius Lucrio, freedman of Lucius Capri.
EDCS-05502840.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Lucrio, freedman of Lucius Capri, likely a funerary marker from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502841.text_edition: \n Titus Cassius Titi libertus Seleucus hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502841.translation: Titus Cassius Seleucus, freedman of Titus, rests here.
EDCS-05502841.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Cassius Seleucus, freedman of Titus, dating to the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502842.text_edition: \n Cassia Titi liberta Erotis viva sibi fecit\n
EDCS-05502842.translation: Cassia Erotis, freedwoman of Titus, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-05502842.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Cassia Erotis, freedwoman of Titus, for herself during her lifetime, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502843.text_edition: \n Claudio Longino qui vixit annis XXX fratri bene merenti fratres fecerunt\n
EDCS-05502843.translation: For Claudius Longinus, who lived 30 years, a brother deserving well, his brothers made this.
EDCS-05502843.summary: This epitaph honors Claudius Longinus, who lived 30 years, and was dedicated by his brothers, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502844.text_edition: \n Caio Clodio Clodi Grathonis liberto Pamphilo libertus\n
EDCS-05502844.translation: Pamphilus, freedman, for Gaius Clodius Pamphilus, freedman of Gratho son of Clodius.
EDCS-05502844.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Pamphilus, a freedman, for Gaius Clodius Pamphilus, also a freedman, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502845.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Titus Didus and [wife?] of Publius.
EDCS-05502845.summary: This inscription appears to record the name Cornelia, daughter of Titus Didus and Publius, possibly a funerary marker from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502846.translation: Lucius Fabricius Fimbria, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05502846.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Fabricius Fimbria, son of Lucius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-05502847.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Firmus annorum VII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502847.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Firmus, aged 7 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502847.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Firmus, a child aged 7, with a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-05502848.text_edition: \n Lucius Fufius Luci libertus Varus pius vixit salve\n
EDCS-05502848.translation: Lucius Fufius Varus, freedman of Lucius, dutiful, lived. Greetings.
EDCS-05502848.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Fufius Varus, a freedman, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-05502849.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Fulvinius Lalus annorum LXXIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502849.translation: Cnaeus Fulvinius Lalus, aged 73 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05502849.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cnaeus Fulvinius Lalus, aged 73.

EDCS-05502850.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Furio Marci filio Galeria Hilarus libertus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05502850.translation: Hilarus, freedman of Cnaeus Furius, son of Marcus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05502850.summary: This inscription records that Hilarus, a freedman of Cnaeus Furius Marci f., commissioned the monument.

EDCS-05502851.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Secunda, a freedwoman of someone named Cnaeus, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-05502852.text_edition: \n Caius Geminius Faustus annorum LXX hic situs est Caesilia Titi filia Cornelia annorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502852.translation: Caius Geminius Faustus, aged 70 years, is buried here. Caesilia Cornelia, daughter of Titus, aged 50 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05502852.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Caius Geminius Faustus (70) and Caesilia Cornelia (50).

EDCS-05502853.text_edition: \n Herennia Cai filia mortua est aetate XV nnorum hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502853.translation: Herennia, daughter of Caius, died at the age of 15 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502853.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial of Herennia, daughter of Caius, at the age of 15, with a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-05502854.text_edition: \n Marcus Insteius Marci libertus Epicrates pius\n
EDCS-05502854.translation: Marcus Insteius Epicrates, freedman of Marcus, dutiful.
EDCS-05502854.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Insteius Epicrates, a freedman, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-05502855.translation: To Marcus Laetilius Faustus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-05502855.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Laetilius Faustus, freedman of Marcus, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-05502856.translation: Nicephorus, freedman of Marcus Laetilius Marcus.
EDCS-05502856.summary: This funerary inscription records the name of Nicephorus, a freedman, likely for a tombstone.

EDCS-05502857.text_edition: \n Caius Licinius Cai filius Torax hospes consiste et Thoracis perlege nomen inmatura iacent ossa relata mea saeva parentibus eripuit fortuna meis me nec ivenem passast ulteriora frui nihil simile aspicias timeant ventura parentes neu nimium matres concupiant parere\n
EDCS-05502857.translation: Gaius Licinius Torax, son of Caius, guest, stand still and read the name of Thorax. Cruel fortune snatched my bones away from my parents while immature; she did not allow me, still young, to enjoy further life. May you see nothing like it. May future parents be afraid, and may mothers not desire too much to give birth.
EDCS-05502857.summary: This epitaph records the death of Gaius Licinius Torax, son of Caius, and expresses grief over his untimely demise and the sorrow of his parents.

EDCS-05502858.text_edition: \n Publius Lollius Publi libertus Philemo vixit probus probis Probata uxor monumentum fecit salve\n
EDCS-05502858.translation: Publius Lollius Philemo, freedman of Publius, lived an upright life; his wife Probata made this monument. Greetings.
EDCS-05502858.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Lollius Philemo, his upright life, and the monument erected by his wife Probata.

EDCS-05502859.text_edition: \n Lucretia Spuri liberta Polla sibi et suiis sic\n
EDCS-05502859.translation: Lucretia Polla, freedwoman of Spurius, for herself and her dependents. Thus.
EDCS-05502859.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucretia Polla, a freedwoman, dedicating a space for herself and her dependents.

EDCS-05502860.translation: Prima, freedwoman of the woman Lucretia. Greetings.
EDCS-05502860.summary: This inscription records the name of Prima, a freedwoman, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-05502862.text_edition: \n Luciae Luci filiae Maeliae mater pieta salve\n
EDCS-05502862.translation: Lucia, daughter of Lucius Maelius. Mother, in piety. Greetings.
EDCS-05502862.summary: This inscription records Lucia, daughter of Lucius Maelius, with a posthumous dedication possibly by her mother.

EDCS-05502863.translation: Of Titus, son of Marcus Spurius Rufus.
EDCS-05502863.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that likely records the name of Titus, son of Marcus Spurius Rufus, possibly for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-05502864.text_edition: \n Decimo Mario Decimi liberto Demetrio fratri\n
EDCS-05502864.translation: To Decimus Demetrius, freedman of Decimus Marius, and brother.
EDCS-05502864.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Decimus Demetrius, freedman of Decimus Marius, also identified as a brother.

EDCS-05502865.translation: Martha, freedwoman of Titus, lies here.
EDCS-05502865.summary: This funerary inscription states that Martha, freedwoman of Titus, is buried here.

EDCS-05502866.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Maticius |(mulieris) libertus Felix hic situs est Lucia ||(mulierum) liberta Aucta hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502866.translation: Gnaeus Felix, freedman of the woman Maticius, is buried here. Lucia, freedwoman of the women, is buried here.
EDCS-05502866.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gnaeus Felix, freedman of Maticius, and Lucia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-05502867.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Numisius Clemens, likely on a monument or tomb, with the name appearing in the dative case.

EDCS-05502868.translation: From Dorio, freedman of Cnaeus Numisius.
EDCS-05502868.summary: This inscription names Dorio, identified as the freedman of Cnaeus Numisius, possibly as a dedicant or the deceased.

EDCS-05502869.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Numisius Cnaei libertus Quinctio hic situs est\n
EDCS-05502869.translation: Cnaeus Numisius Quinctius, freedman of Cnaeus, lies here.
EDCS-05502869.summary: This epitaph records the burial place of Cnaeus Numisius Quinctius, a freedman of Cnaeus.

EDCS-05502870.summary: This is a salutatory inscription, wishing well to Numisia Philematio.

EDCS-05502871.text_edition: \n Numisia Cai liberta Secunda se viva fecit sibi et matri suae postea hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502871.translation: Numisia Secunda, freedwoman of Gaius, made this for herself while alive and for her mother afterwards, and is buried here.
EDCS-05502871.summary: This epitaph records that Numisia Secunda, a freedwoman of Gaius, erected the monument for herself and her mother during her lifetime.

EDCS-05502872.translation: Calliopa, freedwoman of Octavia (a woman), greetings, farewell.
EDCS-05502872.summary: This is a salutatory epitaph for Calliopa, a freedwoman of Octavia.

EDCS-05502873.text_edition: \n Octaviae Titi filiae Hiberae Pompei Flacci uxori Hibera mater\n
EDCS-05502873.translation: From Hibera, the mother, to Hibera, daughter of Titus, wife of Pompeius Flaccus.
EDCS-05502873.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial from Hibera, the mother, to her daughter Hibera, who was the wife of Pompeius Flaccus.

EDCS-05502874.text_edition: \n Cai Ofeli Cai liberte Apulloni salve\n
EDCS-05502874.translation: Greetings, Apullon, freedman of Gaius Ofelius.
EDCS-05502874.summary: This is a salutatory inscription addressed to Apullon, identified as the freedman of Gaius Ofelius.

EDCS-05502875.text_edition: \n Marcus Oppius Marci filius forensis ars hic est sita flet titulus se relictum\n
EDCS-05502875.translation: Marcus Oppius Forensis, son of Marcus, is here; this inscription laments that he was left behind.
EDCS-05502875.summary: This epitaph laments the death of Marcus Oppius Forensis, son of Marcus, and indicates he was 'left behind' (perhaps by companions or family).

EDCS-05502876.text_edition: \n Ovinia Cnaei liberta Laeta hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502876.translation: Ovinia Laeta, freedwoman of Cnaeus, lies here.
EDCS-05502876.summary: This epitaph records the burial place of Ovinia Laeta, a freedwoman of Cnaeus.

EDCS-05502877.text_edition: \n Plotia Luci et Fufiae liberta Prune haec vocitatast ancilla heic sitast haec qualis fuerit contra patronum patronam parentem coniugem monumentum indicat salve salvos seis\n
EDCS-05502877.translation: Plotia Prune, freedwoman of Lucius and Fufia, was called an attendant and is buried here; what she was like, the monument indicates in relation to her patron, patroness, parents, or spouse. Greetings, be well.
EDCS-05502877.summary: This funerary epitaph records Plotia Prune, identifying her social status and relationship to her patrons, and includes a greeting to passers-by, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502878.translation: Gaius Pomponius Philodamus, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-05502878.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph naming Gaius Pomponius Philodamus, a freedman, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05502879.text_edition: \n Primilla matri opse quens placi ta omnibus hic sita est annorum VII\n
EDCS-05502879.translation: Primilla is buried here, pleasing to her mother in every way, aged VII.
EDCS-05502879.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Primilla, who died at age seven, placed there by her mother, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05502880.summary: This is a posthumous dedication for Primilla, likely an addition to an existing monument or a marker for a burial plot.

EDCS-05502881.text_edition: \n Raia Semnis liberta Sophe annorum X hic sita est\n
EDCS-05502881.translation: Raia Sophe, freedwoman of Semnis, aged X, is buried here.
EDCS-05502881.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Raia Sophe, a freedwoman, who died at age ten.

EDCS-05502882.text_edition: \n Quintia Quinta annorum XXI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05502882.translation: Quintia Quinta, aged XXI, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502882.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Quintia Quinta, who died at age twenty-one, and includes the common wish for a light grave.

EDCS-05502883.text_edition: \n Filiolam amisit pater heu materque sequtast ipsa huius nomen Salviolae fuerat Marci Maesti Lucrionis verna annorum XXI\n
EDCS-05502883.translation: A father has lost his little son; alas, and the mother followed. Her name was Salviola, a household slave of Marcus Maestius Lucrio, aged XXI.
EDCS-05502883.summary: This poignant epitaph laments the loss of Salviola, a young household slave, detailing her family's grief and her age at death.

EDCS-05502884.translation: To the departed spirits, Scribonia, aged XXX.
EDCS-05502884.summary: This is a dedication to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) for Scribonia, who died at the age of thirty.

EDCS-05502885.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Agri Secundi et Corneliae libertae Moderatae et Agriae Gai filiae Silvanae\n
EDCS-05502885.translation: To the departed spirits, for Gai Agri Secundus, and for Cornelia Moderata, his freedwoman, and for Agria Silvana, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-05502885.summary: This is a dedication to the departed spirits, commemorating Gai Agri Secundus, his freedwoman Cornelia Moderata, and his daughter Agria Silvana.

EDCS-05502886.text_edition: \n Lucius Sulpicius Quinti filius Quinti nepos Collina hic situs est ille probatus iudicieis multeis cognatis atque propinqueis\n
EDCS-05502886.translation: Lucius Sulpicius, son of Quintus, grandson of Quintus, of the Collina tribe, is buried here; he was approved in many judgments by relatives and kin.
EDCS-05502886.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Sulpicius, detailing his lineage, tribal affiliation, and reputation for good judgment among his family and friends.

EDCS-05502887.summary: A simple greeting or epitaph for Terentia Cleunica.

EDCS-05502888.text_edition: \n Lucius Titinius Publi filius heic sepultus est ave et salve\n
EDCS-05502888.translation: Lucius Titinius, son of Publius, is buried here. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-05502888.summary: An epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Titinius, son of Publius.

EDCS-05502889.text_edition: \n Titinia |(mulieris) liberta Martha hic sitast ave vale\n
EDCS-05502889.translation: Titinia Martha, freedwoman of [a woman], is laid here. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-05502889.summary: An epitaph for Titinia Martha, freedwoman, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-05502890.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Turullio Cnaei liberto Prothymo ex testamento\n
EDCS-05502890.translation: To Gnaeus Turullius Prothymus, freedman of Gnaeus, according to the will.
EDCS-05502890.summary: An inscription indicating a burial or dedication made according to a will for Gnaeus Turullius Prothymus.

EDCS-05502891.text_edition: \n Valeria Luci liberta Erotis se vivo fecit\n
EDCS-05502891.translation: Valeria Erothis, freedwoman of Lucius, made this during her lifetime.
EDCS-05502891.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Valeria Erothis, freedwoman of Lucius, during her own lifetime.

EDCS-05502892.text_edition: \n Valeria Luci liberta Sufun--- heic sita est\n
EDCS-05502892.translation: Valeria Sufun[---], freedwoman of Lucius, is laid here.
EDCS-05502892.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Valeria Sufun[---], freedwoman of Lucius.

EDCS-05502893.text_edition: \n Caio Vario Cai liberto Proto exs testamento Vergiliae Quinti filiae Anui aue\n
EDCS-05502893.translation: To Gaius Varius Proto, freedman of Gaius, according to the will of Vergilia Anus, daughter of Quintus. Hail.
EDCS-05502893.summary: An inscription related to a will, commemorating Gaius Varius Proto, freedman of Gaius, established by Vergilia Anus.

EDCS-05502894.text_edition: \n Publius Vergilius Publi libertus Samnis hic situs est ave et vale\n
EDCS-05502894.translation: Publius Vergilius Samnis, freedman of Publius, is laid here. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-05502894.summary: An epitaph recording the burial of Publius Vergilius Samnis, freedman of Publius.

EDCS-05502895.text_edition: \n Vergilia Quinti filia Anus hic sitast exs testamento ave\n
EDCS-05502895.translation: Vergilia Anus, daughter of Quintus, is laid here according to the will. Hail.
EDCS-05502895.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Vergilia Anus, daughter of Quintus, is buried here according to a will.

EDCS-05502896.summary: An epitaph recording the burial of Vergilia Maxuma.

EDCS-05502897.summary: A very short inscription, likely an offering or dedication, identified by the name Gallus Paetus.

EDCS-05502900.text_edition: \n lius Luci libertus veio hic situs est ave et vale\n
EDCS-05502900.translation: Lucius Veio, freedman of Julius, lies here. Greetings and farewell.
EDCS-05502900.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Veio, a freedman of someone named Julius.

EDCS-05502903.translation: Gnaeus Atelius Philoxenus, freedman of Gnaeus.
EDCS-05502903.summary: An epitaph identifying Gnaeus Atelius Philoxenus as the freedman of a Gnaeus.

EDCS-05502906.translation: To the Genius of Augustus, son of a god. Lucius Trebius Menophilus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-05502906.summary: A dedication to the Genius of Augustus by Lucius Trebius Menophilus, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502920.translation: Gallius Fuscianus arranged for the construction of the curia at his own expense and also approved it.
EDCS-05502920.summary: This inscription records Gallius Fuscianus funding and approving the construction of a curia, a public building, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502932.translation: C. Festa is laid here, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502932.summary: A tombstone recording the death of C. Festa at the age of 55, with a common epitaph wishing her light earth.

EDCS-05502933.translation: Lucana is laid here, aged approximately 16. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502933.summary: A tombstone for Lucana, who died around the age of 16, with the standard epitaph wishing her light earth.

EDCS-05502934.translation: Gaius Postumius Fronto, aged 22, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502934.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Postumius Fronto, who died at the age of 22, with a common epitaph wishing him light earth.

EDCS-05502935.translation: Caelius Primus, aged 80, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05502935.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Caelius Primus at the age of 80, with a common epitaph wishing him light earth.

EDCS-05502937.text_edition: \n Augusto Divi filio Caius Maecius Cai filius Celer dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-05502937.translation: To Augustus, son of a god, Gaius Maecius Celer, son of Gaius, gave and dedicated.
EDCS-05502937.summary: A dedication to Emperor Augustus by Gaius Maecius Celer, son of Gaius, likely dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-05502938.text_edition: \n Tito Statilio Tauro imperatori III consuli II patrono\n
EDCS-05502938.translation: To Titus Statilius Taurus, Imperator for the third time, Consul for the second time, our patron.
EDCS-05502938.summary: This is a dedication to Titus Statilius Taurus, a prominent Roman official, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-05502940.text_edition: \n Lucretia Maura hic sepulta est nnorum \n
EDCS-05502940.translation: Lucretia Maura is buried here, aged [unknown].
EDCS-05502940.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucretia Maura, recording her burial and her age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502942.text_edition: \n Tadius Marci filius Rufus praefectus turres faciundas coeravit\n
EDCS-05502942.translation: Tadius Rufus, son of Marcus, prefect, saw to the construction of towers.
EDCS-05502942.summary: This inscription records that Tadius Rufus, a prefect, was responsible for the construction of towers, probably military or defensive structures.

EDCS-05502944.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Solaniano Severo Murenae filio magistro Marcus Popillius Onyxs IIIIIIvir Augustalis templum de sua pecunia restituit idemque probavit\n
EDCS-05502944.translation: Marcus Valerius Solanianus Severus, son of Murena, as magistratus, Marcus Popillius Onyx, an Augustalis IIIIvir, restored the temple at his own expense and approved it.
EDCS-05502944.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple by Marcus Popillius Onyx, an Augustalis magistrate, who also paid for the work, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502945.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Lolius Rufus, recording his age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502946.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Primigenia Sinphoniaca sic annorum XXV\n
EDCS-05502946.translation: To the Divine Manes. Primigenia Sinphoniaca, aged 25.
EDCS-05502946.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Manes for Primigenia Sinphoniaca, recording her age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502947.text_edition: \n Sicceia Publi liberta Donata Piero filio suo qui obit sic annorum XIIII hoc monumentum posuit\n
EDCS-05502947.translation: Sicceia Donata, freedwoman of Publius, placed this monument for her son Piero, who died aged 14.
EDCS-05502947.summary: This tombstone records Sicceia Donata setting up a monument for her young son Piero, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05502948.text_edition: \n Varro nnorum XVIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
