EDCS-07100152.summary: A funerary inscription for Iunius Aravus, son of Abili, placed by his grandchildren, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07100153.text_edition: \n Monime Necalica Laca Abrunaeni filiae Vadinienses Aeceius posuit omnes tres annorum XXX\n
EDCS-07100153.translation: Monime Necalica, daughter of Laca Abrunaeni, and the two Vadinienses, Aeceius placed [this] for all three, aged thirty.
EDCS-07100153.summary: A funerary inscription by Aeceius, commemorating three individuals, including Monime Necalica and two others from Vadiniensis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07100154.translation: To Pentius Festus, son of Doidus, from the Vadinian tribe, aged 20, lies here, placed by friends for his merits.
EDCS-07100154.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Pentius Festus, a young man, placed by his friends, likely dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-07100155.translation: Monument to Segis[unknown] Mar[unknown] of the Vadinian tribe, aged [unknown]. Here lies [unknown], placed by Docius(?) his father.
EDCS-07100155.summary: This partially preserved funerary monument records the burial of an individual from the Vadinian tribe, with a dedication by their father.

EDCS-07100157.translation: Monument to Andotus Ubalacinus, son of Vadon, of the Vadinian tribe, aged 25. Cludamus placed his friend here, where he lies.
EDCS-07100157.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Andotus Ubalacinus, dedicated by his friend Cludamus, likely dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-07100158.translation: Monument to Caelio, son of Amparus, of the Vadinian tribe.
EDCS-07100158.summary: This is a simple funerary monument dedicated to Caelio, son of Amparus, identifying him as belonging to the Vadinian tribe.

EDCS-07100159.translation: To the Shades Below, monument placed by Cestius, his son, to his father Cestius Boddo of the Vadinian tribe, aged 85. He lies here in the earth.
EDCS-07100159.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cestius Boddo, an elder of the Vadinian tribe, dedicated by his son.

EDCS-07100160.translation: Monument to Ambatus, son of Paramon, of the Vadinian tribe, aged 30. Origenus placed his kinsman here, where he lies.
EDCS-07100160.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Ambatus, a member of the Vadinian tribe, placed by his kinsman Origenus.

EDCS-07100161.text_edition: \n Munimentum sic Tedi vicani Vadiniensis Douiteri filii annorum XXX\n
EDCS-07100161.translation: Monument to Tadius, villager of Vadinius, son of Douiter, aged 30.
EDCS-07100161.summary: This inscription records the monument for Tadius, a villager of Vadinius and son of Douiter.

EDCS-07100162.translation: To the Shades Below, monument placed by Venus Labarus to his father Alius Pendiegino-pom, aged 30.
EDCS-07100162.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Alius Pendiegino-pom, dedicated by his son Venus Labarus.

EDCS-07100163.text_edition: \n Monumentum Tridio Alongun Bodeni filio Vadiniensi annorum XXV Fronto Doideri cum amico suo posuit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07100163.translation: Monument to Tridius Alongun, son of Bodnus, of the Vadinian tribe, aged 25. Fronto son of Doidorus, with his friend, placed him here, where he lies. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-07100163.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tridius Alongun, a young man of the Vadinian tribe, placed by Fronto and a friend, with a traditional wish for the deceased.

EDCS-07100164.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vadone Biracidegino Anemidi filio Vadiniensi annorum XXX Lugua Caddecun amico suo posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-07100164.translation: To the Shades Below, Vadon Biracideginus, son of Anemidus, of the Vadinian tribe, aged 30. Lugua Caddecun placed his friend here, where he lies.
EDCS-07100164.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Vadon Biracideginus, a member of the Vadinian tribe, placed by his friend Lugua Caddecun.

EDCS-07100165.translation: To Medugenus Valens, son of Magilo, aged 40, [died...]
EDCS-07100165.summary: This epitaph records the death of Medugenus Valens, son of Magilo, aged 40.

EDCS-07100166.text_edition: \n Manibus d Pentiocum Tridi filio Vadiniensi annorum XXX Arenus Andoti posuit\n
EDCS-07100166.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Pentiocum, son of Tridus, of the Vadinienses tribe, aged thirty. Arenus, son of Andotus, erected this.
EDCS-07100166.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Pentiocum, son of Tridus, aged thirty, erected by Arenus.

EDCS-07100167.text_edition: \n Manibus us Cadaricum ui filius Vad(iniensis) annorum XX aus filio posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-07100167.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Cadaricus, son of us, of the Vadinienses tribe, aged twenty. His son erected this for him; he rests here.
EDCS-07100167.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Cadaricus, son of us, aged twenty, erected by his son.

EDCS-07100168.translation: Monument for Lucius Antonius Aquila, son of Aquilus, of the Vadinienses tribe, aged 20. Lepidus, his nephew, erected this.
EDCS-07100168.summary: This epitaph records a monument for Lucius Antonius Aquila, son of Aquilus, aged 20, erected by his nephew Lepidus.

EDCS-07100170.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Arenus erected this for his father Manilius Andolus(?), son of Aliomigus, aged 50.
EDCS-07100170.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Manilius Andolus (?), son of Aliomigus, aged 50, erected by his son Arenus.

EDCS-07100192.text_edition: \n legio VII gemina ad portum Bledium Rhana VII milias Ammaia XVIII Villecia V legio V Octaviolca V Iuliobriga Aracillum V Rtus Rie IIvir\n
EDCS-07100192.translation: Legio VII Gemina at Portus Bledium, 7 miles; Rhana, 7 miles; Ammaia, 18 miles; Villecia, 5 miles. Legio V. Octaviolca, 5 miles. Iuliobriga. Aracillum, 5 miles. Rtus. Rie, roads official.
EDCS-07100192.summary: This is a road marker indicating distances to various locations from a point near Legio VII Gemina, with road official Rtus Rie credited.

EDCS-07100193.text_edition: \n Via Luco Augusti ad Iria ponte Martiae XI Brevis XIII Aseconia XI Iria X via Luco Augusti ad Dactonium Aquae Quintinae Dactonium Caius Lep M IIvir\n
EDCS-07100193.translation: Road from Lugo Augusti to Iria, at the Bridge of Martia, 11 miles. Brevis, 13 miles. Aseconia, 11 miles. Iria, 10 miles. Road from Lugo Augusti to Dactonium near Aquae Quintinae. Dactonium. Caius Lepidus, roads official.
EDCS-07100193.summary: This is a road marker indicating distances between several settlements on the road from Lugo Augusti to Dactonium, with road official Caius Lepidus credited.

EDCS-07100195.text_edition: \n Via Asturica ad Bracaram Argentiolum V milias Petavonium VIII Veniatia Compleutica XII Roboretum XII ad Aquas XV Aquis Originis VII Salacia X Bracara XII Caius Lep M IIvir\n
EDCS-07100195.translation: Road from Asturica to Bracara. Argentiolum, 5 miles. Petavonium, 8 miles. Veniatia, 12 miles. Compleutica, 12 miles. To Aquae, 15 miles. Aquis Originis, 7 miles. Salacia, 10 miles. Bracara, 12 miles. Caius Lepidus, roads official.
EDCS-07100195.summary: This is a road marker indicating distances between multiple settlements on the road from Asturica to Bracara, with road official Caius Lepidus credited.

EDCS-07400398.summary: This epitaph records the death of Avilie, aged 60.

EDCS-07400399.translation: Attianus Rufus to his wife Flaccilla, daughter of Flaccus.
EDCS-07400399.summary: This epitaph records the dedication by Attianus Rufus to his wife Flaccilla, daughter of Flaccus.

EDCS-07400400.text_edition: \n Iulianus et Caius Iulius Mustarus patri\n
EDCS-07400400.translation: Iulianus and Caius Iulius Mustarus to [their] father.
EDCS-07400400.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Iulianus and Caius Iulius Mustarus to their father.

EDCS-07400404.summary: This inscription records the age of Cloutina, daughter of Cloutus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-07400414.text_edition: \n Doviterus Triti filius annorum L Addouiesis monumentum hic? situs? est\n
EDCS-07400414.translation: Doviterus, son of Tritus, aged 50, the monument of Addouiesis is placed here.
EDCS-07400414.summary: This inscription marks the monument of Doviterus, son of Tritus, aged 50, likely a tomb inscription.

EDCS-07400415.translation: To Antonio, son of Lucius, aged 9.
EDCS-07400415.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, to a boy named Antonio.

EDCS-07400416.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, to Ocia, daughter of Tai.

EDCS-07400417.text_edition: \n Flacco Flacci filio nnorum XLV Albucio Silvani annorum XX\n
EDCS-07400417.translation: To Flaccus, son of Flaccus, aged 45, and to Albucius, son of Silvanus, aged 20.
EDCS-07400417.summary: This inscription appears to commemorate two individuals, Flaccus and Albucius, detailing their ages and parentage.

EDCS-07400436.text_edition: \n Asturicae sacrum Caesinius Agricola eques Flaviae I Lusitanorum curator\n
EDCS-07400436.translation: Sacred to Asturica by Caesinius Agricola, an equestrian officer and curator of the First Cohort of Lusitanians.
EDCS-07400436.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Asturica by Caesinius Agricola, an equestrian official.

EDCS-07400444.text_edition: \n Valerius Reburrus ex visu Laribus Vialibus sacrum (votum) reddidit libens merito\n
EDCS-07400444.translation: Valerius Reburrus willingly and deservedly paid a vow to the household Lares, after a vision.
EDCS-07400444.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Lares by Valerius Reburrus, prompted by a divine vision.

EDCS-07400446.text_edition: \n Marcellus et Caius Quinti Pentii centurionis filii equites alae I Augustae Marti votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07400446.translation: Marcellus and Caius, sons of Pentius, centurion, cavalrymen of the First Wing Augusta, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Mars.
EDCS-07400446.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mars made by two cavalrymen, Marcellus and Caius, sons of a centurion.

EDCS-07400457.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelio Marcello Popillia Vegeta faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07400457.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Popillia Vegeta arranged for this to be made for Cornelius Marcellus.
EDCS-07400457.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication arranged by Popillia Vegeta for Cornelius Marcellus.

EDCS-07400459.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Messio Abascanto Segontino Iulia Scintilla marito pientissimo et sibi\n
EDCS-07400459.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Scintilla sets this up for her most devoted husband, Marcus Messius Abascantus of Segontia, and for herself.
EDCS-07400459.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a wife for her husband and herself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400460.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a personal name or names from an otherwise lost context, dating potentially to the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-07400461.summary: This inscription records the name and age (partially restored) of an individual, likely from a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400464.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely recording a personal name, possibly from a monument or marker, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400468.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Fannius Aper votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-07400468.translation: Lucius Fannius Aper willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-07400468.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter by Lucius Fannius Aper, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400469.text_edition: \n Arconi Pompeius Placidus Mediugenicum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07400469.translation: Pompeius Placidus of Mediugenicum willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Arcon.
EDCS-07400469.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to a deity named Arcon by Pompeius Placidus of Mediugenicum, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400493.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare divi Nervae filio Nerva Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate \n
EDCS-07400493.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, as pontifex maximus, with tribunician power.
EDCS-07400493.summary: This inscription is a formal preamble citing the emperor Trajan and his titles, likely forming part of a larger official inscription from his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-07400496.text_edition: \n Gaio Grattio filio Nigrino aed(ili) IIIIviro flamini Augusti ex testamento Nigrini patris\n
EDCS-07400496.translation: To Gaius Grattius Nigrinus, son of Gaius, as aedile for the fourth time, as IVvir, and as flamen of Augustus, in accordance with the will of his father Nigrinus.
EDCS-07400496.summary: This inscription records the honors bestowed upon Gaius Grattius Nigrinus by his father's will, including civic and religious offices, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400497.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording a personal name and a magistracy (IIIvir), likely from an official or funerary context, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400498.text_edition: \n Saturnino Gai Gratti Glauci servo Anicetus contubernali\n
EDCS-07400498.translation: Anicetus, a fellow soldier, to Saturninus, slave of Gaius Grattius Glaucus.
EDCS-07400498.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a fellow soldier (contubernalis) to Saturninus, a slave, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400499.text_edition: \n Licini Gallionis annorum XLV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07400499.translation: Licinius Gallio, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07400499.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Licinius Gallio, aged 45, with a standard funerary formula.

EDCS-07400500.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Fla(v)us annorum LXXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07400500.translation: Lucius Aemilius Flavus, aged 90, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07400500.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Lucius Aemilius Flavus, aged 90, with a standard funerary formula.

EDCS-07400504.text_edition: \n Nezutus Caecili Lucani serbus sic hic situs est ]\n
EDCS-07400504.translation: Nezutus, slave of Caecilius Lucanus, is buried here.
EDCS-07400504.summary: A funerary inscription records the burial of Nezutus, a slave of Caecilius Lucanus.

EDCS-07400508.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Carcalia nnorum XVI pia in suis hic sita bene sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07400508.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Carcalia, aged 16, pious, rests here with her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07400508.summary: A tombstone honours the deceased Carcalia, aged 16, and invokes a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-07400511.text_edition: \n Tito Aemilio Veteri Minicia Saturnina mater pio filio annorum XXXV lucum fecit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07400511.translation: Minicia Saturnina erected this grove for her devoted son, Titus Aemilius Veter, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07400511.summary: A mother, Minicia Saturnina, dedicated a grove to her son Titus Aemilius Veter, aged 35, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-07400514.translation: Marcus Ummidius Celer [dedicated this] to Jupiter.
EDCS-07400514.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Ummidius Celer, possibly on a shrine or altar.

EDCS-07400535.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Furio Publi filio Quirina Montano Numisia Victorina uxor optimo marito\n
EDCS-07400535.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Numisia Victorina, wife, [dedicated this] to her excellent husband, Furius Publius filius Quirinus Montanus.
EDCS-07400535.summary: A funerary dedication by Numisia Victorina to her husband Furius Publius filius Quirinus Montanus.

EDCS-07600243.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Doidero Arcaun Boderi filio Vadiniensi annorum XXX Turanio Bodoecun amico suo posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-07600243.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Doideros, son of Arcaunos and of Vadin, aged 30, from Bodo, erected [this] for his friend Turanius Bodoecus. He is buried here.
EDCS-07600243.summary: A funerary monument erected by Doideros for his friend Turanius Bodoecus, with a dedication to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-07600248.text_edition: \n Valerius Larginus Anau hic sepultus est nnrum LXV et Postumia Caesia nnrum LV sibi cum uxore titulum fieri iussit\n
EDCS-07600248.translation: Valerius Larginus Anau, aged 65, and Postumia Caesia, aged 55, ordered this tombstone to be made for themselves together with their wife.
EDCS-07600248.summary: A funerary epitaph recording Valerius Larginus Anau (65) and Postumia Caesia (55) who commissioned their own tomb.

EDCS-07600249.summary: A partial inscription recording a dedication to the deity Pietas by someone named German.

EDCS-07600263.text_edition: \n Ambaicus Ambani filius annorum L hic situs est\n
EDCS-07600263.translation: Ambaicus, son of Ambanius, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-07600263.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Ambaicus, son of Ambanius.

EDCS-07600270.text_edition: \n Seconcus Seco filius annorum h(ic)? s(itus)? e(st)\n
EDCS-07600270.translation: Seconcus, son of Secus, aged [---], is [buried] here.
EDCS-07600270.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph likely recording the burial of Seconcus, son of Secus.

EDCS-07600282.summary: A very short inscription, likely naming two individuals, Medugenus and Carpa.

EDCS-07600284.text_edition: \n mito duuiqum Caii Caronis annorum C sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07600284.translation: May the earth lie lightly on you, Caii Caronis, aged 100.
EDCS-07600284.summary: A funerary epitaph wishing peace to Caii Caronis, who lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-08100570.text_edition: \n Manibus Popilius Luci filius Galeria Flaccus annorum XXXVII obiit frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08100570.translation: Popilius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, Flaccus, aged 37, has died. His brother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08100570.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Popilius Flaccus and stating that his brother arranged the monument.

EDCS-08100572.text_edition: \n Gaius Plotius Vegetus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08100572.translation: Gaius Plotius Vegetus is buried here.
EDCS-08100572.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Gaius Plotius Vegetus.

EDCS-08100573.text_edition: \n Publius Calpurnius Serhuhoris hic situs est\n
EDCS-08100573.translation: Publius Calpurnius Serhuhoris is buried here.
EDCS-08100573.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Publius Calpurnius Serhuhoris.

EDCS-08100574.text_edition: \n Genio Marci Carisi Blandi Carisius Callistus et Carisius Philotimus liberti\n
EDCS-08100574.translation: To the spirit of Marcus Carisius Blandus. Carisius Callistus and Carisius Philotimus, his freedmen.
EDCS-08100574.summary: A dedication to the 'genius' of Marcus Carisius Blandus by his freedmen, Carisius Callistus and Carisius Philotimus.

EDCS-08100586.summary: A brief inscription offering a greeting, possibly to a group of people or spirits.

EDCS-08200004.text_edition: \n Dianae ne Pompeianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08200004.translation: To Diana, Pompeianus gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08200004.summary: A votive inscription recording Pompeianus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-08200005.text_edition: \n Iulia Divis pro fortuna ex voto\n
EDCS-08200005.translation: To the goddesses, for good fortune, according to a vow.
EDCS-08200005.summary: This is a votive dedication to female deities made by an unnamed individual. It is likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200006.text_edition: \n Iovi Augusto Ultori sacrum Lucius Valerius Paternus miles legionis X geminae optio |(centuriae) Censoris exs sic voto\n
EDCS-08200006.translation: Sacred to Jupiter the Avenger. Lucius Valerius Paternus, a soldier of the tenth legion Gemini, optio in the century of Censor, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08200006.summary: This inscription records a military dedication to Jupiter Ultor by Lucius Valerius Paternus, a centurion from the tenth legion Gemini, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200007.summary: This is a simple dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly on a public or private monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200010.translation: To the Endeiterian Mothers, Titus Arrius Natalis.
EDCS-08200010.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Endeiterian Mothers by Titus Arrius Natalis, likely a votive offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200012.translation: To the Abascentian Mothers, from the household of Abascentus, according to a vow.
EDCS-08200012.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Abascentian Mothers made by the household of Abascentus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200013.text_edition: \n Arria Nothis matribus pro Secundo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08200013.translation: Arria fulfilled her vow to the Nothian Mothers for Secundus, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08200013.summary: This inscription records Arria fulfilling a vow to the Nothian Mothers on behalf of Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200017.translation: Festus, a slave, to the divine spirit of the theatre, for the public good of the people of Clunium.
EDCS-08200017.summary: This inscription records Festus, a slave, dedicating something to the theatre's spirit for the benefit of the city of Clunium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200018.text_edition: \n colona Fida S votum solvit libens merito Calvisius \n
EDCS-08200018.translation: The loyal colonist Fida fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly. Calvisius.
EDCS-08200018.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Fida, a loyal colonist, with Calvisius potentially being the dedicator or associated with the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200019.translation: To Minerva Verna, [he/she] fulfilled [his/her] vow, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08200019.summary: This is a partial record of a votive dedication to Minerva Verna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200023.translation: To Gaius Valerius Sereno, son of Gaius of the Galeria tribe; Valeria Atta, his aunt.
EDCS-08200023.summary: This inscription records a dedication or memorial to Gaius Valerius Sereno by his aunt, Valeria Atta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200026.translation: To Nio Fraterno, prefect of the cavalry wing.
EDCS-08200026.summary: This is a dedication to Nio Fraterno, prefect of an auxiliary cavalry unit, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200027.summary: This brief inscription records that Marcus S. T. erected this monument, possibly a tomb, in the Roman period.

EDCS-08200028.text_edition: \n Iulleia Araucia Iullei Philemonis liberta Caesaraugustina annorum XXXX Secundio eius servus annorum XVIII et Celadus servus annorum V hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-08200028.translation: Iulleia Araucia, freedwoman of Iulleius Philemon, 40 years old, Secundius, her slave, 18 years old, and Celadus, a slave, 5 years old, are buried here.
EDCS-08200028.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iulleia Araucia, her slave Secundius, and another slave named Celadus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200030.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to someone named Flaccus, son of Marcus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200032.translation: To Aiia Turellia, daughter of Gaius Turellius, 26 years old, killed by a slave. Gaius Turellius and Valeria [---]
EDCS-08200032.summary: This damaged funerary inscription records the death of Aiia Turellia, who was killed by a slave, and seems to be a memorial set up by Gaius Turellius and Valeria, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-08200034.translation: To Allae Aegguae, daughter of Ai, of the Missiciani tribe [---]
EDCS-08200034.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Allae Aegguae, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200035.text_edition: \n llonis filii onis filii o filio sibi \n
EDCS-08200035.translation: [---] son of Illo, son of Onis, son of O, for himself [---]
EDCS-08200035.summary: This very fragmentary inscription seems to refer to a paternal lineage and possibly a dedication for oneself, from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200039.translation: To Atilius Castor, 4 years old. Aponia Paternus, freedwoman.
EDCS-08200039.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Atilius Castor, and names his freedwoman patron, Aponia Patera, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200040.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a memorial set up by Atilius Eutychus for his son, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-08200044.text_edition: \n Carisiustis Voltinia Tolosa aerum XXI C Sabinus \n
EDCS-08200044.translation: [---] Carisius Voltinia, 21 years old from Tolosa. C. Sabinus [---]
EDCS-08200044.summary: This damaged inscription records the name, age, and origin (Tolosa) of Carisius Voltinia, with a mention of C. Sabinus, likely a funerary monument from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200052.summary: This inscription is a fragment that records the name Marcus, possibly part of a larger funerary monument.

EDCS-08200053.text_edition: \n Martialis nnorum XXII hic situs est Deuter et Sequens conservo de sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt qui legis diqas sic sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08200053.translation: Martialis, aged 22, is buried here. Deuter and Sequens had it made at their own expense, preserving the conservi. May the earth lie lightly upon you who read this.
EDCS-08200053.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Martialis, who was 22 years old, and erected by his fellow slaves Deuter and Sequens.

EDCS-08200056.text_edition: \n S D mi eius Lucius Paternius fratri et \n
EDCS-08200056.translation: To his god, Lucius Paternius, to his brother...
EDCS-08200056.summary: This is a dedication fragment, likely a funerary monument, from Lucius Paternius to his brother.

EDCS-08200057.text_edition: \n Gaio Petelio Paterno Gai Haerigi filio nnrum LVI Anna Maluga uxor marito\n
EDCS-08200057.translation: To Gaius Petelius Paternus, son of Gaius Haerigus, aged 56. Anna Maluga, his wife, to her husband.
EDCS-08200057.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Anna Maluga to her husband, Gaius Petelius Paternus, who died at the age of 56.

EDCS-08200059.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment recording the name Quemus and his age at death, which was 18.

EDCS-08200060.summary: This is a tombstone fragment recording the name Gracilis, son of Monis.

EDCS-08200063.translation: Attus, brother, and Caeno made (this) at their own expense for Segius Lougestericus, son of Aionis.
EDCS-08200063.summary: This tombstone was erected by Attus and Caeno for their brother Segius Lougestericus.

EDCS-08200065.translation: Sempronius Britto lived 60 years. Lucius Sempronius, a cavalryman, is buried here. [---]
EDCS-08200065.summary: This tombstone records that Sempronius Britto lived for 60 years and was buried here, with Lucius Sempronius also mentioned.

EDCS-08200067.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment mentioning Sempronius Talavus and his father, Sempronius.

EDCS-08200068.translation: Severus and Primus, as heirs by will...
EDCS-08200068.summary: This inscription fragment indicates that Severus and Primus acted as heirs, likely in relation to a will and a funerary monument.

EDCS-08200069.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning several names, likely dedicating or recording burials, probably from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08200071.text_edition: \n Urbanus Morcicum Aquilliorum vernaculus Vemastae filius annorum XII Proculus Cardilis posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-08200071.translation: Urbanus, a household slave of Aquillius of Morcicum, son of Vemasta, aged 12, erected this for Proculus Cardilis; he lies here.
EDCS-08200071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Urbanus, a slave, erected by Proculus Cardilis, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08200072.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording a dedication from Valeria to her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200073.translation: Valeriana to her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-08200073.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication from Valeriana to her devoted mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200075.translation: Lucius Valerius Arquocus, son of Lucius, aged 45, lies here. Valeria Muggio [erected this] for her husband...
EDCS-08200075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Arquocus, erected by his wife Valeria Muggio, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08200076.translation: [Va]tricus, son of Gaius Saturninus, [erected this] for his sons and himself...
EDCS-08200076.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording a dedication by Vetricus Saturninus for his sons and himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200184.text_edition: \n Capitoni Bulcae Tirdai filio Vendeleiensi nnorum XXXI\n
EDCS-08200184.translation: To Capitoni Bulcae, son of Tirdas, from Vendeleia, aged 31.
EDCS-08200184.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Capitoni Bulcae, likely from the Roman Imperial period, indicating his origin and age.

EDCS-08200194.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, possibly a dedication to a person named Hermes or the god Hermes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300406.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication to Malceinus, son of Douilo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300408.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Reburria Flavina SV L annorum XL\n
EDCS-08300408.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Reburria Flavina, aged 40.
EDCS-08300408.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Reburria Flavina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300409.text_edition: \n Palacia Abruni annorum XX hic est sita A\n
EDCS-08300409.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Palacia Abruni, aged 20.

EDCS-08300411.text_edition: \n Lucio Iulio Urso Serviano III Publio Vivio sic Varo consulibus V Nonas Octobres Granius Silo et Aemilius Sapienus et Iulius Proculus tesseram hospitalem pro meritis Elaesi Otteaii filii nomine cognationis Magilancum Amallobrigenses Cabrumuria et Paligo renovarunt cum senatu populoque Caucensium in perpetuum sibi liberis posterisque omnibus eorum per legatos Marcum Valerium Lentulum IIvirum et Lucium Sempronium Quadratum\n
EDCS-08300411.translation: Under the consulship of Lucius Iulius Urso Servianus, the third, and Publius Vivius Varus, on the 5th day before the Nones of October, Granius Silo, Aemilius Sapienus, and Iulius Proculus, on behalf of Elaesius Otteaius, son of the Magilancum kinship, renovated the hospitality token for his merits with the Amallobrigenses Cabrumuria and Paligo, along with the senate and people of the Caucensians, forever for themselves, their children, and all their descendants, through their legates Marcus Valerius Lentulus, duovir, and Lucius Sempronius Quadratus.
EDCS-08300411.summary: This inscription records the renewal of a hospitality agreement between several tribes and delegates, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE, given the consular dating.

EDCS-08300414.translation: Anto fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08300414.summary: A votive inscription by Anto, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08300415.text_edition: \n Cesando collegium fabrorum votum solvit libens merito p \n
EDCS-08300415.translation: The guild of smiths fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08300415.summary: A votive inscription by the guild of smiths.

EDCS-08300416.text_edition: \n Ambato Alebbio Bodani filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-08300416.translation: Ambatus Alebbius, son of Bodanus, aged 25.
EDCS-08300416.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Ambatus Alebbius, aged 25.

EDCS-08300417.translation: Vigganus, son of Locaeto, of Seggeius.
EDCS-08300417.summary: A tombstone identifying Vigganus, son of Locaeto, with possible tribal or family affiliation to Seggeius.

EDCS-08300418.text_edition: \n Segilae Maglenae Murcae filiae annorum LXXII\n
EDCS-08300418.translation: Segila Maglena, daughter of Murca, aged 72.
EDCS-08300418.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Segila Maglena, daughter of Murca, aged 72.

EDCS-08300419.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Surilla Alba, aged 18.

EDCS-08300422.summary: A tombstone identifying Magia Acca, daughter of Pupillus.

EDCS-08300427.text_edition: \n Caecilio Ambino Segoviensi annorum LX Caecilius \n
EDCS-08300427.translation: To Caecilius Ambinus, of Segovia, aged 60. Caecilius [erected this].
EDCS-08300427.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Caecilius Ambinus of Segovia, aged 60, likely erected by a relative named Caecilius.

EDCS-08300428.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Colonico annorum X C PROCVL LI\n
EDCS-08300428.translation: To the departed spirits of Colonica, aged 10 years. PROCVL LI [erected this].
EDCS-08300428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a girl named Colonica, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-08300430.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name-only epitaph for Aemilius Reburrus.

EDCS-08300435.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Cornelius Hadrianus defunctus annorum IIII Lucius Cornelius Plemianus pater Victoria mater faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08300435.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Cornelius Hadrianus, deceased at the age of 4 years. His father Lucius Cornelius Plemianus and mother Victoria arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08300435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Lucius Cornelius Hadrianus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-08300437.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name-only epitaph for Caelius Proculus.

EDCS-08300439.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum libens Titurnia Artemonis Titurni Postumi liberta votum solvit\n
EDCS-08300439.translation: Sacred to Diana. Titurnia Artemonis, freedwoman of Titurnius Postumus, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-08300439.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Titurnia Artemonis fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-08300442.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Aeturus Solicum hic situs est annorum LX\n
EDCS-08300442.translation: Gaius Iulius Aeturus Solicus lies here, aged 60 years.
EDCS-08300442.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Aeturus Solicus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-08300445.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-08300445.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the emperor Flavius Claudius Constantinus Augustus.

EDCS-08400312.text_edition: \n Valeria Materna votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08400312.translation: Valeria Materna willingly fulfilled her vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-08400312.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Valeria Materna fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08400313.text_edition: \n Gaius Nigrinius Albinus Gaio Albino Nigrinio annorum XVI filio monumentum posuit Dis Manibus sacrum\n

EDCS-08400314.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Severa Severina Reburri Co Buo mairito sic optimo annorum LXIII\n

EDCS-08400327.text_edition: \n Caio Caesare Augusti filio Lucio Aemilio Paullo consulibus ex gente Asturum conventus Arae Augustae civitas Lougeiorum hospitium fecit cum Caio Asinio Gallo libereis postereisque eius eumque liberos posterosque eius sibi libereis sic postereisque sic suis patronum cooptarunt isque eos in fidem clientelamque suam suorumque recepit egerunt legati Silvanus Clouti Noppius Andami\n
EDCS-08400327.translation: When Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, and Lucius Aemilius Paulus were consuls, the citizens of the Lougei, from the convention of Arae Augustae of the Astures tribe, established a guest-friendship with Gaius Asinius Gallus, his children, and their descendants; and they elected him and his children and descendants as their patron, and he received them and their descendants into his protection and patronage. The envoys who acted were Silvanus, son of Cloutus, Noppius, and Andamus.
EDCS-08400327.summary: This is a treaty of guest-friendship and patronage between the Lougei people and Gaius Asinius Gallus, dated to the consulship of Caesar Augustus' son and Lucius Aemilius Paulus.

EDCS-08400329.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum posuit memoriam coniugi bene merenti carissimo rarissimo incomparabili Iulia Mariano in annis plus minus L\n
EDCS-08400329.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Julia Mariano erected this tomb in memory of her very deserving, dearest, most rare, and incomparable husband, who lived about 50 years.
EDCS-08400329.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb erected by Julia Mariano for her beloved husband, who died around the age of 50.

EDCS-08400332.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodia Reburrina marito optimo posuit sepulcrum\n
EDCS-08400332.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Clodia Reburrina erected this tomb for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-08400332.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb erected by Clodia Reburrina for her husband.

EDCS-08400334.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Carmosine posuit coniugi carissimo\n
EDCS-08400334.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Carmosinus erected this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-08400334.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb erected by Carmosinus for her husband.

EDCS-08400336.text_edition: \n DDis MManibus Ammio Lucius Rennius RIV memoria --- annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-08400336.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ammius Lucius Rennius erected this monument [in memory]... of 35 years.
EDCS-08400336.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by Ammius Lucius Rennius for someone who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-08400337.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Ammus Cuneaus at death.

EDCS-08400338.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to determine its content or purpose.

EDCS-08400339.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to determine its content or purpose.

EDCS-08400340.text_edition: \n Lucio Antonio Cai filio Quirina Aquilo IIviro tribuno militum cohortis \n
EDCS-08400340.translation: Lucius Antonius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, Aquilo, twice appointed magistrate, tribune of the soldiers of the cohort...
EDCS-08400340.summary: This inscription records the career of Lucius Antonius Aquilo, including his civic tribe, magisterial roles, and military service.

EDCS-08400341.text_edition: \n Caio deo Lucius Valerius Reburri filius Reburrus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-08400341.translation: To the god Lucius Valerius Reburri, son of Reburrus, paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-08400341.summary: This votive inscription records Lucius Valerius Reburrus fulfilling a vow to a god.

EDCS-08400342.summary: This epitaph records the death of Severus Pressus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-08400343.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Callais.
EDCS-08400343.summary: This is a dedication to the underworld spirits for Callais.

EDCS-08400344.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Securus suae uxori merenti carissimae Lupulae posuit merenti annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-08400344.translation: Dedicated to the underworld spirits. Securus placed this for his deserving and dearest wife Lupula, aged 35.
EDCS-08400344.summary: Securus dedicated this funerary monument to his deserving wife Lupula, who died at age 35.

EDCS-08400347.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to a grandmother named Elaesa.

EDCS-08400350.summary: This epitaph records the death of Dovidona Pesica, a daughter aged 10.

EDCS-08400352.text_edition: \n Cornelio Dahae Ai Alioni filio annorum LX hic situs est Aius Alionus et Annia Sentica patri faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08400352.translation: Cornelius Dahae, son of Ai Alionus, aged 60, is buried here. Aius Alionus and Annia Sentica arranged for this to be made for their father.
EDCS-08400352.summary: Aius Alionus and Annia Sentica commissioned this tomb for their father, Cornelius Dahae, son of Ai Alionus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-08400353.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Marius Lucretianus et Maria Mariana Lucio Valerio \n
EDCS-08400353.translation: Dedicated to the underworld spirits. Gaius Marius Lucretianus and Maria Mariana [placed this] for Lucius Valerius.
EDCS-08400353.summary: Gaius Marius Lucretianus and Maria Mariana dedicated this to the underworld spirits for Lucius Valerius.

EDCS-08400354.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus consul XI tribunicia potestate XV XV imperator XIII pontifex maxsumus sic legio X gemina milia passuum XXXIIX\n
EDCS-08400354.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Julius Caesar], consul for the 11th time, holder of tribunician power for the 15th time, Imperator for the 13th time, chief priest, ordered the 10th Gemina legion to build 32 miles of road.
EDCS-08400354.summary: This inscription records a public works project ordered by Emperor Augustus, involving the 10th Gemina legion constructing 32 miles of road, dated to his 11th consulship and 15th tribunician power.

EDCS-08400355.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus consul XII tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperator XIIII pontifex maxsumus sic legio VI milia passuum LXX\n
EDCS-08400355.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Julius Caesar], consul for the 12th time, holder of tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 14th time, chief priest, ordered the 6th legion to build 70 miles of road.
EDCS-08400355.summary: This inscription records a public works project ordered by Emperor Augustus, involving the 6th legion constructing 70 miles of road, dated to his 12th consulship and 19th tribunician power.

EDCS-08400356.translation: Valentinus fulfilled his vow to Minerva.
EDCS-08400356.summary: Valentinus dedicated this to the goddess Minerva, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08400357.translation: Domino Simroius made this from a vow [for an unspecified period].
EDCS-08400357.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely religious, by Simroius, who completed a vow, possibly by commissioning this monument.

EDCS-08400358.text_edition: \n Caiae Primigeniai annorum LXXX hic sita est Caius Vitalis matri piae sit tibi terra levis Licinio Quartio annorum LXX hic situs est Caius Vitalis patri sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08400358.translation: To Caia Primigenia, aged eighty, lies here; Caius Vitalis [placed this] for his devoted mother. May the earth lie lightly on you. To Licinius Quartus, aged seventy, lies here; Caius Vitalis [placed this] for his father. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08400358.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Caia Primigenia and Licinius Quartus, for whom Caius Vitalis placed funerary markers and invocations for peace.

EDCS-08400359.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemiliae Panthiae annorum LXXX familia BENSIS \n
EDCS-08400359.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Aemilia Panthia, aged eighty, of the Bensis family.
EDCS-08400359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Aemilia Panthia, specifying her age and family affiliation.

EDCS-08400362.text_edition: \n Sempronio Soterico sodales Io cult CO\n
EDCS-08400362.translation: The associates of the cult of Io [dedicated this] to Sempronius Sotericus.
EDCS-08400362.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a collegium or association ('sodales') of the cult of Io to an individual named Sempronius Sotericus.

EDCS-08400363.summary: This is a very brief epitaph or memorial identifying Anthus, who was a slave owned by Rusticus.

EDCS-08401615.text_edition: \n Publio Aemilio Luci filio Galeria Paterno primipilari Caius Iulius Lepidus primipilaris amico O optimo\n
EDCS-08401615.translation: To Publius Aemilius Paternus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, former senior centurion. To his most excellent friend, Caius Iulius Lepidus, former senior centurion.
EDCS-08401615.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Caius Iulius Lepidus, a former senior centurion, to his friend Publius Aemilius Paternus, who also held the rank of primipilaris.

EDCS-08500363.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Augustina pia in suis annorum XX sodales ine posuerunt hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500363.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Augustina, devoted, in her own [plot], aged twenty. The associates placed this. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08500363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Augustina, who died at age twenty, and was commemorated by her associates.

EDCS-08500366.translation: From the office of Elpidius (?) // To Jupiter Optimus Maximus / [Restored text missing].
EDCS-08500366.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly originating from an official context or office, with a name that is partially restored.

EDCS-08500368.text_edition: \n Pro salute Tiberi Claudi Nigri Iovi Optimo Maximo Hermes votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-08500368.translation: For the safety of Tiberius Claudius Niger. Hermes, gladly paying his vow, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-08500368.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Hermes to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of Tiberius Claudius Niger.

EDCS-08500369.translation: Valerius Rufus placed [this] for the household gods of the family.
EDCS-08500369.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Valerius Rufus to the Lares of his household (Laribus Vialibus), likely for protection or in gratitude.

EDCS-08500372.text_edition: \n Arcuius aram posuit pro voto domini Aecorougi a vesu sic conservis deo ubicue sic terrarum\n
EDCS-08500372.translation: Arcuius erected an altar in fulfillment of a vow to the lord Aecorogus, a god of Vesu, and also to the god of all lands.
EDCS-08500372.summary: An altar dedicated to the gods Aecorogus and a generic 'god of all lands' by Arcuius, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08500373.translation: Sacred to the holy Daeae, Anicius [dedicated it].
EDCS-08500373.summary: A dedication to the holy goddess Daeae by Anicius.

EDCS-08500374.text_edition: \n Lucretius Caturonis filius Lari Beiradiego ex voto posuit aram sacravit\n
EDCS-08500374.translation: Lucretius, son of Caturon, erected and consecrated an altar to Lari Beiradiego in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08500374.summary: An altar dedicated to the deity Lari Beiradiego by Lucretius, son of Caturon, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08500375.text_edition: \n Aurelius Flavus Lari Carieco votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-08500375.translation: Aurelius Flavus willingly paid a vow to Lari Cariecus.
EDCS-08500375.summary: Aurelius Flavus fulfilled a vow to the deity Lari Cariecus.

EDCS-08500376.text_edition: \n Coporici Materni ex voto Marti Tarbuceli fullones\n
EDCS-08500376.translation: Fullers of Coporici, in fulfillment of a vow to Martius Tarbucelius.
EDCS-08500376.summary: A dedication made by fullers to the god Martius Tarbucelius, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08500377.text_edition: \n ivo Vestero Valeria Rufa ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-08500377.translation: Valeria Rufa erected an altar to the god Vester in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08500377.summary: An altar dedicated to the god Vester by Valeria Rufa in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08500378.text_edition: \n Domino sancto K G L La Rufus Rufini aram posuit votum solvit\n
EDCS-08500378.translation: La Rufus, son of Rufus, erected an altar and paid a vow to the holy lord K G L.
EDCS-08500378.summary: An altar dedicated to the deity K G L by La Rufus, son of Rufus, who paid a vow.

EDCS-08500379.text_edition: \n Domino sancto La La aram posuit quod vovit frater Cornelia Rufila Soupi Camali filii\n
EDCS-08500379.translation: Cornelia Rufila, daughter of Soupi Camali, erected an altar to the holy lord La La, as she vowed.
EDCS-08500379.summary: Cornelia Rufila, daughter of Soupi Camali, erected an altar to the deity La La, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08500381.text_edition: \n SE Q F M B V L SO\n
EDCS-08500381.translation: [No clear translation possible due to extreme abbreviation.]
EDCS-08500381.summary: An inscription with highly abbreviated Latin, potentially a dedication or funerary notice, but undecipherable without context.

EDCS-08500383.summary: A tombstone recording the name and age of Quintus Apronius Flavus.

EDCS-08500387.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Claudio Constantino iuniori Pio Felici\n
EDCS-08500387.translation: To our Lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-08500387.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantine Iunior, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-08500392.translation: To Abronia Quinta, daughter of Abrionius Reburrus.
EDCS-08500392.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Abronia Quinta, probably on a tombstone.

EDCS-08500393.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marius Adronius at the age of six.

EDCS-08500394.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Flaccilla et Flaccina et Materna matri et patri et fratri nnm XVIII pro pietate posuerunt\n
EDCS-08500394.translation: Dedicated in piety by Flaccilla, Flaccina, and Materna for their mother, father, and brother, eighteen years ago.
EDCS-08500394.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Flaccilla, Flaccina, and Materna set up a memorial for their family members.

EDCS-08500402.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ano Meducinus filius et Saulcia mater pientissimo fecerunt annorum XXV\n
EDCS-08500402.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ano Meducinus and his mother Saulcia made this for the most dutiful, aged twenty-five years.
EDCS-08500402.summary: This funerary inscription records Ano Meducinus and his mother Saulcia setting up a memorial, possibly for one of them, indicating the deceased was twenty-five.

EDCS-08500404.text_edition: \n Ambatae Dessicae Rufi filiae annorum LV Titus Va socerae Arceae Dessicae Paterni filiae annorum X Titus uxsori sic\n
EDCS-08500404.translation: To Ambata Dessica, daughter of Rufus, aged fifty-five years. Titus for his mother-in-law Arceia Dessica, daughter of Paternus, aged ten years. Titus for his wife. Thus.
EDCS-08500404.summary: This inscription appears to be a double funerary dedication, recording the deaths of Ambata Dessica and Arceia Dessica, with Titus as the dedicant for both.

EDCS-08500406.text_edition: \n Sextio Prisco filio filius Quirina Severus nepoti eidemque sic dedicarunt\n
EDCS-08500406.translation: His son and grandson dedicated this to Sextius Priscus, and to the same also to Quirinus Severus, his grandson.
EDCS-08500406.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a son and grandson to Sextius Priscus and Quirinus Severus, who is identified as the grandson.

EDCS-08500407.text_edition: \n Quinti Sei Publi filii Menenia Postumi AI\n
EDCS-08500407.translation: Of Quintus Seius, son of Publius, of the Menenian tribe. Postumus [set this up].
EDCS-08500407.summary: This is an epitaph or dedication fragment naming Quintus Seius, son of Publius, and likely erected by Postumus.

EDCS-08500409.text_edition: \n Divo Antonino Magno patri domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti res publica Tutugitanorum\n
EDCS-08500409.translation: To the deified Antoninus Magnus, father of our lord the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus. The community of Tuturitanorum.
EDCS-08500409.summary: This inscription records the community of Tuturitanorum dedicating a monument to the deified Emperor Antoninus Pius, father of Severus Alexander.

EDCS-08500410.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio Felici tribunicia potestate Publius Atellius Ser(gia) Chanusius Paulinus IIvir ex decreto decurionum posuit\n
EDCS-08500410.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, holding tribunician power, Publius Atellius Sergius Chanusius Paulinus, duovir, set this up by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08500410.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Atellius Sergius Chanusius Paulinus, a duovir, to Emperor Caracalla, likely for a public building or monument.

EDCS-08500411.text_edition: \n Manliae Psychi sic Manlius Carpophorus et Marce filiae posuerunt Iulius Aemilius Titi libertus amicae optimae\n
EDCS-08500411.translation: Manlius Carpophorus and Marce erected this for their daughter Manlia Psyche, and Julius Aemilius, freedman of Titus, for his excellent friend.
EDCS-08500411.summary: A funerary monument erected by family members for Manlia Psyche, with an additional dedication from a freedman for his friend.

EDCS-08500423.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelio Narcisso annorum LXIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Marcia Patrina uxor\n
EDCS-08500423.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Narcissus, aged 64, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you, Marcia Patrina, your wife.
EDCS-08500423.summary: A tombstone recording the death and burial of Cornelius Narcissus, erected by his wife Marcia Patrina.

EDCS-08500428.text_edition: \n Aphni Pacci Saturnini procuratoris Vespasisiani Augusti liberti\n
EDCS-08500428.translation: To Aphnis, son of Paccius Saturninus, procurator of Vespasianus Augustus Libertus.
EDCS-08500428.summary: An inscription dedicating an honorific monument to Aphnis, son of a procurator under a freedman of the emperor.

EDCS-08600373.text_edition: \n Maternus posuit arudam Iovi Optimo Maximo\n
EDCS-08600373.translation: Maternus erected this sacred grove to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-08600373.summary: A dedication of a sacred grove to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maternus.

EDCS-08600375.text_edition: \n Bulcai Latroni sic filio et Patruinae Corali filiae post mortem fili posuerunt monumentum\n
EDCS-08600375.translation: They erected this monument for their son Bulcai Latro and for their daughter Patruina Coralis, after the death of their son.
EDCS-08600375.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their deceased children, Bulcai Latro and Patruina Coralis.

EDCS-08600377.translation: To Proculus, son of Reburrinus, aged 30.
EDCS-08600377.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Proculus, son of Reburrinus.

EDCS-08600378.translation: To Cariton, son of Licinus, aged 40.
EDCS-08600378.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Cariton, son of Licinus.

EDCS-08600379.translation: To Pitilicus, son of Pintous, aged 80.
EDCS-08600379.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Pitilicus, son of Pintous.

EDCS-08600380.text_edition: \n Fabia Eburi filia Lemava |(castello) Eritaeco annorum XL Virius Caesi filius Lemavus |(castello) eodem annorum VII hic siti sunt Caessius \n
EDCS-08600380.translation: Fabia, daughter of Eburius, aged 40, from the fort of Lemava in Eritaeco; Virius, son of Caelsius, aged 7, from the same fort. Caessius [is buried] here.
EDCS-08600380.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Fabia and Virius, from the fort of Lemava, with a possible mention of Caessius.

EDCS-08600382.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Tiberi Claudi Caesaris Augusti Germanici imperatoris terminus pratorum legionis X geminae inter legionem decimam et civitatem Beduniensium\n
EDCS-08600382.translation: By authority of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus Imperator, this is the boundary of the lands of the Tenth Gemina legion between the tenth legion and the civitas of the Bedunienses.
EDCS-08600382.summary: An official marker establishing a boundary between a Roman legion and a local civitas, authorized by Emperor Claudius.

EDCS-08600384.text_edition: \n M ntovio Elao militi alae Vadiniensi annorum XXV Elanus Arga amico posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600384.translation: To Marcus Antonius Elaus, soldier of the Ala Vadiniensis, aged 25. His friend Elanus Arga erected this. He lies here.
EDCS-08600384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Elaus, a soldier of the Ala Vadiniensis, erected by his friend.

EDCS-08600385.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Aemilius miles legionis VII\n
EDCS-08600385.translation: Gaius Julius Aemilius, soldier of the Seventh Legion.
EDCS-08600385.summary: This inscription records the name and military affiliation of Gaius Julius Aemilius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion.

EDCS-08600386.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Festo Palydino annorum LXV Vernae annorum XXXIII Kraurius et fil et Aestius faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08600386.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Festus Palydinus, aged 65, and Vernae, aged 33. Kraurius, his son, and Aestius saw to its construction.
EDCS-08600386.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Festus Palydinus and Vernae, likely a family group, with construction overseen by Kraurius and Aestius.

EDCS-08600387.text_edition: \n Homicino Asino annorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600387.translation: Homicinus Asinus, aged [---]. He lies here.
EDCS-08600387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Homicinus Asinus, noting his age and that he lies here.

EDCS-08600388.text_edition: \n Eleia Verna annorum LX hic sita est\n
EDCS-08600388.translation: Eleia Verna, aged 60. She lies here.
EDCS-08600388.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eleia Verna, stating her age and that she lies here.

EDCS-08600389.text_edition: \n MA ALM annorum LXX hic sit-- est\n
EDCS-08600389.translation: M.A. ALM, aged 70. He lies here.
EDCS-08600389.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for M.A. ALM, noting his age and that he lies here.

EDCS-08600391.text_edition: \n Terentius Martialis Losae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-08600391.translation: Terentius Martialis willingly fulfilled his vow to Losa.
EDCS-08600391.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Terentius Martialis fulfilling a vow made to Losa.

EDCS-08600392.summary: This is a simple statement of filiation, identifying Araica as the daughter of Licius.

EDCS-08600393.summary: This is a simple statement of filiation, identifying Rimaus as the son of Colisus.

EDCS-08600396.text_edition: \n Opta annorum hic sit-- est posuit\n
EDCS-08600396.translation: Opta, aged [---]. He lies here. Erected.
EDCS-08600396.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Opta, noting his age, that he lies here, and that it was erected.

EDCS-08600399.translation: To Maiduana, the spirit of Animpis, thus to Minerva.
EDCS-08600399.summary: This is a dedication to the goddesses Maiduana, Animpis, and Minerva.

EDCS-08600400.text_edition: \n Deo Maximo Iovi votum ICINI solvvit animo libens BABAE\n
EDCS-08600400.translation: Babae willingly paid his vow to the Great God Jupiter with a willing spirit.
EDCS-08600400.summary: This records a votive offering made by Babae to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-08600401.text_edition: \n Herculi Virbia serva votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-08600401.translation: Virbia, the slave woman, willingly paid her vow to Hercules as deserved.
EDCS-08600401.summary: This is a votive inscription by Virbia, a slave, to Hercules.

EDCS-08600402.translation: To Hercules, from Martialis, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08600402.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules by Martialis.

EDCS-08600404.text_edition: \n Publicius Asmenus annorum LXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Iullia Auctilla sepulta Romae annorum XL sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600404.translation: Publicius Asmenus, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iullia Auctilla, buried in Rome, aged 40. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08600404.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publicius Asmenus and Iullia Auctilla, with standard wishes for their repose.

EDCS-08600405.text_edition: \n Fulvia Luci filia Hibera hic sita est\n
EDCS-08600405.translation: Fulvia, daughter of Lucius, from Iberia, is buried here.
EDCS-08600405.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Fulvia, daughter of Lucius, who was from Iberia.

EDCS-08600406.text_edition: \n Hic situs est Felix Fabius Lucius Caeboqum\n
EDCS-08600406.translation: Felix Fabius Lucius Caeboqum is buried here.
EDCS-08600406.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Felix Fabius Lucius Caeboqum.

EDCS-08600407.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mar Valeriano nnorum VIIII semis Mar Ianuarius alumno dulcissimo sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600407.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Mar Valeriano, aged 9 and a half. Mar Ianuarius to his sweetest pupil. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08600407.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Mar Ianuarius for his pupil Mar Valeriano, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-08600408.text_edition: \n Hic sita est Rufina Latini liberta\n
EDCS-08600408.translation: Rufina, the freedwoman of Latinus, is buried here.
EDCS-08600408.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Rufina, a freedwoman of Latinus.

EDCS-08600409.text_edition: \n Cavaboni sibus armis suis de suo PVTEVMA F putiario sic Placido\n
EDCS-08600409.translation: To Cavabon, with his arms, of his own accord, the well-digger Placido.
EDCS-08600409.summary: This records a dedication by the well-digger Placido to Cavabon, possibly a local deity or hero, with his own military equipment.

EDCS-08600412.text_edition: \n Fortillae Cantaber uxori optimae annorum XLV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600412.translation: Fortilla to his most excellent wife, aged 45. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600412.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a woman named Fortillae, with her husband Fortillae as the dedicant.

EDCS-08600413.text_edition: \n M Perannorum LXXV Pat marito optimo sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600413.translation: Pat to his most excellent husband, aged 75. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600413.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a man named P., aged 75, with his wife P. as the dedicant.

EDCS-08600414.text_edition: \n us et Secundina mater sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600414.translation: To us and Secundina, mother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600414.summary: A funerary epitaph with a mother named Secundina as the deceased, possibly dedicated by her child(ren).

EDCS-08600416.text_edition: \n Caius Terentius Placidus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600416.translation: Gaius Terentius Placidus is buried here.
EDCS-08600416.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Gaius Terentius Placidus.

EDCS-08600417.text_edition: \n Corneliae Faystinae sic Queta mater annorum XX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600417.translation: To Cornelia Faystina, whom Queta, mother, bore, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600417.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Faystina, aged 20, dedicated by her mother Queta.

EDCS-08600418.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Stertoni annorum VII sui faciendum curaverunt sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600418.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods. Sterton, aged 7, had this prepared. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600418.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Sterton, aged 7, dedicated to the gods by his family.

EDCS-08600419.text_edition: \n Lucius Scribonius Tiro hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600419.translation: Lucius Scribonius Tiro is buried here.
EDCS-08600419.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Scribonius Tiro.

EDCS-08600420.text_edition: \n Luci<f=E>er Luci Semproni Numidae libertus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Crispus Syrus Satur fratres \n
EDCS-08600420.translation: Lucifer, freedman of Lucius Sempronius Numida, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Crispus and Satur, brothers.
EDCS-08600420.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucifer, a freedman, with his brothers Crispus and Satur as dedicants.

EDCS-08600421.text_edition: \n Caecilius Senicionis filius hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600421.translation: Caecilius, son of Senicio, is buried here.
EDCS-08600421.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Caecilius, son of Senicio.

EDCS-08600423.translation: To Minerius, from a vow. Ianuarius.
EDCS-08600423.summary: A dedication to the deity Minerius by Ianuarius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08600424.text_edition: \n Gaius Malius Urssus sic miles legionis VII geminae felicis cohorte VIIII Nymphis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600424.translation: Gaius Malius Urssus, a soldier of the Seventh Twin Legion Felix, in the ninth cohort, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-08600424.summary: A votive inscription by a soldier of the Seventh Legion Felix, dedicated to the Nymphs.

EDCS-08600427.translation: To Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger.
EDCS-08600427.summary: An inscription, likely a dedication or memorial, for Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger.

EDCS-08600430.text_edition: \n Quintus Postumius Verecundus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600430.translation: Quintus Postumius Verecundus is buried here.
EDCS-08600430.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Postumius Verecundus.

EDCS-08600431.text_edition: \n Severi l Viticula Clementis filia hic siti sunt sit tibi sic terra levis\n
EDCS-08600431.translation: Severus Viticula, daughter of Clementis, are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600431.summary: A tombstone for Severus Viticula, daughter of Clementis, with a common epitaph requesting lightness of earth.

EDCS-08600432.text_edition: \n Faustus Marci Publici Flori filius nnorum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600432.translation: Faustus, son of Marcius Publicius Florus, aged 20, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600432.summary: A tombstone for Faustus, son of Marcius Publicius Florus, aged 20, including a standard epitaph.

EDCS-08600433.text_edition: \n Arescusa annorum XXVI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600433.translation: Arescusa, aged 26, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600433.summary: A tombstone for Arescusa, aged 26, with the customary wish for a light burial.

EDCS-08600436.text_edition: \n Lucius Minicius Galeria Severus nnorum LXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis hoc monumentum heredes non sequetur\n
EDCS-08600436.translation: Lucius Minicius Severus, from the Galerian tribe, aged 75, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you; his heirs will not inherit this monument.
EDCS-08600436.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Minicius Severus, aged 75, specifying that the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-08600445.text_edition: \n Caeciliae Anthidi Propertia Quinti Iucunda de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08600445.translation: Propertia Iucunda arranged for this to be made at her own expense for Caecilia Anthis.
EDCS-08600445.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Propertia Iucunda for Caecilia Anthis.

EDCS-08900433.text_edition: \n Ddominis nnostris Constanti nobilissimo Caesari posuit Flavinus miliarium XXVIIII\n
EDCS-08900433.translation: Flavinus erected this milestone, marking 29 miles, for our Lords Constantius, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-08900433.summary: A milestone inscription indicating 29 miles, dedicated to Caesar Constantius.

EDCS-08900434.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maxsimino sic imperatori \n
EDCS-08900434.translation: For our Lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, Emperor.
EDCS-08900434.summary: An inscription honouring Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximinus.

EDCS-08900436.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari milia passuum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-08900436.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar, 34 miles.
EDCS-08900436.summary: This is a milestone, likely dating to the early 4th century CE, indicating a distance of 34 miles to the emperor Maximinus.

EDCS-08900437.translation: To the emperor, our lord Flavius Constantius, the greatest, victorious, most noble, triumphant Augustus, son of the divine Maximianus Galerius, grandson of the divine Flavi Constanti and Valeri Maximiani, great-grandson of Claudius.
EDCS-08900437.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantius Chlorus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE, listing his imperial titles and lineage.

EDCS-08900450.text_edition: \n Calpurniae Ambatae Lougei filiae annorum XXXV Calbisus sic uxori\n
EDCS-08900450.translation: To Calpurnia Ambata, daughter of Lougeius, aged 35, wife of Calbisus.
EDCS-08900450.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman named Calpurnia Ambata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900452.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Pecellio Aemiliano Papiria Emerita annorum XLVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Lucius Pecellius Avitianus patri faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08900452.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Pecellius Avitianus arranged for this to be made for his father, Lucius Pecellius Aemilianus, aged 47. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08900452.summary: This tombstone honors Lucius Pecellius Aemilianus, who died at age 47, and was erected by his son Lucius Pecellius Avitianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901014.text_edition: \n Voconia Quinti filia Avita thermas rei publicae suae Tagilitanae solo suo sua pecunia fecit easdemque circensibus editis et epulo dato dedicavit at sic quot sic opus tuendum usumque perpetuum thermarum praebandum sic rei publicae Tagilitanae denariorum duo milia quingentos dedit\n
EDCS-08901014.translation: Voconia Avita, daughter of Quintus, made the baths for her public state of Tagilitana, on her own land, with her own money; and she dedicated them after providing games and a feast, and gave 2,500 denarii to the Tagilitanian public state for the upkeep of the work and the perpetual use of the baths.
EDCS-08901014.summary: This inscription records the construction and endowment of public baths in Tagilitana by Voconia Avita, a wealthy benefactor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901026.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Laturina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09000837.text_edition: \n Publius Frontin<i>us Sciscola medicus colonorum coloniae Patriciae hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000837.translation: Publius Frontinius Sciscola, physician of the colonists of the colony Patricia, rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000837.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Frontinius Sciscola, a physician, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001433.text_edition: \n Artifices Calubrigenses et Abinieses faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09001433.translation: The artisans of Calubriga and Abinienses arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09001433.summary: This inscription records that the artisans from the towns of Calubriga and Abinienses commissioned this work, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001435.text_edition: \n Marcus Licinius Veleiensis Laribus Gumeinu votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09001435.translation: Marcus Licinius Veleiensis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Gumeine Lares.
EDCS-09001435.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Licinius Veleiensis to local deities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001446.translation: To Medugena, daughter of Arron, aged 20.
EDCS-09001446.summary: This is a tombstone for Medugena, aged 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001452.text_edition: \n Caio Moenio Nigrini filio Quirina Frontoni IIviro annorum LXV\n
EDCS-09001452.translation: To Gaius Moenius Frontonius, son of Nigrinus, of the Quirina tribe, a magistrate of the duumvirs, aged 65.
EDCS-09001452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Moenius Frontonius, a local magistrate, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001454.text_edition: \n Valerius Crescen IIvir Valerius St IIvir Valerius C \n
EDCS-09001454.translation: Valerius Crescens, duumvir; Valerius St[---], duumvir; Valerius C[---].
EDCS-09001454.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to civic office, with two individuals identified as duumvirs.

EDCS-09001456.text_edition: \n Aestivo Manuciqum annorum XXXV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001456.translation: To Aestivus, son of Manucius, aged 35. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001456.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aestivus, son of Manucius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001457.text_edition: \n Niddo Belainocum C filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001457.translation: To Niddo, son of Belainocus, aged [---]. Here lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Niddo, son of Belainocus, likely from a region with Celtic influence, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100001.text_edition: \n Asclepio Quintus Calpurnius Alypion votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-09100001.translation: Quintus Calpurnius Alypion willingly fulfilled his vow to Asclepius.
EDCS-09100001.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Asclepius by Quintus Calpurnius Alypion.

EDCS-09100002.text_edition: \n Asclepio deo Lucius Cornelius Hyginus sevir Augustalis\n
EDCS-09100002.translation: To Asclepius the god, Lucius Cornelius Hyginus, a member of the college of seviri Augustales.
EDCS-09100002.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Asclepius by Lucius Cornelius Hyginus, a member of an imperial religious college.

EDCS-09100003.text_edition: \n Fatis Quintus Fabius Nysus ex voto\n
EDCS-09100003.translation: To the Fates, Quintus Fabius Nysus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09100003.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Fates by Quintus Fabius Nysus.

EDCS-09100004.text_edition: \n Fortunae Titus Rubrius Titi filius Restitutus de suo fecit\n
EDCS-09100004.translation: Titus Rubrius Restitutus, son of Titus, made this at his own expense for Fortune.
EDCS-09100004.summary: This is a dedication of a structure or object to the goddess Fortuna by Titus Rubrius Restitutus.

EDCS-09100005.text_edition: \n Marcus Marcius Marci filius Celsus Herculem cum basi et ara et subsellis suo et Marci Marci Antonini fili sui nomine dono dedit\n
EDCS-09100005.translation: Marcus Marcius Celsus, son of Marcus, dedicated Hercules with a base, altar, and benches, both in his own name and in the name of his son, Marcus Marcius Antoninus.
EDCS-09100005.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules by Marcus Marcius Celsus, who also dedicated the items in the name of his son, Marcus Marcius Antoninus.

EDCS-09100007.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Ammoni Lucius Antonius Luci filius Galeria Sabinus et Antonia Luci filia Procula\n
EDCS-09100007.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Ammon, Lucius Antonius Sabinus, son of Lucius, and Antonia Procula, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-09100007.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Ammon by Lucius Antonius Sabinus and Antonia Procula.

EDCS-09100008.text_edition: \n Nymphis Marcus Valerius Munitus Astur ex voto\n
EDCS-09100008.translation: To the Nymphs, Marcus Valerius Munitus Astur, according to his vow.
EDCS-09100008.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Valerius Munitus Astur to the Nymphs.

EDCS-09100009.text_edition: \n Serapi pro salute Publi Herenni Severi Callinicus servus\n
EDCS-09100009.translation: To Serapis, for the health of Publius Herennius Severus, Callinicus his slave.
EDCS-09100009.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Serapis by a slave named Callinicus for the well-being of his master, Publius Herennius Severus.

EDCS-09100012.text_edition: \n Quintus Sertorius Quinti libertus Abascantus sevir Augustalis de sua pecunia faciendum curavit idemque dedicavit\n
EDCS-09100012.translation: Quintus Sertorius Abascantus, freedman of Quintus Sertorius, an Augustalis priest, had this made at his own expense and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-09100012.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a monument by Quintus Sertorius Abascantus, an Augustalis priest, likely during the Flavian or Antonine period.

EDCS-09100013.text_edition: \n Caesari Tito Imperatori Vespasiano Augusto Vespasiani filio conservatori pacis Augustae \n
EDCS-09100013.translation: To Caesar Titus Imperator Vespasian Augustus, son of Vespasian, preserver of the Augustan peace.
EDCS-09100013.summary: This is an inscription honoring Emperor Vespasian, praising him as the preserver of peace, likely from the Flavian period.

EDCS-09100016.text_edition: \n Gnaeae Seiae Herenniae Sallustiae Barbiae Orbianae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Augusti Valentini veterani et veteres\n
EDCS-09100016.translation: To Gnaea Seia Herennia Sallustia Barbia Orbiana, wife of our lord Augustus Valentinus, from veterans and former soldiers.
EDCS-09100016.summary: This inscription dedicates something to Gnaea Seia Herennia Sallustia Barbia Orbiana, wife of Valentinus, by veterans and former soldiers, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-09100017.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis Valentini veterani et veteres\n
EDCS-09100017.translation: To Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, from the veterans and former soldiers of Valentinus.
EDCS-09100017.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, a Caesar and prince of youth, dedicated by veterans and former soldiers of Valentinus, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09100019.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli Valentini veterani et veteres magis\n
EDCS-09100019.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, pious, fortunate, unconquered, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, from the veterans and former soldiers, magistrates.
EDCS-09100019.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Claudius Gothicus, listing his titles and dedication by veterans, former soldiers, and magistrates, likely dating to his reign (268-270 AD).

EDCS-09100021.text_edition: \n Pietate iustitia fortitudine et pleno omnium virtutum principi vero Gothico veroque Germanico ac victoriarum omnium nominibus inlustri Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V patri patriae consuli IIII proconsuli Allius Maximus vir clarissimus legatus iuridicus provinciae Hispaniae Tarraconensis maiestati eius ac numini dicatissimus\n
EDCS-09100021.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Probus, pious, fortunate, unconquered, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, four-time consul, proconsul, illustrious Allius Maximus, imperial legate with jurisdiction in the province of Hispania Tarraconensis, dedicated to his majesty and divinity.
EDCS-09100021.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Probus, detailing his virtues and titles, and is dedicated by Allius Maximus, an imperial legate, likely dating to Probus' reign (276-282 AD).

EDCS-09100022.text_edition: \n Marco Nummio Senecioni Albino clarissimo viro pontifici legato Auggustorum pro praetore Valentini veterani et veteres patrono curantibus Brinnio Marco et Licinio Quinto\n
