EDCS-09100022.translation: To Quintus Herennius Senecio Albinus, most distinguished man, priest, legate of the emperors, governor with proconsular power, patron, from the veterans and former soldiers of Valentinus, under the care of Brinius Marcus and Licinius Quintus.
EDCS-09100022.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Herennius Senecio Albinus, a prominent official, as a patron by veterans and former soldiers, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-09100025.text_edition: \n Lucio Antonio Luci filio Galeria Crescenti aedili IIviro flamini huic defuncto ab universo ordine Valentinorum decreta est publica laudatio et locus sepulturae et funeris impensa et statua ex decreto decurionum veteranorum Lucio Antonio Luci filio Galeria Crescenti filio Iulia Cai filia Maxima mater\n
EDCS-09100025.translation: To Lucius Antonius Crescentius, son of Lucius Antonius, former aedile and duovir, and high priest; upon his death, the entire order of Valentini decreed a public eulogy, burial place, funeral expenses, and a statue, by decree of the veteran decurions, to Lucius Antonius Crescentius, son of Lucius Antonius; Iulia Maxima, daughter of Caius, mother.
EDCS-09100025.summary: This inscription records a posthumous honor, including a public eulogy and burial, decreed by the veteran decurions for Lucius Antonius Crescentius, a former local magistrate, with his mother also mentioned, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09100026.text_edition: \n Lucio Antonio Luci filio Galeria Nigro Iulia Gai filia Maxima mater Caio Iulio Cai filio Galeria Nigro aedili decurioni Valentinorum veteranorum Iulia Cai filia Maxima patri\n
EDCS-09100026.translation: To Lucius Antonius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe. His mother, Iulia Maxima, daughter of Gaius, [erected this to] Lucius Iulius Niger, son of Gaius, aedile and decurion of the veteran Valentinorum. Iulia Maxima, daughter of Gaius, [erected this to] her most loving father.
EDCS-09100026.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Antonius Niger, a decurion of the Valentinorum veterans, set up by his mother, dating likely to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100030.text_edition: \n Lucio Scribonio Euphemo seviro Augustali Lucius Rubrius Eutyches amico\n
EDCS-09100030.translation: To Lucius Scribonius Euphemus, Augustalis priest. Lucius Rubrius Eutyches [erected this to] his friend.
EDCS-09100030.summary: This is a dedication from Lucius Rubrius Eutyches to his friend, Lucius Scribonius Euphemus, an Augustalis priest, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100031.text_edition: \n Quinto Sertorio Galeria Abascanto seviro Augustali Philon et Prima liberti patrono optimo Quinto Sertorio Quinti filio Galeria Crescenti Philon et Prima filio carissimo Quinto Sertorio Quinti filio Galeria Flaviano Flavus pater filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100031.translation: To Quintus Sertorius Abascantus, Augustalis priest. To Quintus Sertorius Crescentianus, son of Quintus, his very dear son, from Philon and Prima, freedmen. To Quintus Sertorius Flavianus, son of Quintus, his most dutiful son, from Flavus, his father. To their patron and best master, Quintus Sertorius Abascantus.
EDCS-09100031.summary: This is a complex funerary monument honoring Quintus Sertorius Abascantus and his son Quintus Sertorius Flavianus, with inscriptions from freedmen and his father, dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100032.text_edition: \n Publio Herennio Publi filio Palatina Novatiano Herennia Leontis patrono et marito\n
EDCS-09100032.translation: To Novatianus, son of Publius Herennius, of the Palatina tribe, her patron and husband.
EDCS-09100032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Novatianus, son of Publius Herennius, set up by Herennia Leontis, who identifies him as both her patron and husband, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100033.text_edition: \n Caio Virio Cai filio Galeria Nepoti Valeria Marci filia Maxima mater\n
EDCS-09100033.translation: To Gaius Virius Nepos, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe. His mother, Valeria Maxima, daughter of Marcus, [erected this].
EDCS-09100033.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Virius Nepos by his mother, Valeria Maxima, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100036.text_edition: \n Iulius a ratione tegularia Anthimus cum censum pariavit DXXXIII ago gratias domine Solve quod minus fecisti et sic gratias age et duas amplius quia minus numerasti\n
EDCS-09100036.translation: I, Anthimus, in charge of roof tiles, having paid taxes of 533 [denarii], give thanks to the lord. You have done less [than you should have], and give thanks thus, and [pay] two [more] because you paid less.
EDCS-09100036.summary: This inscription is a tax receipt or a statement concerning tax payment for roof tiles, likely from a provincial setting and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Iulio Apolausto argentario vasclario fecit Lettia Valentina marito optimo\n
EDCS-09100037.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lettia Valentina [erected this] to her most excellent husband, Lucius Iulius Apolaustus, a silversmith and maker of vessels.
EDCS-09100037.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Apolaustus, a silversmith, set up by his wife Lettia Valentina, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100038.text_edition: \n Viriae Acte Ampliatus qui fabricae ararum et signorum praefuit et Callirhoe et liberti\n
EDCS-09100038.translation: To Viria Acte. Ampliatus, who was in charge of the workshop of altars and statues, and Callirhoe, and their freedmen [set this up].
EDCS-09100038.summary: This is a dedication honoring Viria Acte, set up by Ampliatus, who managed a workshop for religious artifacts, and others, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100040.text_edition: \n Marcus Baebius Marci filius Marcellus Urcitanus \n
EDCS-09100040.translation: Marcus Baebius Marcellus, son of Marcus, from Urci.
EDCS-09100040.summary: This is a simple funerary or commemorative inscription for Marcus Baebius Marcellus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100044.text_edition: \n Antoniae Marci filiae Lepidae Anatellon libertus\n
EDCS-09100044.translation: To Antonia Lepida, daughter of Marcus. Anatellon, her freedman, [erected this].
EDCS-09100044.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Antonia Lepida by her freedman Anatellon, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoniae Gai filiae Maximillae annorum LXXVI\n
EDCS-09100045.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Antonia Maxmillia, daughter of Gaius, aged 76.
EDCS-09100045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Maxmillia, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoniae Valentinae nnorum XL Antonia Veneriosa sorori pientissimae\n
EDCS-09100046.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Antonia Valentina, aged 40. Antonia Veneriosa to her most devoted sister.
EDCS-09100046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Antonia Valentina, aged 40, with a dedication from her sister Antonia Veneriosa.

EDCS-09100047.text_edition: \n Marco Aquilio Aquilino nnorum XXXVI Lucius Porcius Severus amico optimo\n
EDCS-09100047.translation: For Marcus Aquilius Aquilinus, aged 36. Lucius Porcius Severus to his best friend.
EDCS-09100047.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marcus Aquilius Aquilinus, aged 36, dedicated by his friend Lucius Porcius Severus.

EDCS-09100050.text_edition: \n Brinnio Asclepiodoto filio innocentissimo Babeliae Feliculae nore obsequentissimae Publio Brinnio Andronico et Antoniae Alexandriae nepotibus dulcissimis Fulvius Asclepiades\n
EDCS-09100050.translation: To their most innocent son Brinnius Asclepiodotus, to their most obedient wife Babelia Felicula, to their sweetest grandchildren Publius Brinnius Andronicus and Antonia Alexandria; from Fulvius Asclepiades.
EDCS-09100050.summary: This funerary monument records the deaths of Brinnius Asclepiodotus, Babelia Felicula, Publius Brinnius Andronicus, and Antonia Alexandria, dedicated by Fulvius Asclepiades.

EDCS-09100051.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caeciliae Primitivae annorum XVII Antonia Onesicratia alumnae pientissimae\n
EDCS-09100051.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Caecilia Primitiva, aged 17. Antonia Onesicratia to her most devoted foster-daughter.
EDCS-09100051.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caecilia Primitiva, aged 17, with a dedication from her foster-mother Antonia Onesicratia.

EDCS-09100052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Calventio Mattio annorum LXX Victoricus fratri Herennia Protis marito bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09100052.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Calventius Mattius, aged 70. Victoricus to his brother, Herennia Protis to her deserving husband. They made this.
EDCS-09100052.summary: This funerary monument records the deaths of Calventius Mattius, aged 70, and a husband, with dedications from Victoricus and Herennia Protis.

EDCS-09100053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Carviliae Martae posuit bene merenti Zoe alumna\n
EDCS-09100053.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Carvilia Marta. Zoe, her foster-daughter, placed this to her deserving [mother].
EDCS-09100053.summary: This funerary epitaph records Carvilia Marta, for whom her foster-daughter Zoe erected a monument.

EDCS-09100054.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Cassi Urbici Cassia Accepta marito bene merenti et sibi annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-09100054.translation: To the spirits of the departed; for Lucius Cassius Urbicus. Cassia Accepta to her deserving husband and herself, aged 35.
EDCS-09100054.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Cassius Urbicus and Cassia Accepta, who dedicated it for her husband and herself.

EDCS-09100055.translation: To their most dutiful daughter Cornelia.
EDCS-09100055.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph dedicated to Cornelia.

EDCS-09100056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Glyce, aged 16.

EDCS-09100057.translation: Crescens and his wife Viria Acte.
EDCS-09100057.summary: This is a short epitaph naming Crescens and his wife, Viria Acte, likely recording their burial.

EDCS-09100058.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitio Victori defuncto annis XLVIII Herennia Primitiva uxor marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-09100058.translation: To the Divine Shades. Herennia Primitiva, wife, to her husband Domitius Victor, who died at 48 years, deserving well.
EDCS-09100058.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Herennia Primitiva to her husband, Domitius Victor, who died at the age of 48.

EDCS-09100059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabio Caridiano annorum LXXV Fabius Caridianus patri pientissimo et Fabia Aegiale marito optimo bene merenti\n
EDCS-09100059.translation: To the Divine Shades. Fabius Caridianus to his most dutiful father Fabio Caridianus, aged 75, and to his excellent husband deserving well, Fabia Aegiale.
EDCS-09100059.summary: This funerary monument records the dedication by Fabius Caridianus to his father, also named Fabius Caridianus, and to his wife, Fabia Aegiale.

EDCS-09100060.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavinus Flaviae sorori bene mer(enti) vicxix sic annos XX\n
EDCS-09100060.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavinus to his sister Flavia, deserving well, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-09100060.summary: This funerary inscription records Flavinus dedicating a monument to his sister Flavia, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09100061.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Fonteius Antitheus et Antonia Onesicratia viventes fecerunt sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09100061.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Fonteius Antitheus and Antonia Onesicratia, while alive, made this for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-09100061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a couple, Marcus Fonteius Antitheus and Antonia Onesicratia, who established a tomb for themselves and their family while they were alive.

EDCS-09100062.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely records the name Hyacinthus the elder, possibly as a dedication or part of a larger epitaph.

EDCS-09100063.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Blandi nnorum XXVIIII mensium XI dierum XX Iulia COIMOIHO maritho sic\n
EDCS-09100063.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia COIMOIHO to her husband Caius Iulius Blandus, aged 27 years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-09100063.summary: This funerary inscription records Iulia COIMOIHO dedicating a monument to her husband, Caius Iulius Blandus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-09100064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Eutychiae Barbar<u=A>s maritus bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-09100064.translation: To the Divine Shades. Her husband placed [this] for Iulia Eutychia Barbara, deserving well.
EDCS-09100064.summary: This funerary inscription records that a husband placed a monument for his wife, Iulia Eutychia Barbara.

EDCS-09100065.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Tyche Iulia Amica libertae\n
EDCS-09100065.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Amica to her freedwoman Iulia Tyche.
EDCS-09100065.summary: This funerary epitaph records Iulia Amica dedicating a monument to her freedwoman, Iulia Tyche.

EDCS-09100066.text_edition: \n Quinto Iunio Crati Caius Riccius Atimetus et Riccia Nymphe amico\n
EDCS-09100066.translation: Caius Riccius Atimetus and Riccia Nymphe to their friend Quintus Iunius Cratus.
EDCS-09100066.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Caius Riccius Atimetus and Riccia Nymphe to their friend Quintus Iunius Cratus.

EDCS-09100067.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cnaeus L G sibi vivus fecit annorum LX\n

EDCS-09100069.text_edition: \n Manliae Ianuariae amicae I A bene merenti fecit\n

EDCS-09100070.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Marcius Verecundus Marciae Augustinae libertae bene merenti\n

EDCS-09100072.text_edition: \n Marciae Publi filiae Postumae Messeniae Lucillae Aemilia Cai filia Postuma filiae piissimae\n

EDCS-09100074.text_edition: \n Massa annorum XXV Hermes annorum XX hic siti sunt Marcia C atfinibus sic\n

EDCS-09100075.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Meliae annorum XXVIII et Felici nnorum XV memoriam fecit Diadumenus bene merentibus\n

EDCS-09100076.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Otacilia Silvana quae vixi annis XXXX quae feci mihi mimoria sic me viva filius fecit\n

EDCS-09100077.text_edition: \n ete annorum XVII hic sita est Apollonius et He cognatae de suo fecerunt\n

EDCS-09100078.text_edition: \n Sertoriae Quinti filiae Maximae Marcus Antonius Avitus uxori Sertoriae Quinti filiae Maximae Antonia Marci filia Lepida matri\n

EDCS-09100079.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Marci filius Silo Baebia Marci filia Babulla hic sita est Valeria Luci filia Maxsuma nnorum XXII\n
EDCS-09100079.translation: Lucius Valerius Silo, son of Marcus, and Babulla, daughter of Marcus, daughter of Lucius Valerius, are laid here. Valeria Maxsuma, daughter of Lucius, aged 22.
EDCS-09100079.summary: This inscription is a tombstone recording the burial of Lucius Valerius Silo and Babulla, and the death of Valeria Maxsuma at the age of 22.

EDCS-09100080.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Vario Proculo annorum XVI Varia Procula filio pientissimo\n
EDCS-09100080.translation: To the departed spirits. Varia Procula set this up for her most devoted son, Lucius Vario Proculus, aged 16.
EDCS-09100080.summary: This tombstone was erected by Varia Procula for her devoted son, Lucius Vario Proculus, who died at age 16.

EDCS-09100081.text_edition: \n Lucius Venuleius sibi et Venuleiae HOEMI uxori et Venuleio siano fil\n
EDCS-09100081.translation: Lucius Venuleius for himself, his wife Venuleia [---], and his son Venuleius Siano.
EDCS-09100081.summary: This tombstone records Lucius Venuleius dedicating it for himself, his wife, and his son.

EDCS-09100082.text_edition: \n Sibi et suis posuit Dis Manibus Lucius Vinuleius Hesper V Primitivae coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-09100082.translation: Lucius Vinuleius Hesper set this up for himself and his family, and for his deserving wife Primitiva.
EDCS-09100082.summary: This inscription records Lucius Vinuleius Hesper dedicating a monument for himself, his family, and his deserving wife Primitiva.

EDCS-09100083.translation: To Viria Acte, an excellent woman.
EDCS-09100083.summary: This is a dedication to Viria Acte, described as an excellent woman.

EDCS-09100084.translation: Caius Atilius Severus to Viria Acte.
EDCS-09100084.summary: This monument was dedicated by Caius Atilius Severus to Viria Acte.

EDCS-09100085.text_edition: \n Viriae Acte Fabia Luci filia Grattia Maximilla\n
EDCS-09100085.translation: Fabia Grattia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius, to Viria Acte.
EDCS-09100085.summary: This monument was dedicated by Fabia Grattia Maximilla to Viria Acte.

EDCS-09100108.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Manio Philumeno Parthenope marito\n
EDCS-09100108.translation: To the departed spirits. Parthenope for her husband Manius Philumenus.
EDCS-09100108.summary: This tombstone was dedicated by Parthenope to her husband, Manius Philumenus.

EDCS-09100118.text_edition: \n P Luci filia Postumiana Maurae libertae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09100118.translation: P. [Name] set this up for his deserving freedwoman Maura, daughter of Postumius.
EDCS-09100118.summary: This funerary monument was erected by P. [Name] for his deserving freedwoman Maura.

EDCS-09100119.translation: To the unconquered Mithras. Lucanus, a slave.
EDCS-09100119.summary: This dedication to the god Mithras was made by Lucanus, who identifies himself as a slave.

EDCS-09100120.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Salvino patri annorum XL et matri karissimae annorum XL Herclianis sic filius bene merentibus\n
EDCS-09100120.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Salvinus, father, aged 40, and to his dearest mother, aged 40, of the Herculian tribe. Thus a son to his deserving parents.
EDCS-09100120.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a son for his parents, Salvinus and his wife, both aged 40.

EDCS-09100121.translation: Alfia Paulina dedicated this, having fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09100121.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Alfia Paulina.

EDCS-09100123.text_edition: \n Eumenes Marci Proculei servus annorum XL hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09100123.translation: Eumenes, the slave of Marcus Proculeius, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09100123.summary: This is a tombstone for Eumenes, a slave of Marcus Proculeius, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-09100124.text_edition: \n Marcia Quinti filia Alba annorum XL hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-09100124.translation: Marcia Alba, daughter of Quintus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09100124.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcia Alba, daughter of Quintus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-09100127.text_edition: \n Titus Caedius Fuscus annorum XLV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100127.translation: Titus Caedius Fuscus, aged 45, is buried here.
EDCS-09100127.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Caedius Fuscus, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-09100128.summary: This is a tombstone for Caesia.

EDCS-09100129.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Proculus annorum LX Vitoria Omullina annorum XXXV hic siti E sunt\n
EDCS-09100129.translation: Lucius Fabius Proculus, aged 60, and Victoria Omullina, aged 35, are buried here.
EDCS-09100129.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Fabius Proculus and Victoria Omullina, aged 60 and 35 respectively.

EDCS-09100130.text_edition: \n Lucio Sentio Carchedonio Sentiae Thaliae Sentia Carchedonia filia\n
EDCS-09100130.translation: To Lucius Sentius Carchedonius, Sentia Thalia [dedicated this]. Sentia Carchedonia, his daughter.
EDCS-09100130.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Sentia Carchedonia to her husband, Lucius Sentius Carchedonius.

EDCS-09100131.text_edition: \n Lucius Clodius Fabianus annorum LIIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100131.translation: Lucius Clodius Fabianus, aged 54, is buried here.
EDCS-09100131.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Clodius Fabianus, who died at the age of 54.

EDCS-09100132.text_edition: \n Semproniae Melanthidi nnorum LV Quintus Caecilius Leonas fecit\n
EDCS-09100132.translation: Quintus Caecilius Leonas made this for Sempronia Melanthi, aged 55.
EDCS-09100132.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Quintus Caecilius Leonas for Sempronia Melanthi, aged 55.

EDCS-09100133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Postumiae Cai filiae Aprullae flaminicae Saetabaugustanae nnum XVIIII Caius Postumius Successus filiae piissimae et sibi\n
EDCS-09100133.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Postumia Aprulla, daughter of Gaius, Flaminca of Saetabaugustana, aged 19 years. Gaius Postumius Successus [made this] for his most dutiful daughter and for himself.
EDCS-09100133.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Postumia Aprulla and was set up by her father, Gaius Postumius Successus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100134.text_edition: \n Antistia Antulla annorum LXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100134.translation: Antistia Antulla, aged 70 years, lies here.
EDCS-09100134.summary: This is a simple tombstone recording the death of Antistia Antulla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoniae Eucharidis Brinnius Anthinus uxori piae fidelissimae inconparabili nnorum XXXV fecit\n
EDCS-09100135.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Brinnius Anthinus made [this] for his wife Antonia Eucharidis, pious, most faithful, incomparable, aged 35 years.
EDCS-09100135.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Antonia Eucharidis and was dedicated by her husband, Brinnius Anthinus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacr(um) Liciniae Heracliae uxori carissimae mulieri super omnes simplicissimae Quintus Aelius Saturninus\n
EDCS-09100137.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Aelius Saturninus [made this] for his dearest wife Licinia Heraclia, a woman simpler than all others.
EDCS-09100137.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Licinia Heraclia and was dedicated by her husband, Quintus Aelius Saturninus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100140.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Terentiae patronae pientissimae Euphemus libertus\n
EDCS-09100140.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Euphemus, the freedman, [made this] for his most dutiful patroness Terentia.
EDCS-09100140.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Terentia and was dedicated by her freedman Euphemus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100141.text_edition: \n Iuliae Getulae annorum XXXVII mensium VII dierum VII Iulius Fortunalis uxsori innocentissime et inconparabili bene merenti\n
EDCS-09100141.translation: Iulius Fortunalis [made this] for his most innocent and incomparable wife Iulia Getula, aged 37 years, 7 months, and 7 days, who deserved well.
EDCS-09100141.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Getula and was dedicated by her husband, Iulius Fortunalis, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100142.text_edition: \n Publius Clodius Publi libertus Berullus annorum XCIIII\n
EDCS-09100142.translation: Publius Clodius Berullus, freedman of Publius, aged 94 years.
EDCS-09100142.summary: This tombstone records the death of Publius Clodius Berullus, a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100143.text_edition: \n Melpomene Thalia Heuterpe sic Terpsichore Clio Calliope Erato Polymnia Urania\n
EDCS-09100143.translation: Melpomene, Thalia, Euterpe, thus Terpsichore, Clio, Calliope, Erato, Polymnia, Urania.
EDCS-09100143.summary: This inscription lists the nine Muses, possibly as part of a dedication or a symbolic representation, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100144.text_edition: \n Iovi Publius Herennius Abascantus Herennia Rustica Herennia Laudata \n
EDCS-09100144.translation: To Jupiter. Publius Herennius Abascantus, Herennia Rustica, Herennia Laudata.
EDCS-09100144.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Jupiter made by Publius Herennius Abascantus and two women named Herennia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100145.text_edition: \n Fulcinia |(mulieris) liberta Iucunda Fulcinia |(mulieris) liberta Hilara\n
EDCS-09100145.translation: Fulcinia Iucunda, freedwoman of [a woman named] Fulcinia. Fulcinia Hilara, freedwoman of [a woman named] Fulcinia.
EDCS-09100145.summary: This inscription records two freedwomen named Fulcinia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100146.text_edition: \n Templum Nympharum Quintus Sertorius Euporistus Sertorianus et Sertoria Festa uxor a solo ita uti exculptum est in honorem Edetanorum et patronorum suorum sua pecunia fecerunt\n
EDCS-09100146.translation: Quintus Sertorius Euporistus Sertorianus and his wife Sertoria Festa built the Temple of the Nymphs from the ground up, exactly as it is carved, at their own expense, in honor of the Edetani and their patrons.
EDCS-09100146.summary: This inscription records the construction of a Temple of the Nymphs by Quintus Sertorius Euporistus Sertorianus and his wife Sertoria Festa, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100148.text_edition: \n Otaciliae Severae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Marci Iuli Philippi Pii Felicis Augusti ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100148.translation: To Otacilia Severa Augusta, wife of our lord Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100148.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Empress Otacilia Severa, wife of Emperor Philip the Arab, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100150.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Marci filio Galeria Nigrino Curiatio Materno consuli legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Moesiae provinciae Syriae\n
EDCS-09100150.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus Curiatius Maternus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, consul, legate of Augustus with praetorian power in the province of Moesia, in the province of Syria.
EDCS-09100150.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus Curiatius Maternus, a Roman official who served as consul and governor in Moesia and Syria, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100151.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Marci filio Galeria Nigrino Curiatio Materno consuli legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Moesiae provinciae Syriae\n
EDCS-09100151.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus Curiatius Maternus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, consul, legate of Augustus with praetorian power in the province of Moesia, in the province of Syria.
EDCS-09100151.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus Curiatius Maternus, a Roman official who served as consul and governor in Moesia and Syria, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100153.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Marci filio Galeria Nigrino filio\n
EDCS-09100153.translation: To Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100153.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Cornelius Nigrinus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100156.text_edition: \n Lucio Cornelio Luci filio Galeria Potito honore aedilicio functo qui primus pilus in bello Maurico periit Lucius Cornelius Valerianus et Fulvia Zosime filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100156.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Potitus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who held the office of aedile and was the first to perish in the Mauric War as a primus pilus; to their most devoted son, by Lucius Cornelius Valerianus and Fulvia Zosime.
EDCS-09100156.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Cornelius Potitus, aedile and fallen soldier, by his parents Lucius Cornelius Valerianus and Fulvia Zosime, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100157.text_edition: \n Baebio Marci filio Galeria Baebiano Severo IIviro flamini\n
EDCS-09100157.translation: To Baebius Baebianus Severus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, who served as quaestor and flamen.
EDCS-09100157.summary: This inscription honors Baebius Baebianus Severus, a local magistrate and priest of the Galeria tribe, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100158.text_edition: \n Lucio Fabio Fabullo quaestori IIIIviro bis\n
EDCS-09100158.translation: To Lucius Fabius Fabullus, quaestor, duovir twice.
EDCS-09100158.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Fabius Fabullus, who served as quaestor and duovir twice, likely in a Roman colony, in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100159.text_edition: \n Lucio Iunio Iusti filio Galeria Severo IIviro bis flamini bis Iunia Apronia patrono et marito et Iunio Crescentio liberto dignissimo\n
EDCS-09100159.translation: To Lucius Junius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who served twice as duovir and twice as flamen, to their most worthy patron and husband, and to Junius Crescentius, their freedman, by Iunia Apronia.
EDCS-09100159.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iunia Apronia to her husband Lucius Junius Severus and their freedman Junius Crescentius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100160.text_edition: \n Liciniae Luci filiae Celerinae Lucio Caecilio Luci filio Galeria Cassiano omnibus honoribus hic functo Licinia Celerina marito ex testamento Luci Caecili Crassi Cassiani fili\n
EDCS-09100160.translation: To Licinia Celerina, daughter of Lucius, and to Lucius Caecilius Cassianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, who held all the offices here, by Licinia Celerina, from the will of her son Lucius Caecilius Crassus Cassianus.
EDCS-09100160.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Licinia Celerina to her deceased husband Lucius Caecilius Cassianus, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100164.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Quinti filio Galeria Potito Quintillae patronae\n
EDCS-09100164.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Potitus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, patron of Quintilla.
EDCS-09100164.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Quintus Caecilius Potitus, likely set up by Quintilla, his patroness.

EDCS-09100167.translation: To Licinius, son of Licinius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100167.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Licinius, son of Licinius, likely from the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-09100169.translation: To Crescentius, son of Valerius, of the Galeria tribe, grandfather.
EDCS-09100169.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Crescentius, son of Valerius, of the Galeria tribe, identified as a grandfather.

EDCS-09100171.translation: Seranus, son of Tannegiserris, from Otobesa.
EDCS-09100171.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of an individual named Seranus.

EDCS-09100172.text_edition: \n Kaeso A Severus Cornelia Rufilla hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09100172.translation: Kaeso A. Severus and Cornelia Rufilla lie here.
EDCS-09100172.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the burial of Kaeso A. Severus and Cornelia Rufilla.

EDCS-09100174.text_edition: \n Atiliae Marci filiae Potitae Iunia Tannegadinia posuit nepti annorum XX\n
EDCS-09100174.translation: Iunia Tannegadinia set this up for her niece Potita, daughter of Marcius, aged 20.
EDCS-09100174.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Iunia Tannegadinia for her niece Potita, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09100175.text_edition: \n Marcus Baebius Quietus Baebia Aphrodisia Gallicus filius annorum II Harmonia annorum XVI\n
EDCS-09100175.translation: Marcus Baebius Quietus and Baebia Aphrodisia, son Gallicus, aged 2, and Harmonia, aged 16.
EDCS-09100175.summary: This inscription lists names and ages, likely recording the deaths of two children, Marcus Baebius Quietus and Baebia Aphrodisia, and two individuals Gallicus and Harmonia.

EDCS-09100176.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Modestus sibi et Caeciliae Vitali libertae\n
EDCS-09100176.translation: Quintus Caecilius Modestus for himself and for Caecilia Vitalis, his freedwoman.
EDCS-09100176.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Caecilius Modestus for himself and his freedwoman, Caecilia Vitalis.

EDCS-09100177.translation: To his sister Caecilia Thyce, thus.
EDCS-09100177.summary: This inscription, likely a continuation or addendum to another, is a dedication to Caecilia Thyce.

EDCS-09100178.text_edition: \n Publius Clodius Eutychus sibi et Clodiae Natali uxori carissimae\n
EDCS-09100178.translation: Publius Clodius Eutychus for himself and for his dearest wife, Clodia Natalia.
EDCS-09100178.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Clodius Eutychus for himself and his wife, Clodia Natalia.

EDCS-09100179.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius M(arci)? f(ilius) Missus sibi et suis \n
EDCS-09100179.translation: Marcus Cornelius Missus, son of Marcus (?), for himself and his family.
EDCS-09100179.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Cornelius Missus for himself and his relatives.

EDCS-09100180.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Augurinus Cornelia Publi filia Aannorum XXX \n
EDCS-09100180.translation: Lucius Cornelius Augurinus, and Cornelia, daughter of Publius, aged 30.
EDCS-09100180.summary: This inscription records the names and age of two individuals, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-09100182.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Pantherae uxori optumae Lucius Licinius Nicomedes et sibi\n
EDCS-09100182.translation: To the divine spirits of Cornelia Panthera, his excellent wife, Lucius Licinius Nicomedes, and for himself.
EDCS-09100182.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Licinius Nicomedes to his wife Cornelia Panthera and for himself.

EDCS-09100183.text_edition: \n Eupor servus annorum VIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100183.translation: Eupor, a slave, 8 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-09100183.summary: This inscription records the burial of a young slave named Eupor.

EDCS-09100184.text_edition: \n Charite nnorum XVIII Fabia Char<a=E> filiae posuit \n
EDCS-09100184.translation: Fabia Charita dedicated this for her daughter Charite, aged 18.
EDCS-09100184.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Fabia Charita for her daughter Charite.

EDCS-09100185.text_edition: \n Fabiae Pieridi Fabia Crocale filiae et sibi\n
EDCS-09100185.translation: Fabia Crocale for her daughter Fabia Pieris and for herself.
EDCS-09100185.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Fabia Crocale for her daughter Fabia Pieris and for herself.

EDCS-09100186.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Fulvius Achilles vivus fecit Fulvia Marci filia Viticula Fulvia Arbuscula\n
EDCS-09100186.translation: Gnaeus Fulvius Achilles made this while alive, along with Fulvia Viticula, daughter of Marcus, and Fulvia Arbuscula.
EDCS-09100186.summary: This is an inscription recording that Gnaeus Fulvius Achilles, along with Fulvia Viticula and Fulvia Arbuscula, erected this monument during their lifetime.

EDCS-09100187.summary: This inscription simply records the name and age of Lucius Granius Polycletus, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-09100188.text_edition: \n Marcus Horatius Vitalio annorum XII Horatia Vitalis filio et sibi\n
EDCS-09100188.translation: Horatia Vitalis for her son Marcus Horatius Vitalio, aged 12, and for herself.
EDCS-09100188.summary: This funerary dedication by Horatia Vitalis is for her son Marcus Horatius Vitalio and for herself.

EDCS-09100189.text_edition: \n Liciniae Ursinae nnorum XXX Licinius Philon libertus\n
EDCS-09100189.translation: Licinius Philon, his freedman, for Licinia Ursa, aged 30.
EDCS-09100189.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by the freedman Licinius Philon for Licinia Ursa.

EDCS-09100190.text_edition: \n Licinia Vetusta fecit Anthusae sorori piae annorum XXXVIII\n
EDCS-09100190.translation: Licinia Vetusta made this for her dutiful sister Anthusa, aged 38.
EDCS-09100190.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Licinia Vetusta to her sister Anthusa, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100192.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100193.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primigenia hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100193.translation: Primigenia is buried here, to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-09100193.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the deceased Primigenia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100194.text_edition: \n Probo ia Vitalis marito et sibi\n
EDCS-09100194.translation: To Probus and Vitalis for herself.
EDCS-09100194.summary: A funerary epitaph possibly set up by a woman for her husband Probus and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100195.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publiciae Luci filiae Paetinae annorum XXIII Didia Ionice filiae et sibi\n
EDCS-09100195.translation: To Publicia, daughter of Lucius, of the Paetina tribe, aged 23. Didia Ionice for her daughter and herself, to the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-09100195.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Didia Ionice for her deceased daughter Publicia and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100196.text_edition: \n Sempronius --- Lucanus annorum XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100196.translation: Sempronius [---] Lucanus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-09100196.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sempronius [---] Lucanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100197.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sempronia Urbana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100200.text_edition: \n Lucano nnorum LVII a Successa marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-09100200.translation: To Lucanus, aged 57, for her husband who deserved well, from Successa.
EDCS-09100200.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Successa to her well-deserving husband Lucanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100201.text_edition: \n Lucius Turellius Procu<l=B>us annorum XXV hic situs est \n
EDCS-09100201.translation: Lucius Turellius Proculus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-09100201.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Turellius Proculus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100202.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Hylas hic situs est annorum XXV\n
EDCS-09100202.translation: Gaius Valerius Hylas is buried here, aged 25.
EDCS-09100202.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Hylas, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100214.text_edition: \n Lucius Caecilius Luci filius Galeria Certus sibi et Aemiliae Crispinae uxori et Lucio Caecilio Luci filio Galeria Severo filio annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-09100214.translation: Lucius Caecilius Certus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, for himself, and for his wife Aemilia Crispina, and for his son Lucius Caecilius Severus, son of Lucius, aged 18 years.
EDCS-09100214.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Caecilius Certus, his wife Aemilia Crispina, and their son Severus, with the dedicator likely setting it up for his family.

EDCS-09100215.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Grattiae Cai filiae Crispinae annorum XXIIX Caecilia Artemis filiae piissimae et Caio Grattio Polynico nnum XX marito optimo et sibi\n
EDCS-09100215.translation: To the departed spirits. For Grattia Crispina, daughter of Caius, aged 28 years, most devoted daughter of Caecilia Artemis, and for her excellent husband Gaius Grattius Polynicus, aged 20 years, and for herself.
EDCS-09100215.summary: This epitaph commemorates Grattia Crispina, her husband Gaius Grattius Polynicus, and possibly her mother Caecilia Artemis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100216.summary: This brief epitaph records the burial of a woman identified as 'Mother Itus' who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-09100217.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Liciniae Vetustae Lucius Caecilius Nicander uxori optimae\n
EDCS-09100217.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Nicander for his excellent wife Licinia Vetusta.
EDCS-09100217.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Caecilius Nicander setting up a memorial for his wife, Licinia Vetusta.

EDCS-09100218.text_edition: \n C Tempestiva annorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100218.translation: C. Tempestiva, aged 30 years, lies here.
EDCS-09100218.summary: This epitaph records the burial of C. Tempestiva, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-09100219.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Lucius Iulius Placidus at the age of 81.

EDCS-09100222.text_edition: \n Quintus Fabius Augurinus annorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100222.translation: Quintus Fabius Augurinus, aged [X] years, lies here.
EDCS-09100222.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Quintus Fabius Augurinus, with his age partially missing.

EDCS-09100226.text_edition: \n Marco C Quirina Avito superfluenti pecunia quam in mausoleum sibi faciendum et statuam marmoream ponendam testamento impendi iussit\n
EDCS-09100226.translation: For Marcus C. Avitus, of the Quirina tribe, from the surplus money which he ordered by his will to be spent on building a mausoleum for himself and erecting a marble statue.
EDCS-09100226.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous dedication of a mausoleum and statue for Marcus C. Avitus, funded by his will.

EDCS-09100227.text_edition: \n Lucio Otacilio Luci filio Galeria Flavo annorum LXVI Atilia Celsilla marito merentissimo et sibi\n
EDCS-09100227.translation: Lucius Otacilius Flavus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 66 years, for his most deserving husband, Atilia Celsilla, and for herself.
EDCS-09100227.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Otacilius Flavus, who set it up for his deserving husband, and for himself.

EDCS-09100229.text_edition: \n Publius Baebius Leuchio annorum LV et Baebia IA \n
EDCS-09100229.translation: Publius Baebius Leuchius, aged 55 years, and Baebia IA [age missing].
EDCS-09100229.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Baebius Leuchius and Baebia IA, with the age of the latter missing.

EDCS-09100231.summary: This is a tombstone identifying the deceased as Marcus Clodius.

EDCS-09100234.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Cornelius Cio.

EDCS-09100237.summary: This is a tombstone identifying the deceased as Licinius, a Celtiberian of 60 years.

EDCS-09100238.translation: To the departed spirits of Licinius Eutychus, [dedicated by] Eutyches.
EDCS-09100238.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Licinius Eutychus by Eutyches.

EDCS-09100240.text_edition: \n Caio P Castalio annorum L P Campana marito fecit\n
EDCS-09100240.translation: P. Campana made this for her husband, Gaius P. Castalius, aged 50.
EDCS-09100240.summary: This is a tombstone indicating that P. Campana erected it for her husband, Gaius P. Castalius, who died at age 50.

EDCS-09100242.text_edition: \n Lucio Postumio Silvestro Lucius Postumius Ambatus filio annorum XXV et \n
EDCS-09100242.translation: To Lucius Postumius Silvester, Lucius Postumius Ambatus [dedicated this] for his son, aged 25, and...
EDCS-09100242.summary: This incomplete tombstone records a dedication by Lucius Postumius Ambatus for his son, Lucius Postumius Silvester, who died at age 25.

EDCS-09100243.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to Postumia Proba, daughter of Lucius, perhaps on a tombstone.

EDCS-09100244.summary: This very fragmented inscription refers to someone named Privatus.

EDCS-09100245.text_edition: \n Luci filia Severa annorum LXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100245.translation: Lucius' daughter Severa, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-09100245.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Severa, daughter of Lucius, who died at age 70.

EDCS-09100247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victorinae filiae nnorum XX Chrysopolis mater\n
EDCS-09100247.translation: To the departed spirits of Victorina, aged 20, [dedicated by] Chrysopolis her mother.
EDCS-09100247.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Chrysopolis to her daughter Victorina, who died at age 20.

EDCS-09100248.text_edition: \n lius us annorum LXI elia tiva\n
EDCS-09100248.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of two individuals,lius and Elia Tiva.

EDCS-09100258.text_edition: \n Baebia mulieris lib(erta) Lasciva hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100258.translation: Baebia Lasciva, a freedwoman, is laid here.
EDCS-09100258.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebia Lasciva, identifying her as a freedwoman.

EDCS-09100259.text_edition: \n Caius Fabius Cai filius Celsus Galeria Edeta annorum LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100259.translation: Gaius Fabius Celsus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 75, is buried here.
EDCS-09100259.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Fabius Celsus, recording his name, tribal affiliation, and age.

EDCS-09100260.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Chariteni Damanitanae annorum LXX Valeria Charis liberta patronae optimae fecit\n
EDCS-09100260.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Charis, freedwoman, made this for her most excellent patroness Valeriae Chariteni Damanitanae, aged 70.
EDCS-09100260.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a freedwoman, Valeria Charis, for her deceased patroness, Valeriae Chariteni Damanitanae, noting her age.

EDCS-09100261.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Luci filio Galeria Aemiliano filio piissimo annorum XXXII\n
EDCS-09100261.translation: To Lucius Aemilianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, most dutiful son, aged 32.
EDCS-09100261.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Aemilianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, noting his age and filial piety.

EDCS-09100262.text_edition: \n M optime de se meritis et sibi Marcus Clodius Marci filius Galeria Fabianus vivus vivis fecit\n
EDCS-09100262.translation: Marcus Clodius Fabianus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, made this for himself and for those who deserved well of him while he was alive.
EDCS-09100262.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Clodius Fabianus for himself and for those who served him well.

EDCS-09100263.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Valerio Gai filio Galeria Rufino annorum XVII Porcia Charite mater filio pientissimo et s(ibi)\n
EDCS-09100263.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Valerius Rufinus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 17. Porcia Charite, mother, for her most devoted son and for herself.
EDCS-09100263.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by a mother, Porcia Charite, for her deceased son, Gaius Valerius Rufinus, and for herself.

EDCS-09100264.text_edition: \n Marco Porcio Marci filio Galeria Rufo Quintia Marci filia Proba marito posuit\n
EDCS-09100264.translation: Quintia Proba, daughter of Marcus, set up this monument for her husband Marcus Porcius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100264.summary: This monument was erected by Quintia Proba for her husband, Marcus Porcius Rufus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-09100265.text_edition: \n Quintia Proba sibi et Porcio Rufo et Porcio Rufino arcum fecit et statuas superimposuit HS nummum XLmilibus et posteris\n
EDCS-09100265.translation: Quintia Proba made this arch and erected statues on top for herself and for Porcius Rufus and Porcius Rufinus and their descendants, at a cost of 40,000 sesterces.
EDCS-09100265.summary: This is a funerary monument record by Quintia Proba, detailing the construction of an arch with statues for herself, two other individuals, and their descendants, along with the significant cost.

EDCS-09100267.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription recording the name and age of a slave named Aster.

EDCS-09100268.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Lucius Atilius Phileros.

EDCS-09100269.text_edition: \n Aufidius Saturninus annorum LXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100269.translation: Aufidius Saturninus, aged 65, is buried here.
EDCS-09100269.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aufidius Saturninus and his age.

EDCS-09100270.text_edition: \n Domitia Marci Peregrina annorum XXXI T \n
EDCS-09100270.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Domitia Marci Peregrina.

EDCS-09100271.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitiae Tertullae annorum LV\n
EDCS-09100271.translation: To the departed spirits of Domitia Tertulla, aged 55.
EDCS-09100271.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Domitia Tertulla, noting her age.

EDCS-09100273.text_edition: \n Lucius Iunius Cai Prhonimus sic annorum XXVI hic situs (est) \n
EDCS-09100273.translation: Lucius Iunius Cai Prhonimus, aged 26, is buried here.
EDCS-09100273.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial and age of Lucius Iunius Cai Prhonimus.

EDCS-09100275.text_edition: \n Maritima annorum XIX hic sita est Vettia Temporina mater p(osuit)\n
EDCS-09100275.translation: Maritima, aged 19, is buried here. Her mother, Vettia Temporina, placed this.
EDCS-09100275.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Maritima, set up by her mother.

EDCS-09100276.text_edition: \n annorum LXX sibi et Postumiae Cupitae uxori annorum LXV\n
EDCS-09100276.translation: Aged 70, for himself and his wife Postumia Cupita, aged 65.
EDCS-09100276.summary: This funerary inscription records that a man aged 70 dedicated a tomb for himself and his wife, Postumia Cupita, aged 65.

EDCS-09100278.text_edition: \n Terentius Aprilis sic annorum XXV Valeria Primia annorum V \n
EDCS-09100278.translation: Terentius Aprilis, aged 25, and Valeria Primia, aged 5.
EDCS-09100278.summary: This inscription records the ages of Terentius Aprilis and Valeria Primia, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-09100279.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Martialis annorum LX hic situs est Terentia Temporina annorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100279.translation: Marcus Valerius Martialis, aged 60, is buried here. Terentia Temporina, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-09100279.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and ages of Marcus Valerius Martialis and Terentia Temporina.

EDCS-09100280.text_edition: \n Valeria Turpia annorum L Marcus Cornelius Amandus annorum L hic situs est Cornelia Silvana uxsor sic annorum XXXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100280.translation: Valeria Turpia, aged 50, and Marcus Cornelius Amandus, aged 50, are buried here. Cornelia Silvana, wife, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-09100280.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and ages of Valeria Turpia, Marcus Cornelius Amandus, and his wife Cornelia Silvana.

EDCS-09100285.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Marci filius Galeria Potitus annorum LV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100285.translation: Marcus Aemilius Potitus, son of Marcus, aged 55, is buried here.
EDCS-09100285.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius Potitus, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-09100287.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Marci filio annorum et Corneliae Elpidi annorum Marciae Marci filiae Celerae annorum Aemilia Marci filia Nigrina fratri et matri etet viva fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-09100287.translation: Marcia Celeres, daughter of Marcus, made this for her brother and mother, and for herself, while alive, for Marcus Aemilius, son of Marcus, aged [XX], and Cornelia Elpis, aged [XX].
EDCS-09100287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Marcia Celeres for her brother and mother, and also for herself and other family members.

EDCS-09100288.text_edition: \n Gaius Aurelius Reburrinus annorum LX Cassia Agile annorum XXXI\n
EDCS-09100288.translation: Gaius Aurelius Reburrinus, aged 60, and Cassia Agile, aged 31.
EDCS-09100288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Gaius Aurelius Reburrinus and Cassia Agile.

EDCS-09100289.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Aequali annorum XX et Cornelio Carpo annorum XXX Harmonia \n
EDCS-09100289.translation: Harmonia made this for Marcus Cornelius Aequalis, aged 20, and Cornelius Carpus, aged 30.
EDCS-09100289.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Harmonia for Marcus Cornelius Aequalis and Cornelius Carpus.

EDCS-09100290.text_edition: \n Publio Domitio Sabino et Fabiae Atticae statuas posuit\n
EDCS-09100290.translation: He placed the statues for Publius Domitius Sabinus and Fabia Attica.
EDCS-09100290.summary: This inscription records the placement of statues for Publius Domitius Sabinus and Fabia Attica.

EDCS-09100291.translation: To L. Agricola, from L. Exoriens, his brother.
EDCS-09100291.summary: This is a dedication by L. Exoriens to his brother, L. Agricola.

EDCS-09100292.text_edition: \n Lucusta serva annorum VIII Graecina annorum XII\n
EDCS-09100292.translation: Lucusta, a slave girl, aged 8, and Graecina, aged 12.
EDCS-09100292.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of two young slave girls, Lucusta and Graecina.

EDCS-09100293.text_edition: \n Marcia Luci filia Marcella annorum IIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100293.translation: Marcia Marcella, daughter of Lucius, aged 4, is buried here.
EDCS-09100293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcia Marcella, a young child aged 4.

EDCS-09100294.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Otaciliae Seranae nnorum XVIII Otacilius Seranus filiae naturali et Otacilia Chrysopolis filiae pientissimae hic sitae\n
EDCS-09100294.translation: To the divine shades: Otacilia Serana, aged 18, and Otacilia Chrysopolis, her most devoted daughter, are buried here, as well as Otacilius Seranus for his natural daughter.
EDCS-09100294.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) and records the burial of Otacilia Serana, Otacilia Chrysopolis, and mentions Otacilius Seranus.

EDCS-09100295.text_edition: \n Lucius Porcius Marci filius Probus annorum XXV hic situs est Lucius Porcius L(uci) filius Aureus annorum XV hic situs est Valeria Marci filia Crassilla annorum hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100295.translation: Lucius Porcius Probus, son of Marcus, aged 25, is buried here. Lucius Porcius Aureus, son of Lucius, aged 15, is buried here. Valeria Crassilla, daughter of Marcus, aged [XX], is buried here.
EDCS-09100295.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of three individuals: Lucius Porcius Probus (25), Lucius Porcius Aureus (15), and Valeria Crassilla.

EDCS-09100296.summary: This is a tombstone for Sicilia, a slave woman who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-09100297.text_edition: \n Lucius Terentius Iuvenis annorum XXV hic situs est Paternus \n
EDCS-09100297.translation: Lucius Terentius Iuvenis, 25 years old, is buried here. Paternus.
EDCS-09100297.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Terentius Iuvenis, who died at 25, possibly commissioned by Paternus.

EDCS-09100298.text_edition: \n Aemiliae Tertullae annorum LX Sergia Repentina matri\n
EDCS-09100298.translation: To Aemilia Tertulla, 60 years old, from her mother Sergia Repentina.
EDCS-09100298.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Sergia Repentina to her mother Aemilia Tertulla, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-09100299.text_edition: \n anus annorum XIIII hic situs est mater Fabiorum liberta annorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100299.translation: An old woman, 14 years old, is buried here. Her mother, Fabia, freedwoman, 30 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-09100299.summary: This inscription records two burials: an unnamed 14-year-old girl and her mother Fabia, a freedwoman who died at 30.

EDCS-09100301.text_edition: \n Quintia Surula annorum X hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100301.translation: Quintia Surula, 10 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-09100301.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintia Surula, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-09100302.text_edition: \n Astedumae annorum LXXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100302.translation: Asteduma, 80 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-09100302.summary: This is a tombstone for Asteduma, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-09100303.translation: Private road of Marcus Baebius Severinus.
EDCS-09100303.summary: This inscription indicates the dedication or ownership of a private road belonging to Marcus Baebius Severinus.

EDCS-09100304.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Galeria Seranus annorum L Marcus Aemilius Marci filius Galeria Valerianus annorum XIIII hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09100304.translation: Marcus Aemilius Galeria Seranus, 50 years old, and Marcus Aemilius, son of Marcus, Galeria Valerianus, 14 years old, are buried here.
EDCS-09100304.summary: This is a tombstone for two individuals, Marcus Aemilius Galeria Seranus (50) and Marcus Aemilius Valerianus (14).

EDCS-09100305.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius Silanus nnorum LXX Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus nnorum XL\n
EDCS-09100305.translation: Lucius Aemilius Silanus, 70 years old, and Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus, 40 years old.
EDCS-09100305.summary: This inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Lucius Aemilius Silanus (70) and Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus (40), likely on a shared tomb.

EDCS-09100306.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Marci filius Severus annorum LXX Sempronia Luci filia Antulla annorum L hic siti sunt Fabia Marci filia Severa annorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100306.translation: Marcus Fabius Severus, son of Marcus, 70 years old, Sempronia Antulla, daughter of Lucius, 50 years old, and Fabia Severa, daughter of Marcus, 50 years old, are buried here.
EDCS-09100306.summary: This inscription records the burial of three people: Marcus Fabius Severus (70), Sempronia Antulla (50), and Fabia Severa (50).

EDCS-09100308.text_edition: \n Iulia Aemilia annorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Aemilia, aged 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100311.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Marci filiae Marcellae nnum LXV Lucius Antonius Placidus matri optimae\n
EDCS-09100311.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Cornelia Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 65. Lucius Antonius Placidus made this for his most excellent mother.
EDCS-09100311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Antonius Placidus for his mother, Cornelia Marcella, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-09100312.text_edition: \n Cornelia Placida annorum XXX hic sita est Gaius Viccius Urbanus annorum L hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100312.translation: Cornelia Placida, aged 30, rests here. Gaius Viccius Urbanus, aged 50, rests here.
EDCS-09100312.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Cornelia Placida (aged 30) and Gaius Viccius Urbanus (aged 50).

EDCS-09100313.text_edition: \n Cornelia Luci filia Proba annorum LX hic sita est Lucius Antonius Luci filius Blastinus annorum LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100313.translation: Cornelia Proba, daughter of Lucius, aged 60, rests here. Lucius Antonius Blastinus, son of Lucius, aged 75, rests here.
EDCS-09100313.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for Cornelia Proba (aged 60) and Lucius Antonius Blastinus (aged 75).

EDCS-09100314.text_edition: \n Mario Euschemo nnorum XXXVII Maria Urbana marito incomparabili fecit\n
EDCS-09100314.translation: Maria Urbana made this for her incomparable husband, Mario Euschemos, aged 37.
EDCS-09100314.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Maria Urbana for her husband, Mario Euschemos, who died at the age of 37.

EDCS-09100316.text_edition: \n Severa Gaius Viccius Bargates annorum LXX hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09100316.translation: Severa and Gaius Viccius Bargates, aged 70, rest here.
EDCS-09100316.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Severa and Gaius Viccius Bargates, both aged 70.

EDCS-09100318.text_edition: \n Publius Marius Initialis et Maria Procope hic siti sunt Antonia Placida \n
EDCS-09100318.translation: Publius Marius Initialis and Maria Procope rest here. Antonia Placida...
EDCS-09100318.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Marius Initialis and Maria Procope, with an incomplete mention of Antonia Placida.

EDCS-09100319.text_edition: \n Marcus Porcius ESCERIOR annorum LXX hic situs est Marcus Porcius Nigrinus miles legionis XXI rapacis P LAIRII PRO P P\n
EDCS-09100319.translation: Marcus Porcius, aged 70, rests here. Marcus Porcius Nigrinus, soldier of the XXI Rapax legion, prefect of the province.
EDCS-09100319.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Porcius, aged 70, and Marcus Porcius Nigrinus, a soldier of the XXI Rapax legion and a provincial prefect.

EDCS-09100320.text_edition: \n Marcus Marius Lascivos hic situs est tu qui praeteriens spectas aram quam fecit casus poni quo sistis lege et invenies quit sic mihi contigerit trimus ego et mensum iam sex tuam lucem sensi\n
EDCS-09100320.translation: Marcus Marius Lascivos rests here. You who pass by and look at this altar, which chance placed here, stop and read, and you will find out what happened to me; I was three years old and six months when I felt your light.
EDCS-09100320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Lascivos, who died at the age of three and a half, accompanied by a lament and a description of the altar.

EDCS-09100321.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name or a dedication, possibly to a deity or a person named Durnacos.

EDCS-09100322.text_edition: \n Asclepio Augusto ex testamento Asini Heliodori in honorem Asini \n
EDCS-09100322.translation: To Asclepius Augustus, from the will of Asinius Heliodorus, in honor of Asinius.
EDCS-09100322.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Asclepius Augustus, funded by the will of Asinius Heliodorus.

EDCS-09100326.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Fidus Isidi Pelagiae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100326.translation: Lucius Valerius Fidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Isis Pelagia.
EDCS-09100326.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Isis Pelagia, dedicated by Lucius Valerius Fidus.

EDCS-09100327.translation: To the Augustan Lares, from a vow of Fabius.
EDCS-09100327.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Augustan Lares, made by Fabius.

EDCS-09100329.translation: To Mercury Augustus, Lucius Baebius Eunomus.
EDCS-09100329.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury Augustus by Lucius Baebius Eunomus.

EDCS-09100330.text_edition: \n Lucius Antonius Seleuci libertus Florus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100330.translation: Lucius Antonius Florus, freedman of Seleucus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09100330.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Lucius Antonius Florus, a freedman.

EDCS-09100337.text_edition: \n Caio Caesari Augusti filio pontifici consuli designato principi iuventutis\n
EDCS-09100337.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, pontifex, consul-designate, prince of youth.
EDCS-09100337.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, listing his various important titles and offices.

EDCS-09100338.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari Augusti filio Augusto testamento Cnaei Baebi i\n
EDCS-09100338.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of Augustus, Augustus, from the will of Gnaeus Baebius.
EDCS-09100338.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Tiberius Caesar Augustus, made according to the will of Gnaeus Baebius.

EDCS-09100339.text_edition: \n Germanico Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio deivi sic Augusti nepoti Deivi sic Iuli pronepoti consuli II imperatori II\n
EDCS-09100339.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Julius, consul II, imperator II.
EDCS-09100339.summary: This inscription honors Germanicus Caesar, detailing his imperial lineage and consulships.

EDCS-09100340.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio deivi sic Augusti nepoti Deivi sic Iuli pronepoti consuli\n
EDCS-09100340.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Julius, consul.
EDCS-09100340.summary: This inscription honors Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, detailing his imperial lineage and consulship.

EDCS-09100344.summary: This is a simple dedication to Antoninus Augustus.

EDCS-09100345.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Valeriano nobilissimo Caesari \n
EDCS-09100345.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-09100345.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100346.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Invicto Pio Felici imperatori consuli pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae proconsuli\n
EDCS-09100346.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate, Consul, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-09100346.summary: This inscription lists the full titles of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Claudius, dating to his reign in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100348.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Aurelianus, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100349.text_edition: \n Imperatori Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli\n
EDCS-09100349.translation: To Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul.
EDCS-09100349.summary: This inscription lists the full titles of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Carinus, dating to his reign in the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100359.text_edition: \n Publio Scipioni consuli imperatori ob restitutam Saguntum ex senatus consulto bello Punico secundo\n
EDCS-09100359.translation: To Publius Scipio, Consul and Imperator, by decree of the Senate, for the restoration of Saguntum during the Second Punic War.
EDCS-09100359.summary: This inscription honors Publius Scipio for his actions in restoring Saguntum during the Second Punic War, a significant event in the mid-2nd century BCE.

EDCS-09100361.text_edition: \n Paullo Aemilio Paulli filio Palatina Regillo viro sacris faciendis praefecto urbis iuri dicundo quaestori Tiberi Caesaris Augusti patrono\n
EDCS-09100361.translation: To Paullus Aemilius Paulli filius, of the Palatine tribe, Regillus, priest, prefect of the city, judge, quaestor of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, his patron.
EDCS-09100361.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Paullus Aemilius, listing his various public and religious offices, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-09100363.text_edition: \n Lucio? Baebio Luci filio Galeria Hispano Pompeio Marcell Antonio Siloni Paulino \n
EDCS-09100363.translation: To Lucius? Baebius Luci filius, Galeria, Hispanus, Pompeius Marcellus, Antonius Silo, Paulinus.
EDCS-09100363.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly a dedication or a record of individuals, with uncertain dating.

EDCS-09100365.text_edition: \n Marco Acilio Marci filio Cornelia Rufo procuratori Caesarum conventus Tarrachonensis sic\n
EDCS-09100365.translation: To Marcus Acilius Marci filius, Cornelia, Rufus, procurator of the Emperors, of the conventus of Tarraco.
EDCS-09100365.summary: This inscription records Marcus Acilius Rufus as a procurator of the emperors for the administrative district of Tarraco, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100366.text_edition: \n Marco Acilio Marci filio Cornelia Rufo procuratori Caesarum ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100366.translation: To Marcus Acilius Marci filius, Cornelia, Rufus, procurator of the Emperors, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100366.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Acilius Rufus as procurator of the emperors, based on a decree of the local town council, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100367.text_edition: \n Caio Cornelio Quinti filio Galeria Restituto Grattio Ceriali tribuno militum legionis III Gallicae tribuno militum cohortis I Gallicae civium Romanorum praefecto alae \n
EDCS-09100367.translation: To Gaius Cornelius Quinti filius, Galeria, Restitutus, Grattius Cerialis, military tribune of the III Gallica Legion, military tribune of the I Cohors Gallica Civium Romanorum, prefect of a cavalry wing.
EDCS-09100367.summary: This inscription details the military career of Gaius Cornelius Restitutus, listing his various equestrian and tribunate positions, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100369.text_edition: \n Lucio Fulvio Luci filio Lessoni tribuno militum divi Augusti Quintus Fabius Niger Quinti et Luci pater avo materno\n
EDCS-09100369.translation: To Lucius Fulvius Lessus, son of Lucius, military tribune of the divine Augustus, Quintus Fabius Niger, father and maternal grandfather, [made this dedication].
EDCS-09100369.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Fabius Niger to his father and maternal grandfather, Lucius Fulvius Lessus, who was a military tribune of Augustus, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-09100381.text_edition: \n Marcus Acilius Luci filius Fontanus eripuit nobeis sic undevicensumus sic annus ingressum iuvenem militiam cupide Parcae falluntur Fontanum quae rapuerunt cum sit perpetuo fama futura viri XXII\n
EDCS-09100381.translation: Marcus Acilius Fontanus, son of Lucius, snatched us away while he was eagerly entering military service at the age of nineteen; the Fates are deceived, for Fontanus, who was snatched away, will have eternal fame, as will the 22 men.
EDCS-09100381.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Marcus Acilius Fontanus, commemorating his eager entry into military service at a young age and his early death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100382.text_edition: \n Elaesus Coelonis filius eques ala Asturum III turma Nasonis stipendiorum V nnum Lucius Annius Odephorus Manlia Cnaei filia Procula Cnaeus Manlius Auctus\n
EDCS-09100382.translation: Elaesus, son of Coelo, cavalryman of the Ala Asturum, third troop of Nason, after 5 years of service. Lucius Annius Odephorus, Procula daughter of Manlia and Gnaeus, Gnaeus Manlius Auctus.
EDCS-09100382.summary: This inscription records the service of Elaesus, a cavalryman from the Ala Asturum, and lists other individuals, possibly his family or fellow soldiers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100383.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Luci filio Galeria Gallo aedili IIviro flamini II Saliorum magistro quaestori pontifici Aemilia Luci filia Severa filio\n
EDCS-09100383.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Gallus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen of the Salii, master of the quaestors, pontiff. His son.
EDCS-09100383.summary: This is a dedication by Aemilia Severa to her son, Lucius Aemilius Gallus, who held several prominent magistracies and religious offices in his community, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100385.text_edition: \n Marco Baebio Marci filio Galeria Crispo aedili pontifici Salio conlusores\n
EDCS-09100385.translation: To Marcus Baebius Crispus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, pontiff. His fellow students.
EDCS-09100385.summary: This is a dedication by fellow students to Marcus Baebius Crispus, who served as aedile and pontiff, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100386.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Baebio Cnaei filio Galeria Gemino pontifici aedili Salio decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100386.translation: To Gnaeus Baebius Geminus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, pontiff, aedile, Salian priest, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100386.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gnaeus Baebius Geminus for his service as pontiff, aedile, and Salian priest, made by decree of the decurions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100387.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Baebio Cnaei filio Galeria Gemino municipes Saguntini\n
EDCS-09100387.translation: To Gnaeus Baebius Geminus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, by the citizens of Saguntum.
EDCS-09100387.summary: This is a dedication by the citizens of Saguntum to Gnaeus Baebius Geminus, likely honoring him for public service, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100388.text_edition: \n Publio Baebio Luci filio Galeria Maximo Iuliano aedili flamini Popillia Avita ex testamento Cai Popilli Cupiti patris\n
EDCS-09100388.translation: To Publius Baebius Julianus Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, flamen. Popillia Avita, from the will of her father, Caius Popillius Cupitus.
EDCS-09100388.summary: This is a dedication by Popillia Avita, according to her father's will, to Publius Baebius Julianus Maximus, who served as aedile and flamen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100389.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Quinti filio Galeria Rufo aedili IIviro pontifici equo publico honorato \n
EDCS-09100389.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, pontiff, honored with the public horse.
EDCS-09100389.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Quintus Caecilius Rufus for his civic and religious offices, including aedile, duovir, and pontiff, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100390.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Publi? filio Galeria Valeriano aedili IIviro II quaestori flamini pontifici \n
EDCS-09100390.translation: To Quintus Valerianus, son of Publius(?), of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, quaestor, flamen, pontiff.
EDCS-09100390.summary: This inscription lists the extensive public and religious offices held by Quintus Valerianus, including aedile, duovir, quaestor, flamen, and pontiff, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100391.text_edition: \n Marco Calpurnio Marci filio Galeria Luperco aedili IIviro pontifici Manlia Cnaei filia\n
EDCS-09100391.translation: To Marcus Calpurnius Lupercus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, and pontiff, from Manlia, daughter of Cnaeus.
EDCS-09100391.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Calpurnius Lupercus, aedile, duovir, and pontiff, and is dedicated by Manlia.

EDCS-09100392.text_edition: \n Caius Cornelius Maximus Valentinus aedilis IIvir Fabia Marcellina uxor Saguntina hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09100392.translation: Gaius Cornelius Maximus Valentinus, aedile and duovir, and his wife Fabia Marcellina of Saguntum, have decreed that this monument shall not follow an heir.
EDCS-09100392.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription for Gaius Cornelius Maximus Valentinus and his wife Fabia Marcellina, stating the property will not pass to heirs.

EDCS-09100393.text_edition: \n Quinto Fabio Cnaei filio Galeria Gemino pontifici Salio decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100393.translation: To Quintus Fabius Geminus, son of Cnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, pontiff and Salian priest, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100393.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Fabius Geminus, pontiff and Salian priest, following a decree by the local council.

EDCS-09100394.text_edition: \n Q(uinto) Fabio Quinti filio Galeria Nigro IIviro pontifici ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100394.translation: To Quintus Fabius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, duovir and pontiff, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100394.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Fabius Niger, duovir and pontiff, by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-09100395.text_edition: \n Fulvius filius Titinianus Caius Lucilius Luci filius viri ex decreto decurionum turris et muros reficiendos coeraverunt\n
EDCS-09100395.translation: Fulvius, son of Fulvius, and Titinianus Gaius Lucilius, son of Lucius, as men (or citizens), by decree of the decurions, saw to the repair of the tower and walls.
EDCS-09100395.summary: This inscription records that Fulvius and Titinianus Gaius Lucilius, by decree of the decurions, supervised the repair of a tower and walls.

EDCS-09100396.text_edition: \n Caio Licinio Quinti filio Galeria Campano aedili IIviro II flamini ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100396.translation: To Gaius Licinius Campanus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, and flamen, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100396.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Licinius Campanus, aedile, duovir, and flamen, by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-09100398.text_edition: \n Quinto Varvio Quinti filio Galeria Celeri aedili IIviro flamini bis Saliorum magistro quaestori Publius Baebius Venustus amico\n
EDCS-09100398.translation: To Quintus Varvius Celer, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen twice, master of the Salii, and quaestor, from Publius Baebius Venustus, a friend.
EDCS-09100398.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Varvius Celer, who held multiple public offices, and is dedicated by his friend Publius Baebius Venustus.

EDCS-09100399.text_edition: \n Caio Voconio Cai filio Galeria Placido aedili IIviro II flamini II quaestori Saliorum magistro Caius Licinius Cai filius Galeria Marinus Voconius Romanus patri optimo Popilliae Luci filiae Rectinae nnorum XVIII Caius Licinius Cai filius Galeria Marinus Voconius Romanus uxori\n
EDCS-09100399.translation: To Gaius Voconius Placidus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, duovir, flamen twice, quaestor twice, master of the Salii. Gaius Licinius Marinus Voconius Romanus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, to his excellent father. Popillia Rectina, daughter of Lucius, aged 18. Gaius Licinius Marinus Voconius Romanus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, to his wife.
EDCS-09100399.summary: This inscription contains multiple dedications: one for Gaius Voconius Placidus, who held numerous offices, and two funerary dedications by Gaius Licinius Marinus Voconius Romanus for his father and wife.

EDCS-09100402.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anerio r X primo pontifici Fulvia marito C \n
EDCS-09100402.translation: To the Divine Shades. Anerius X Primus, pontiff. Fulvia [dedicated this] to her husband C...
EDCS-09100402.summary: This is a funerary inscription to Anerius X Primus, a pontiff, dedicated by his wife Fulvia.

EDCS-09100403.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Luci filio Galeria Calvo ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100403.translation: To Lucius Licinius Calvus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09100403.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Licinius Calvus, by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-09100404.text_edition: \n Publio Popillio Publi filio Galeria Avito ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09100404.translation: To Publius Popillius Avitus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, by decree of the local councilors.
EDCS-09100404.summary: This inscription records a public honor bestowed upon Publius Popillius Avitus by local officials.

EDCS-09100411.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Aeli Caerialis magistro artis grammaticae Lucius Aelius Aelianus libertus patrono bene merito vixit nnis LXXXV\n
EDCS-09100411.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Aelius Aelianus, freedman, erected this for his well-deserving patron, Lucius Aelius Caerialis, a master of grammar, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-09100411.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aelius Caerialis, a grammar teacher, set up by his freedman.

EDCS-09100413.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Narcissus Saguntinus vivus fecit sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09100413.translation: Publius Cornelius Narcissus of Saguntum, while alive, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-09100413.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Publius Cornelius Narcissus for himself and his household.

EDCS-09100414.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Marci filio Galeria Favonio Iucundus libertus fecit cum Iucundo et Pudente filis\n
EDCS-09100414.translation: Iucundus, freedman, made this for Marcus Aemilius Favonius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, along with his sons Iucundus and Pudens.
EDCS-09100414.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a freedman, Iucundus, for his patron Marcus Aemilius Favonius and his sons.

EDCS-09100416.translation: Maxumus, son of Aemilius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100416.summary: This inscription appears to record the name and tribal affiliation of an individual, possibly on a tombstone or public marker.

EDCS-09100418.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Baebius Cnaei filius Galeria Placidus Baebia Hospita uxor\n
