EDCS-09100418.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Placidus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe; Baebia Hospita, his wife.
EDCS-09100418.summary: This inscription names a couple, Gnaeus Baebius Placidus and his wife Baebia Hospita, likely on a shared tomb.

EDCS-09100419.text_edition: \n Caio Fabio Recti filio Galeria Probo pater\n
EDCS-09100419.translation: Probus, father, for Gaius Fabius Rectus, son of Rectus, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-09100419.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a father, Probus, for his son, Gaius Fabius Rectus.

EDCS-09100421.translation: Lucius Aemilius Macer for his daughter Aemilia.
EDCS-09100421.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely funerary, made by Lucius Aemilius Macer for his daughter Aemilia.

EDCS-09100424.text_edition: \n Antonia Villana Se sibi et suis IV\n
EDCS-09100424.translation: Antonia Villana for herself and her family, IV.
EDCS-09100424.summary: This inscription records Antonia Villana dedicating a monument for herself and her household, possibly related to a burial or family plot.

EDCS-09100426.text_edition: \n Titi filio Vitulo Iuliensi annorum ae Marci filiae Marcellae annorum XXXV Vitulo filio annorum XX us libertus ex testamento fecit\n
EDCS-09100426.translation: A freedman, by his will, made this for Vitulo, son of Titus, of Iuliensis, aged [unknown]; for Marcella, daughter of Marcus, aged 35 years; for Vitulo, son, aged 20 years.
EDCS-09100426.summary: This funerary monument was erected according to a will for several individuals: Vitulo (son of Titus), Marcella (daughter of Marcus), and another son named Vitulo.

EDCS-09100430.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription naming Marcus Aemilius Quietus.

EDCS-09100431.text_edition: \n Lucius Aemilius filius Tartigap Pompeia P uxsor sic\n
EDCS-09100431.translation: Lucius Aemilius, son of Tartigap, and Pompeia, his wife. Thus.
EDCS-09100431.summary: This inscription names Lucius Aemilius, son of Tartigap, and his wife Pompeia, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-09100432.summary: This inscription names Aimilia Fabia Paul, likely for a funerary monument.

EDCS-09100434.summary: This inscription identifies Philaenis as the freedwoman of someone named Marcus.

EDCS-09100435.summary: This inscription names Lucius An Phi and Baebia, possibly as dedicants or deceased individuals.

EDCS-09100437.summary: This inscription mentions Antonius and an age of thirty, likely part of a funerary or military record.

EDCS-09100438.translation: Maxima, daughter of Argentaria and Lucius.
EDCS-09100438.summary: This inscription names Maxima, daughter of Argentaria and Lucius, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-09100440.translation: Baebia and Lucius Baebius, ... years old.
EDCS-09100440.summary: This inscription names Baebia and Lucius Baebius along with an age, suggesting a funerary monument.

EDCS-09100441.translation: To Publius Baebius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-09100441.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Baebius, son of Lucius, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-09100443.text_edition: \n Vivi fecerunt Cnaeus Baebius Eros Chilonianus Oppia Montana uxor in suis horteis sic siti sunt hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes XXIII in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-09100443.translation: Cnaeus Baebius Eros and his wife Oppia Montana made this in their gardens. Thus, this monument is situated and will not pass to an heir. In the front 23 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-09100443.summary: This funerary monument, erected by Cnaeus Baebius Eros and his wife Oppia Montana in their garden, specifies plot dimensions and that it is not inheritable.

EDCS-09100445.text_edition: \n Cn(aeus) Baebius Inventus sibi et filis suis et Hedone libertae fecit\n
EDCS-09100445.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Inventus made this for himself, his children, and Hedone, his freedwoman.
EDCS-09100445.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the self-funded burial arrangements of Gnaeus Baebius Inventus for himself, his children, and his freedwoman.

EDCS-09100446.text_edition: \n Lucius Baebius Optatus annorum XLV Baebia Euche viro et sibi\n
EDCS-09100446.translation: Lucius Baebius Optatus, aged 45, [made this for] Baebia Euche, his wife, and for himself.
EDCS-09100446.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Lucius Baebius Optatus, aged 45, set up this monument for his wife Baebia Euche and himself.

EDCS-09100447.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of Baebius Paganus, possibly on a tombstone or other marker.

EDCS-09100448.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Lucius Baebius Philippus, with context for his family or associates missing.

EDCS-09100449.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Baebius Pylades et Cnaeus Baebius \n
EDCS-09100449.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Pylades and Gnaeus Baebius [set this up?].
EDCS-09100449.summary: This fragmentary inscription names two individuals, Gnaeus Baebius Pylades and Gnaeus Baebius, likely as dedicants or beneficiaries of the monument.

EDCS-09100450.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Baebius Cnaei libertus Repentinus Baebia Cnaei liberta Eucole Nome filia annorum XIV\n
EDCS-09100450.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Repentinus, freedman of Gnaeus, and Baebia Eucole, freedwoman of Gnaeus, daughter of Nome, aged 14.
EDCS-09100450.summary: This inscription records the names and ages of two individuals, Gnaeus Baebius Repentinus and Baebia Eucole, likely as deceased family members commemorated on a monument.

EDCS-09100451.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Baebius Globuli libertus libertus Salvius \n
EDCS-09100451.translation: Gnaeus Baebius Salvius, freedman of Globuli, [and?] freedman [of Gnaeus?].
EDCS-09100451.summary: This inscription records the name Gnaeus Baebius Salvius and his status as a freedman, with some ambiguity about his patrons.

EDCS-09100452.text_edition: \n Lucius Baebius Th annorum I Iuniae P uxori \n
EDCS-09100452.translation: Lucius Baebius [name?], aged 2 years, [set this up for?] Iunia P[---], his wife.
EDCS-09100452.summary: This funerary inscription records the very young age of Lucius Baebius and likely commemorates him along with his wife, Iunia P[---].

EDCS-09100458.translation: Baebia, freedwoman of Lucius, [and?] [name?], to [her?] dearest [family members?].
EDCS-09100458.summary: This fragmentary inscription records Baebia, a freedwoman of Lucius, and seems to be dedicating the monument to beloved family members.

EDCS-09100460.text_edition: \n Baebia Megale Nymphidiae libertae annorum XII et Saturninae \n
EDCS-09100460.translation: Baebia Megale, for Nymphidia, her freedwoman, aged 12, and Saturnina.
EDCS-09100460.summary: This inscription records Baebia Megale setting up a memorial for her freedwoman Nymphidia, aged 12, and for Saturnina.

EDCS-09100461.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebiae Niceni Felix uxori dulcissimae\n
EDCS-09100461.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Felix to his dearest wife, Baebia Niceni.
EDCS-09100461.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Felix to his wife, Baebia Niceni.

EDCS-09100462.text_edition: \n Viva vivo fecit Baebia Placida sibi et Quinto Geminio \n
EDCS-09100462.translation: Baebia Placida made this for herself and for Quintus Geminio while she was alive.
EDCS-09100462.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-provided tombstone made by Baebia Placida for herself and Quintus Geminio.

EDCS-09100463.translation: Baebia, freedwoman of Cnaeus, from Tavaccalaur.
EDCS-09100463.summary: This inscription identifies Baebia, a freedwoman of Cnaeus, possibly indicating her origin from Tavaccalaur.

EDCS-09100466.text_edition: \n Vivi fecerunt Quintus Caecilius Epagathio Caecilia Aucta uxor Caecilia Helene filia annorum XXII\n
EDCS-09100466.translation: The survivors, Quintus Caecilius Epagathio, his wife Caecilia Aucta, and their daughter Caecilia Helene, aged 22, made this.
EDCS-09100466.summary: This funerary inscription records a tombstone erected by Quintus Caecilius Epagathio and his family for their deceased daughter Caecilia Helene.

EDCS-09100467.text_edition: \n Caecilius Eutichus annorum XL Babullia Successa viva fecit\n
EDCS-09100467.translation: Babullia Successa, aged 40, made this while alive.
EDCS-09100467.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-provided tombstone made by Babullia Successa.

EDCS-09100468.text_edition: \n Publio Calpurnio Harmonico annorum XXV Calpurnia\n
EDCS-09100468.summary: This inscription commemorates Publius Calpurnius Harmonicus, aged 25, likely on his tombstone.

EDCS-09100469.text_edition: \n Calpurnia Quinti filia Atilia Quinti filia Prisca\n
EDCS-09100469.translation: Calpurnia, daughter of Quintus; Atilia Prisca, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-09100469.summary: This inscription lists two women, Calpurnia and Atilia Prisca, both daughters of a Quintus, possibly as witnesses or part of a family record.

EDCS-09100471.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Claudio Rogatello annorum V mensium IIII dierum \n
EDCS-09100471.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: To Gaius Claudius Rogatellus, aged 5 years, 4 months, [and some] days.
EDCS-09100471.summary: This funerary inscription records the early death of Gaius Claudius Rogatellus, aged 5 years and 4 months.

EDCS-09100473.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that possibly names Clodia Procula.

EDCS-09100476.summary: This inscription commemorates Cornelius Aper, aged 38, likely on his tombstone.

EDCS-09100477.translation: Cornelius Onesimus for himself and his family.
EDCS-09100477.summary: A funerary inscription recording Cornelius Onesimus dedicating the monument for himself and his family.

EDCS-09100478.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Zosimus Aemilia Pusilla uxor sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09100478.translation: Publius Cornelius Zosimus and his wife Aemilia Pusilla for themselves and their families.
EDCS-09100478.summary: A funerary inscription likely recording Publius Cornelius Zosimus and his wife Aemilia Pusilla dedicating a tomb for their families.

EDCS-09100479.text_edition: \n Corneliae Secunda Quint--- M nnorum XXXII\n
EDCS-09100479.translation: For Cornelia Secunda, daughter of Quintus, 32 years old.
EDCS-09100479.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Cornelia Secunda, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-09100480.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Auge nnorum XXX\n
EDCS-09100480.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Cornelia Auge, 30 years old.
EDCS-09100480.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, recording the age of Cornelia Auge.

EDCS-09100481.text_edition: \n Cornelia Cai liberta Auge Secunda soror hic sitae sunt\n
EDCS-09100481.translation: Cornelia Auge, freedwoman of Caius, and her sister Secunda are buried here.
EDCS-09100481.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Cornelia Auge, a freedwoman of Caius, and her sister Secunda are buried together.

EDCS-09100482.summary: A funerary inscription identifying Cornelia Faustilla as the daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-09100484.text_edition: \n Luci libertus Cr et Publi filius \n
EDCS-09100484.translation: Freedman of Lucius, son of Cr(---) and Publius.
EDCS-09100484.summary: A funerary inscription identifying an individual as a freedman of Lucius and son of Cr(---) and Publius.

EDCS-09100485.text_edition: \n Lucius Egnatius Tertius Egnatia |(mulieris) liberta Primigenia annorum L\n
EDCS-09100485.translation: Lucius Egnatius Tertius and Egnatia Primigenia, freedwoman of Egnatius, aged 50.
EDCS-09100485.summary: A funerary inscription recording the names and age of Lucius Egnatius Tertius and Egnatia Primigenia, his freedwoman.

EDCS-09100488.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name Quintus Fabius and his age.

EDCS-09100489.text_edition: \n Quintus Fabius Quinti libertus Cappadox Quintus Fabius Anicetus Quintus Fabius Felix Quintus Fabius Faustillus\n
EDCS-09100489.translation: Quintus Fabius Cappadox, freedman of Quintus Fabius; Quintus Fabius Anicetus; Quintus Fabius Felix; Quintus Fabius Faustillus.
EDCS-09100489.summary: A funerary inscription listing several individuals, likely members of the Quintus Fabius family, including a freedman.

EDCS-09100494.text_edition: \n Quintus Fabius Sotericus hic situs est annorum XL Fabia Quinti liberta Anatole annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-09100494.translation: Quintus Fabius Sotericus rests here, aged 40. Fabia Anatole, freedwoman of Quintus Fabius, aged 23.
EDCS-09100494.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Quintus Fabius Sotericus and Fabia Anatole, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100496.text_edition: \n Fabia Quinti liberta Hirundo annorum XXX\n
EDCS-09100496.translation: Fabia Hirundo, freedwoman of Quintus Fabius, aged 30.
EDCS-09100496.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Fabia Hirundo, aged 30, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100497.text_edition: \n Vivi fecerunt Fabia Marci liberta Pindara Marcus Fabius Propinquus filius\n
EDCS-09100497.translation: Vivi made this. Fabia Pindara, freedwoman of Marcus Fabius; Marcus Fabius Propinquus, her son.
EDCS-09100497.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning the makers and the deceased, Fabia Pindara and her son Marcus Fabius Propinquus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100498.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deceased Fabia, daughter of Fabius Terentianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100502.text_edition: \n ius Felix filia Flavina uxsor sic\n
EDCS-09100502.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Felix, daughter of Flavina. His wife.
EDCS-09100502.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Felix, daughter of Flavina, and her unnamed wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100503.text_edition: \n Flavius Marci filius Superstes annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-09100503.translation: Flavius Superstes, son of Marcus, aged 35.
EDCS-09100503.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Flavius Superstes, son of Marcus, aged 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100504.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Flavius Thalamus, aged 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100505.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a funerary marker or a name on a monument, for Publius Fulcinius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100506.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deceased Paulla, daughter of Marcus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100507.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fulviae Mansuetae annorum XXXV Fulvius Cip uxori piissimae\n
EDCS-09100507.translation: To the departed spirits. Fulvius [erected this] to his most dutiful wife, Fulvia Mansueta, aged 35.
EDCS-09100507.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument by Fulvius to his wife Fulvia Mansueta, aged 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100508.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Gaius Soter.

EDCS-09100511.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Geminiae Myrines nnorum XXX Lucius Baebius Pardus omni bono de se meritae fecit\n
EDCS-09100511.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Baebius Pardus made this for Geminia Myrine, aged 30, for all the good she deserved from him.
EDCS-09100511.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Baebius Pardus dedicating a monument to his wife Geminia Myrine.

EDCS-09100512.translation: Quintus Granius Plutarcus, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-09100512.summary: This inscription records the name and freedman status of Quintus Granius Plutarcus.

EDCS-09100514.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Quintus Grattius Xanthus.

EDCS-09100515.text_edition: \n Vivus fecit Gaius Grattius Halys sibi et Grattiae Myrsini uxori rarissimae annorum XXXXVII sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09100515.translation: Gaius Grattius Halys made this in his lifetime for himself and for his wife Grattia Myrsine, most rare, aged 47, for himself and his family.
EDCS-09100515.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Grattius Halys dedicating a tomb for himself, his wife Grattia Myrsine, and their family.

EDCS-09100516.text_edition: \n Gaio Grattio Polyantho nnorum LV Geminia Phoebas viro et sibi\n
EDCS-09100516.translation: To Gaius Grattius Polyanthus, aged 55. Geminia Phoebas [made this] for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-09100516.summary: This funerary inscription records Geminia Phoebas dedicating a monument to her husband Gaius Grattius Polyanthus and herself.

EDCS-09100517.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Grattia Charite.

EDCS-09100518.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Gratus.

EDCS-09100521.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Iulia Nereis.

EDCS-09100523.translation: Cnaeus Liberius made this in his lifetime.
EDCS-09100523.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Cnaeus Liberius dedicating a tomb for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-09100529.translation: Publius Lucretius, son of Publius' freedman.
EDCS-09100529.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius Lucretius, son of a freedman, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100531.text_edition: \n Lucretia |(mulieris) liberta Prima hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100531.translation: Lucretia Prima, freedwoman of a woman, is buried here.
EDCS-09100531.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucretia Prima, freedwoman of a woman, from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100532.text_edition: \n Lucio Magidio Luci filio Gallo Magidia Sodalis fecit\n
EDCS-09100532.translation: Magidia Sodalis made this for Lucius Magidius Gallus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-09100532.summary: This funerary inscription records that Magidia Sodalis dedicated this monument to Lucius Magidius Gallus, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100533.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Marcius Cnaei libertus Optatus nnorum L Marcia Cnaei liberta Flora\n
EDCS-09100533.translation: Gnaeus Marcius Optatus, son of Gnaeus' freedman, [aged X] years. Marcia Flora, daughter of Gnaeus' freedwoman.
EDCS-09100533.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Marcius Optatus and Marcia Flora, possibly relatives or freedmen of a common master, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100534.text_edition: \n Caius Marius Amphio hic situs est Caius Marius --- Maria Cai liberta Corenis\n
EDCS-09100534.translation: Caius Marius Amphio is buried here. Caius Marius [---]. Maria Corenis, freedwoman of Caius.
EDCS-09100534.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Marius Amphio, with other individuals including Maria Corenis, his freedwoman, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100535.text_edition: \n Caius Marius Iaccus Maria Hygiaenusa annorum XVIII [] Maria Hygia Maria Primigenia sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09100535.translation: Caius Marius Iaccus, [aged X] years, and Maria Hygiaenusa, aged 18 years. Maria Hygia and Maria Primigenia for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-09100535.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Marius Iaccus and Maria Hygiaenusa, with Maria Hygia and Maria Primigenia providing for themselves and their families, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100536.text_edition: \n icius Meleager nnorum et icia Perpetua annorum sibi et suis et - Caecilio Acto de se bene merito\n
EDCS-09100536.translation: [---]icius Meleager, [aged X] years, and [---]icia Perpetua, [aged X] years, for themselves and their kin, and for Caecilius Actus, who deserved well of them.
EDCS-09100536.summary: This is a funerary inscription for [---]icius Meleager and [---]icia Perpetua, who also provide for Caecilius Actus, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100538.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name and age of a young individual named Philete, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100541.text_edition: \n E Popillius Agathemerus et Baebia EE \n
EDCS-09100541.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning E. Popillius Agathemerus and Baebia, possibly a dedicatory or funerary text from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100542.text_edition: \n Marcus Popillius Martialis vivus fecit Marco Popillio IIABS \n
EDCS-09100542.translation: Marcus Popillius Martialis made this while alive for Marcus Popillius [---]
EDCS-09100542.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcus Popillius Martialis, during his lifetime, made this for Marcus Popillius, likely a relative, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09100543.text_edition: \n Viva fecit Popillia Marcia sibi et Lucio Coelio filio Censo tabulario viro optimo annorum \n
EDCS-09100543.translation: Popillia Marcia, a living woman, made this for herself and for her excellent husband, Lucius Coelius Cens, her son, who lived [years].
EDCS-09100543.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Popillia Marcia for herself and her son, Lucius Coelius Cens. The age of the son is missing.

EDCS-09100545.text_edition: \n Practicini annorum XIIII fecit Philtate mater\n
EDCS-09100545.translation: Philtate, his mother, made this for Practicinus, who was 14 years old.
EDCS-09100545.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Philtate for her 14-year-old son, Practicinus.

EDCS-09100546.text_edition: \n o Probo annorum XXIIX ia Prote filio et sibi\n
EDCS-09100546.translation: For Probus, 28 years old, and for his son Prote, and for himself.
EDCS-09100546.summary: This is a funerary monument likely made by Probus for himself, his son Prote, and possibly another individual. Probus was 28 years old.

EDCS-09100548.text_edition: \n Proculus Cnaeus Baebius Cnaei libertus Philocomus\n
EDCS-09100548.translation: Proculus, freedman of Gnaeus Baebius, called Philocomus.
EDCS-09100548.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of Proculus, a freedman of Gnaeus Baebius, identified by his given name Philocomus.

EDCS-09100549.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Publicius Philodamus.

EDCS-09100551.translation: To Quietus, a most devoted companion.
EDCS-09100551.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Quietus, described as a most devoted companion.

EDCS-09100552.summary: This is an epitaph identifying Sebosus as the son of Publius, with the inscription potentially continuing.

EDCS-09100553.text_edition: \n Semproniae Luci filiae Nepotillae Calpurnia Quinti filia Fusca filiae optimae\n
EDCS-09100553.translation: Calpurnia Fusca, daughter of Quintus, to her excellent daughter Sempronia Nepotilla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-09100553.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Calpurnia Fusca for her beloved daughter Sempronia Nepotilla.

EDCS-09100554.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Turpa.

EDCS-09100555.translation: Sergia Prote, a living woman, made this.
EDCS-09100555.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sergia Prote for herself.

EDCS-09100558.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severi annorum XXXXIIX bene merenti fecit Prote\n
EDCS-09100558.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Prote made this for Severus, aged forty-eight, who deserved well.
EDCS-09100558.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Prote to Severus, who died at the age of 48.

EDCS-09100562.text_edition: \n s sibi et Statiae Threpteni libertae su\n
EDCS-09100562.translation: To himself and to Statia Threpten, his freedwoman.
EDCS-09100562.summary: This is a funerary epitaph intended for the dedicator and his freedwoman, Statia Threpten.

EDCS-09100563.translation: Trophimus for his most devoted daughter Para.
EDCS-09100563.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Trophimus to his daughter Para.

EDCS-09100566.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment mentioning Quintus Valerius.

EDCS-09100568.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Super annorum LIII hic situs est Fulvia Marci liberta Amoena nnorum XXV\n
EDCS-09100568.translation: Lucius Valerius Super, son of Lucius, aged fifty-three, is buried here. Fulvia Amoena, freedwoman of Marcus, aged twenty-five.
EDCS-09100568.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Super and Fulvia Amoena, recording their ages.

EDCS-09100571.text_edition: \n Valeria Marco Popilio Optato filio annorum XXII\n
EDCS-09100571.translation: Valeria for her son Marcus Popilius Optatus, aged twenty-two.
EDCS-09100571.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Valeria to her son Marcus Popilius Optatus, recording his age.

EDCS-09100572.text_edition: \n annorum XX Valeria E filiae piissimae\n
EDCS-09100572.translation: Valeria for her most devoted daughter, aged twenty.
EDCS-09100572.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Valeria to her daughter, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09100573.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment mentioning Valeria, daughter of Publius.

EDCS-09100574.text_edition: \n Vivus fecit Marcus Varvius Marci libertus Galaticus annorum \n
EDCS-09100574.translation: Marcus Varvius Galaticus, freedman of Marcus, aged [---], made this while alive.
EDCS-09100574.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Marcus Varvius Galaticus, a freedman, states he commissioned the monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-09100575.text_edition: \n Libertorum Marci Varvi monimentum sic fecerunt Marcus Varvius Valens Marcus Varvius Galaticus Marcus Varvius Chresimus Marcus Varvius Hermeros Marcus Varvius Syntrophus Aemilia S<y=I>ntyche \n
EDCS-09100575.translation: The freedmen of Marcus Varvius made this monument for Marcus Varvius: Marcus Varvius Valens, Marcus Varvius Galaticus, Marcus Varvius Chresimus, Marcus Varvius Hermeros, Marcus Varvius Syntrophus, Aemilia Syntyche.
EDCS-09100575.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the freedmen of Marcus Varvius, including Marcus Varvius Valens and Marcus Varvius Galaticus, along with Aemilia Syntyche.

EDCS-09100582.summary: This inscription likely records the dedication of a monument by a son to his parents, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100615.translation: Asclepias, daughter of Vitalius, [dedicated this] to the gods, being of sound mind.
EDCS-09100615.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Asclepias to the gods, likely on a religious monument, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100616.summary: This inscription records the age of a deceased individual named Fortunate, likely on a tombstone, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100619.summary: This inscription records the age of a deceased individual named Valerius Marcellinus, likely on a tombstone, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100621.text_edition: \n Titus Mamilius Severi filius Martialis Tritio Magallo II \n
EDCS-09100621.translation: Titus Mamilius Martiallis, son of Severus, [dedicated this to] Tritius Magallus, twice.
EDCS-09100621.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Titus Mamilius Martialis to Tritius Magallus, possibly for services rendered, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100622.text_edition: \n nnorum XXXVII dierum XXX Calpurnia Philaenis fil piissim\n
EDCS-09100622.translation: Calpurnia Philaenis, aged thirty-seven years and thirty days, [dedicated this] to her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-09100622.summary: This inscription records the age of Calpurnia Philaenis and is likely a dedication to her daughter, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100623.text_edition: \n Lucio Iulio Porcio annorum LX Calpurnia Syntyche B \n
EDCS-09100623.translation: Lucius Iulius Porcius, aged sixty; Calpurnia Syntyche [dedicated this].
EDCS-09100623.summary: This inscription records the age of Lucius Iulius Porcius and a dedication by Calpurnia Syntyche, likely on a tombstone, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100625.text_edition: \n pro salute Luci nostri Laribus Surus ostiarius votum solvit\n
EDCS-09100625.translation: For the safety of our Lucius, Surus the doorkeeper fulfilled his vow to the household gods.
EDCS-09100625.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Surus, a doorkeeper, to the household gods (Laribus) for the well-being of Lucius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100626.text_edition: \n Baebius Privatus et Baebia Oraxxes Baebius Primi<g=C>enius sibi vivi <f=T> ecerunt \n
EDCS-09100626.translation: Baebius Privatus and Baebia Oraxxes had this made for themselves while alive, [dedicated by] Baebius Primigenius.
EDCS-09100626.summary: This inscription records the self-funded construction of a monument by Baebius Privatus and Baebia Oraxxes, with a dedication by Baebius Primigenius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100628.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Niger hic situs est Rustica Baebia uxor\n
EDCS-09100628.translation: Gaius Aemilius Niger is buried here; his wife Rustica Baebia [erected this].
EDCS-09100628.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gaius Aemilius Niger and is likely a tombstone erected by his wife, Rustica Baebia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100629.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Thalamus, identifying him as a freedman, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-09100630.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuli Pompeiani annorum Pompeia \n
EDCS-09100630.translation: To the divine shades of Iulius Pompeianus, of Pompeia.
EDCS-09100630.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the shades of Iulius Pompeianus and Pompeia, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Corneliae Iustae nnorum VII mensium VII dierum VI Cornelius Restitutus et Annia Iustina filiae dulcissimae\n
EDCS-09100633.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Cornelia Iusta, aged 7 years, 7 months, and 6 days. Cornelius Restitutus and Annia Iustina to their most sweet daughter.
EDCS-09100633.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Cornelia Iusta, set up by her parents, Cornelius Restitutus and Annia Iustina, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100636.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lucius E P fecit\n
EDCS-09100636.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius E. P. made this.
EDCS-09100636.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius E. P., the abbreviation likely referring to his name and possibly his status or role, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100637.translation: Sacred to Liber. Lucius E. P.
EDCS-09100637.summary: This is a dedication to the god Liber by Lucius E. P., likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100639.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Heraclides vixit annos XXVIII menses X dies XVIIII Marcia Charis fratri piissimo\n
EDCS-09100639.translation: To the divine shades. Heraclides lived 28 years, 10 months, and 19 days. Marcia Charis to her most dutiful brother.
EDCS-09100639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heraclides, detailing his age and set up by his sister Marcia Charis, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100640.text_edition: \n Marcus Rubrius Marci libertus Firmanus Baebia Cnaei liberta Quieta hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09100640.translation: Marcus Rubrius Firmanus, freedman of Marcus, and Baebia Quieta, freedwoman of Cnaeus. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-09100640.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription for Marcus Rubrius Firmanus and Baebia Quieta, who stipulated that the monument would not be inherited, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100658.text_edition: \n Geminius Quinti filius Galeria Iuvenis annorum LV Baebia Publi filia Maxsuma sic annorum XXI\n
EDCS-09100658.translation: Geminius Iuvenis, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 55. Baebia Maxsuma, daughter of Publius, aged 21.
EDCS-09100658.summary: This inscription records the ages of Geminius Iuvenis and Baebia Maxsuma, likely a married couple or individuals commemorated together, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100659.text_edition: \n Ac annorum XXI Sulpicia Manliana filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100659.translation: Aged 21. Sulpicia Manliana to her most dutiful son.
EDCS-09100659.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a son, aged 21, set up by his mother Sulpicia Manliana, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100661.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Acilius Urbicus annorum Maxsimus sic et Martialis fili patri incomparabili\n
EDCS-09100661.translation: To the divine shades. Acilius Urbicus, aged [Maximus?] and Martialis, to their incomparable father.
EDCS-09100661.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Acilius Urbicus and Martialis, set up by their children (or perhaps wife) to their 'incomparable father', likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100666.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Cai filio Anniano Caius Iulius Cai filius Sabinus fecit\n
EDCS-09100666.translation: Gaius Julius Sabinus, son of Gaius, made this for Gaius Julius Annianus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-09100666.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Julius Sabinus to Gaius Julius Annianus.

EDCS-09100667.text_edition: \n Valeriae Luci filiae Firmanillae nnorum XXXX Quintus Caecilius Porcianus uxori optimae\n
EDCS-09100667.translation: To Firmanilla, daughter of Lucius, aged forty years, Quintus Caecilius Porcianus (erected this) for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-09100667.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Quintus Caecilius Porcianus to his wife, Firmanilla.

EDCS-09100668.text_edition: \n Caecilia Quinti liberta Artemis nnorum XX Caecilia Restituta annorum L\n
EDCS-09100668.translation: Artemis, freedwoman of Caecilius, aged twenty years; Caecilia Restituta, aged fifty years.
EDCS-09100668.summary: This inscription records the ages of Artemis, freedwoman of Caecilius, and Caecilia Restituta, likely for funerary purposes.

EDCS-09100669.text_edition: \n Festo annorum XXII Iustinus fratri piissimo\n
EDCS-09100669.translation: To Festus, aged twenty-two years, Iustinus (erected this) for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-09100669.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Iustinus to his brother, Festus.

EDCS-09100670.text_edition: \n Valeriae Luci filiae Priscae annorum LX Valeria Saguntina patronae et suis fecit\n
EDCS-09100670.translation: Valeria Saguntina made this for her patroness Prisca, daughter of Lucius, aged sixty years, and for her own (family).
EDCS-09100670.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Valeria Saguntina to her patroness Prisca and her own relatives.

EDCS-09100674.text_edition: \n Pro salute et reditu fili Quintus Fabius Fabullus Tutelae votum solvit\n
EDCS-09100674.translation: For the health and return of his son, Quintus Fabius Fabullus fulfilled his vow to Tutela.
EDCS-09100674.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Fabius Fabullus to the deity Tutela for the well-being and safe return of his son.

EDCS-09100675.text_edition: \n Baebiae Luci filiae Marcellae sorori annorum XII hic sita est Baebio Luci filio Galeria C EST O annorum XXX hic situs est Quintillannorum XV VSOE\n
EDCS-09100675.translation: To Marcella, daughter of Lucius, sister, aged twelve years, (she) is laid here; to Baebius, son of Lucius, aged thirty years, (he) is laid here; Quintillus, aged fifteen years.
EDCS-09100675.summary: This inscription records the burial of Marcella, her brother Baebius, and Quintillus, likely family members.

EDCS-09100676.text_edition: \n Fabiae Ursae annorum et Baebio Pyramo annorum Lucius Baebius Artemas uxori et liberto bene merentibus\n
EDCS-09100676.translation: Lucius Baebius Artemas (erected this) for his wife Ursa, aged [...], and for his freedman Pyramus, aged [...], who deserved well.
EDCS-09100676.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Baebius Artemas to his wife Ursa and his freedman Pyramus.

EDCS-09100678.translation: Baebia Fulvia, daughter of Lucius, (this is for) her brother.
EDCS-09100678.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Baebia Fulvia to her brother.

EDCS-09100679.text_edition: \n Grattio Lucifero annorum L Antestia Syra marito optimo et sibi ]\n
EDCS-09100679.translation: Antestia Syra (erected this) for her husband Luciferous, aged fifty years, and for herself.
EDCS-09100679.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Antestia Syra to her husband Luciferous and for herself.

EDCS-09100680.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Trophimus, likely to a female relative, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100682.text_edition: \n annorum IIII mensium Marcus Fabius Maxim- --- filio \n
EDCS-09100682.translation: Marcus Fabius Maximus, four years and [some] months old, to his son.
EDCS-09100682.summary: This inscription records the death of a young child, Marcus Fabius Maximus, and is likely a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100683.text_edition: \n Heracla annorum LX Eutychus filius patri piissimo C vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09100683.translation: Heracla, sixty years old. Eutychus made this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-09100683.summary: This epitaph records Heracla, aged sixty, and states that Eutychus erected it for his devoted father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100685.text_edition: \n Cornelio Marci filio Maximo Cornelia Maxima Marcus Cornelius Maurus Marcus Cornelius Longinus Lucius Cornelius Maximus Lucius Cornelius Modestus Lucius Cornelius Dento Lucius Cornelius Rufus Lucius Cornelius Maternus Lucius Cornelius Silo Cornelia Luci filia Lucia gentiles\n
EDCS-09100685.translation: Cornelius Maximus, son of Marcus; Marcus Cornelius Maurus; Marcus Cornelius Longinus; Lucius Cornelius Maximus; Lucius Cornelius Modestus; Lucius Cornelius Dento; Lucius Cornelius Rufus; Lucius Cornelius Maternus; Lucius Cornelius Silo; Cornelia Lucia, daughter of Lucius; and their relatives.
EDCS-09100685.summary: This is a lengthy funerary inscription recording multiple family members of the gens Cornelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100687.summary: This is a dedication to Publicia Paeza, possibly a funerary inscription, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100688.text_edition: \n Gaius Aemilius Gai filius Galeria Nepos aedilis IIvir\n
EDCS-09100688.translation: Gaius Aemilius Nepos, son of Gaius, aedile and former duovir.
EDCS-09100688.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Aemilius Nepos, who held the offices of aedile and duovir, likely in a Roman colonial city, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100689.text_edition: \n Heras Didae filius Graecus Didia Luci liberta Murtis Lucius Didius Fuscus annorum XXVIII hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09100689.translation: Heras, son of Didas, a Greek; Didia Murtis, freedwoman of Lucius; Lucius Didius Fuscus, twenty-eight years old; they are buried here.
EDCS-09100689.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Heras, Didia Murtis, and Lucius Didius Fuscus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100690.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebio Seneciano annorum XVIII Sergius Ursio et Baebia Marcia filio pientissimo et sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-09100690.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sergius Ursio and Baebia Marcia made this for their eighteen-year-old son Baebius Senecianus, most dutiful, and for themselves.
EDCS-09100690.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Sergius Ursio and Baebia Marcia for their son Baebius Senecianus and themselves, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100694.text_edition: \n Viva fecit Aemilia Nigra sibi et suis et Valerio Civi viro annorum LXX Valeriae Nusae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur F \n
EDCS-09100694.translation: Viva made this monument for herself, her family, and for her husband Valerius Civi, seventy years old, and for Valeria Nusa; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-09100694.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Viva for herself, her family, and her husband Valerius Civi and Valeria Nusa, specifying that the property will not be inherited, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100695.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Manlius Cnaei filius Crescens hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09100695.translation: Cnaeus Manlius Crescens, son of Cnaeus, will not pass this monument to an heir.
EDCS-09100695.summary: This funerary inscription records Cnaeus Manlius Crescens and states that the monument will not be inherited, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09100697.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Fabius Felix et Fabius Fabianus IIviri ex decreto decurionum pecunia publica faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09100697.translation: Fabius Felix and Fabius Fabianus, IIviri, had this made at public expense by decree of the town councillors, dedicated to Father Liber.
EDCS-09100697.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument dedicated to Father Liber, funded by the state and commissioned by two officials, Fabius Felix and Fabius Fabianus, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-09100698.text_edition: \n Baebia Cnaei liberta Graeca votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100698.translation: Graeca, freedwoman of Baebius, gladly fulfilled her vow in a deserving cause.
EDCS-09100698.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Graeca, a freedwoman, to a deity, likely Father Liber given the context of the surrounding inscriptions.

EDCS-09100699.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Superstes Libero Patri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100699.translation: Marcus Cornelius Superstes gladly fulfilled his vow to Father Liber in a deserving cause.
EDCS-09100699.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Marcus Cornelius Superstes to Father Liber, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100702.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of an individual, M. Iucundus, possibly as a dedicant or a benefactor.

EDCS-09100703.text_edition: \n Caius Lig Crassus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100703.translation: Caius Lig Crassus gladly fulfilled his vow in a deserving cause.
EDCS-09100703.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Caius Lig Crassus, likely to Father Liber, during the Roman period.

EDCS-09100704.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Mal Florus Libero votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-09100704.translation: Cnaeus Mal Florus gladly fulfilled his vow to Liber.
EDCS-09100704.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Cnaeus Mal Florus to Liber, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100705.text_edition: \n Terentius Axsiocus sic Libero votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09100705.translation: Terentius Axsiocus gladly fulfilled his vow to Liber in a deserving cause.
EDCS-09100705.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Terentius Axsiocus to Liber, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09100707.summary: This is a very brief inscription that simply records the name of an individual, Valerius Severus, potentially as a dedicant or for identification.

EDCS-09100708.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Cn. L., likely the beginning of a Roman personal name, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-09100710.summary: This is a very short inscription indicating a vow made to the deity Liber.

EDCS-09100728.text_edition: \n Attius Endymion sua pecunia fecit et dedicavit\n
EDCS-09100728.translation: Attius Endymion made and dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-09100728.summary: This is a dedication made by Attius Endymion for a structure built at his own cost.

EDCS-09100732.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Geminio filio mo annorum omnibus honoribus in re publica sua functo --- Geminia Barbara patrono optimo et Quinto Lucretio Lucretiano annorum XIIX filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100732.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Geminius, his son, who completed all public honors in his republic, and for Quintus Lucretius Lucretianus, his son, 18 years old, most devoted.
EDCS-09100732.summary: This funerary inscription honors a son, Geminius, who had held public office, and another son, Quintus Lucretius Lucretianus, dedicated by Geminia Barbara.

EDCS-09100733.text_edition: \n Valerio Luci filio Galeria Optato annorum XXXV aedili flamini IIviro Saliorum magistro Varvia Sa filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100733.translation: To Valerius Optatus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, 35 years old, former aedile, flamen, and duovir, and to Salvius Magistrus, his most devoted son.
EDCS-09100733.summary: This funerary inscription honors Valerius Optatus, who held several local magistracies, and his son Salvius Magistrus, who is described as devoted.

EDCS-09100735.text_edition: \n Antonianae annorum XXX mensium II Gaius Valerius Lupus uxori incomparabili\n
EDCS-09100735.translation: To Antoniana, 30 years and 2 months old, Gaius Valerius Lupus to his incomparable wife.
EDCS-09100735.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Gaius Valerius Lupus to his wife Antoniana, who died at the age of 30 years and 2 months.

EDCS-09100736.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Baebiae Crispinae Quintus Baebius Baebianus filiae piissimae nnrum XVII posuit\n
EDCS-09100736.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Baebius Baebianus erected this for his most devoted daughter Baebia Crispinna, 17 years old.
EDCS-09100736.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Quintus Baebius Baebianus for his devoted daughter, Baebia Crispinna, aged 17.

EDCS-09100737.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Bellies Gai filiae Staphyleni nnorum XXX\n
EDCS-09100737.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Bellies, daughter of Gaius Staphylenus, 30 years old.
EDCS-09100737.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bellies, daughter of Gaius Staphylenus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-09100738.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitiani anno uno menses VI Domitius Onesimus et Domitia Leontis filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100738.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Domitianus, one year and six months old. Domitius Onesimus and Domitia Leontis to their most devoted son.
EDCS-09100738.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Domitianus at the age of one year and six months, dedicated by his parents Domitius Onesimus and Domitia Leontis.

EDCS-09100739.text_edition: \n Caius Licinius Cai libertus dus annorum LXX sibi et Calpurniae Spataleni uxori annorum LX vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09100739.translation: Caius Licinius, freedman of Caius, 70 years old, made this for himself and for Calpurnia Spatalenus, his wife, 60 years old, during his lifetime.
EDCS-09100739.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Caius Licinius for himself and his wife Calpurnia Spatalenus, specifying their ages and that it was made during their lifetime.

EDCS-09100740.translation: To Materno, from Perpetua, most devoted.
EDCS-09100740.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Perpetua to Materno, described as most devoted.

EDCS-09100742.text_edition: \n ae Cai filiae Paulinae nnorum LV Cornelius Myrismus uxori\n
EDCS-09100742.translation: To Paulina, daughter of Caius, 55 years old, Cornelius Myrismus to his wife.
EDCS-09100742.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Paulina, wife of Cornelius Myrismus, at the age of 55.

EDCS-09100745.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Valeri Maximi annorum XV dierum XLVII Gaius Valerius Ianuarius filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100745.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Valerius Maximus, aged 15 years and 47 days. Gaius Valerius Ianuarius made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-09100745.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Gaius Valerius Ianuarius to his son Gaius Valerius Maximus, who died at the age of 15 years and 47 days.

EDCS-09100746.text_edition: \n liae Si nnorum XXV cilius Philarmonicus uxori karissimae fecit\n
EDCS-09100746.translation: For Iulia, aged 25 years. Philarmonicus dedicated this to his dearest wife.
EDCS-09100746.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Philarmonicus to his wife Iulia, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-09100748.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely recording the name and filiation of a person.

EDCS-09100749.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aged 30 years.
EDCS-09100749.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased.

EDCS-09100752.translation: Marcus Aemilius Latro, son of Marcus.
EDCS-09100752.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Marcus Aemilius Latro.

EDCS-09100753.text_edition: \n Flacco filio annorum posuit Aemilius Phronimus\n
EDCS-09100753.translation: Aemilius Phronimus set this up for his son Flaccus, aged... years.
EDCS-09100753.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Aemilius Phronimus for his son Flaccus.

EDCS-09100754.text_edition: \n Myron anuclus sic mesum sic V memoriae posiut sic pater \n
EDCS-09100754.translation: Myron, old man, set this up in memory of himself, his father.
EDCS-09100754.summary: An inscription by Myron, an old man, commemorating himself and his father.

EDCS-09100755.text_edition: \n Publius Popilius Tancini filius Publius Popilius Paullus\n
EDCS-09100755.translation: Publius Popilius Paullus, son of Publius Popilius Tancinus.
EDCS-09100755.summary: This inscription records the names of Publius Popilius Paullus and his father Publius Popilius Tancinus.

EDCS-09100756.translation: To Popillius the Younger. Fulvia, his wife.
EDCS-09100756.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Fulvia to her husband Popillius the Younger.

EDCS-09100757.text_edition: \n us Rusticus --- annorum LVI us Gai filius annorum XXXXI le annorum XXX\n
EDCS-09100757.translation: Rusticus, son of Gaius, aged 56 years; [ Gaius? ], aged 41 years; [ another person? ], aged 30 years.
EDCS-09100757.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription listing the names and ages of three individuals, possibly a family.

EDCS-09100758.text_edition: \n Varvia Varvi filia Rustica annorum XXIIII\n

EDCS-09100759.text_edition: \n Appalia Pietas votum solvit libens merito\n

EDCS-09100763.text_edition: \n Coelia Rhodine votum solvit libens animo\n

EDCS-09100771.text_edition: \n Lucius Calpurnius Luci filius Lucillianus annorum LXXXXV Calpurnia Quinti filia Pudentilla annorum XXXXVII\n

EDCS-09100772.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fulviae Filenidi annorum XXIII Zoticus maritus uxori karissimae fidelissimae\n

EDCS-09100775.text_edition: \n Marcus Tettienus Marci filius Galeria Pollio aedilis IIvir flamen augur quaestor Baebia Luci filia Lepida uxor\n

EDCS-09100778.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Siciliae annorum XXXX Lupinula matri et Onesimeni sorori piissimis annorum XVIII\n

EDCS-09100779.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Antonius Leo Pardae uxori\n
EDCS-09100779.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Antonius Leo for his wife Pardaea.
EDCS-09100779.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gaius Antonius Leo for his wife Pardaea, likely marking her tomb.

EDCS-09100780.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Cornelius Saturninus Baebia Agile |(mulieris) liberta suis filiis fecit mater\n
EDCS-09100780.translation: Cnaeus Cornelius Saturninus made this for his sons; Baebia Agilis, his freedwoman, [made this] as a mother.
EDCS-09100780.summary: This epitaph records a monument made by Cnaeus Cornelius Saturninus and his freedwoman Baebia Agilis for their sons.

EDCS-09100781.text_edition: \n Fulviae Te nnorum Fulvius Verus uxori carissimae et fidelissimae f(ecit)\n
EDCS-09100781.translation: Fulvius Verus made this for his wife Fulvia Tenna, most beloved and most faithful.
EDCS-09100781.summary: This funerary inscription records Fulvius Verus dedicating a monument to his wife Fulvia Tenna.

EDCS-09100782.text_edition: \n Marco Sergio Materno annorum XXX Marcus Sergius Numida liberto optimo et sibi\n
EDCS-09100782.translation: Marcus Sergius Numida made this for his freedman Marcus Sergio Maternus, aged 30, and for himself.
EDCS-09100782.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Sergius Numida dedicating a monument to his excellent freedman Marcus Sergio Maternus and himself.

EDCS-09100784.text_edition: \n Lucius Marcius filius Galeria Marcellus annorum X Fabia Luci filia uxor annorum Lucius Marcius Marcellus? ---\n
EDCS-09100784.translation: Lucius Marcius Marcellus, son of Lucius Marcius, aged 10; Fabia Lucia, daughter of Lucius, wife, aged [---].
EDCS-09100784.summary: This damaged inscription appears to record the ages and familial relationships of Lucius Marcius Marcellus and his wife Fabia Lucia.

EDCS-09100785.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Fronto annorum LX Coelia filia Aestiva annorum LX Antistia Publi filia Festiva Fabi Aviti nummulari uxor annorum XXV hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09100785.translation: Gaius Aemilius Fronto, aged 60; Coelia Aestiva, daughter of Aemilius, aged 60; Antistia Festiva, daughter of Publius, wife of Fabius Avitus the money-changer, aged 25. This monument will not pass to heirs.
EDCS-09100785.summary: This epitaph details the ages and relationships of several individuals, including Gaius Aemilius Fronto and the wife of a money-changer, and states the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-09100786.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that likely records a name and possibly titles or relationships.

EDCS-09100789.text_edition: \n Lucio? Pomponio Luci filio Marcello --- Marcia Marcella filio piissimo --- AT \n
EDCS-09100789.translation: To Lucius? Pomponius Marcellus, son of Lucius, [---]; Marcia Marcella for her most dutiful son, [---].
EDCS-09100789.summary: This fragmentary epitaph seems to dedicate a monument to Lucius Pomponius Marcellus and his mother Marcia Marcella.

EDCS-09100790.text_edition: \n - Pomponius annorum LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100790.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Pomponius, aged 70.

EDCS-09100795.text_edition: \n nius Gai filius tianus aedilis IIvir annorum LXX Rufus annorum XX Terentia Luci filiaa annorum XX\n
EDCS-09100795.translation: [---]nius, son of Gaius, [---]tianus, aedile, IIvir, aged 70; Rufus, aged 20; Terentia, daughter of Lucius, aged 20.
EDCS-09100795.summary: This damaged inscription lists individuals with their ages and possible offices, including an aedile and a IIvir, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-09100796.text_edition: \n Aemilia Marci filia Aemiliana annorum LX Antonius Rufinus annorum L\n
EDCS-09100796.translation: Aemilia Aemiliana, daughter of Marcus, aged 60; Antonius Rufinus, aged 50.
EDCS-09100796.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of two individuals, Aemilia Aemiliana and Antonius Rufinus.

EDCS-09100797.translation: To the departed spirits; Gaius Cornelius Me...
EDCS-09100797.summary: This is a funerary inscription, a dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits), with the beginning of a name.

EDCS-09100799.text_edition: \n Quintus Munnius Quinti filius Graecus Valeria Gai filia Rosula Quintus Munnius Quinti filius Graecinus annorum XXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100799.translation: Quintus Munnius Graecus, son of Quintus; Valeria Rosula, daughter of Gaius; Quintus Munnius Graecinus, son of Quintus, aged 21, is buried here.
EDCS-09100799.summary: This funerary inscription records three individuals, likely family members, with their names, parentage, and the age of one of them.

EDCS-09100800.text_edition: \n Marcus Sulpicius Lucanus Villano nepoti anni I hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100800.translation: Marcus Sulpicius Lucanus to his nephew Villano, aged 1, is buried here.
EDCS-09100800.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Sulpicius Lucanus dedicating a monument to his nephew Villano, who died at the age of one.

EDCS-09100801.text_edition: \n Fabio Calisto annorum LXXI et Quinto Fabio Lupo annorum XXXIIII patri et fratri piissimis fecit Fabia Calityche\n
EDCS-09100801.translation: Fabia Calityche made this for Fabio Calisto, aged 71, and Quintus Fabio Lupo, aged 34, her most devoted father and brother.
EDCS-09100801.summary: This funerary inscription records Fabia Calityche dedicating a monument to her father, Fabio Calisto, and brother, Quintus Fabio Lupo.

EDCS-09100802.text_edition: \n Quintula cum Fortunali sit semel et numquam\n
EDCS-09100802.translation: Quintula, may she be with Fortunatus once and never.
EDCS-09100802.summary: This is a short epitaph for Quintula, expressing a wish for her union with Fortunatus.

EDCS-09100805.text_edition: \n Quintus Geminius Quinti filius Niger annorum LV Calpurnia Severa Tannegaldunis filia annorum XXCII hic sita est\n
EDCS-09100805.translation: Quintus Geminius Niger, son of Quintus, aged 55; Calpurnia Severa, daughter of Tannegaldus, aged 22, is buried here.
EDCS-09100805.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Quintus Geminius Niger and Calpurnia Severa, with their parentage and ages.

EDCS-09100807.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Privata as a slave of Calpurnia Vitalis.

EDCS-09100809.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Lucano annorum XXX VIII Valeria Marcella mater filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-09100809.translation: Valeria Marcella made this for her most devoted son, Lucius Valerius Lucanus, aged 38.
EDCS-09100809.summary: This funerary inscription records Valeria Marcella dedicating a monument to her son, Lucius Valerius Lucanus.

EDCS-09100811.text_edition: \n Caio Aemilio Cai filio Galeria Restituto nnorum XX Caecilia Cai filia Procula filio piissimo se viva fecit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-09100811.translation: Caecilia Procula, daughter of Caius, made this monument while alive for her most devoted son, Caius Aemilius Restitutus, son of Galeria, aged 20; he will not follow this monument as heir.
EDCS-09100811.summary: This funerary inscription records Caecilia Procula dedicating a monument to her son, Caius Aemilius Restitutus, with a stipulation that he will not inherit it.

EDCS-09100812.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Calpurnio Lascivo Licinia Liciniana \n
EDCS-09100812.translation: To the Divine Shades. Licinia Liciniana dedicated this to Lucius Calpurnius Lascivus.
EDCS-09100812.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Calpurnius Lascivus by Licinia Liciniana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100817.text_edition: \n Iovi Conservatori ob incolumitatem Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Pii Felicis res publica Leserensis aram posuit et dedicavit\n
EDCS-09100817.translation: The republic of Leserensis erected and dedicated this altar to Jupiter the Protector for the safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix.
EDCS-09100817.summary: A state dedication to Jupiter Conservator for the well-being of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100818.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Baebio Cnaei filio Galeria Gemino et Papiriae Cai filiae Festae Cnaeus Baebius Taurus parentibus optimis\n
EDCS-09100818.translation: Cnaeus Baebius Taurus to his most excellent parents, Gnaeus Baebius Geminus, son of Gnaeus, of the Galeria tribe, and Papiria Festa, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-09100818.summary: A funerary dedication by Cnaeus Baebius Taurus to his parents, Gnaeus Baebius Geminus and Papiria Festa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100819.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Galeria Frontoni annorum XIIX C \n
EDCS-09100819.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Fronton, who died at the age of 18, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100821.text_edition: \n us Publi filius panus a Gai filia uxor \n
EDCS-09100821.translation: [This person was the] son of Publius, and his wife [was] Pana, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-09100821.summary: A fragment of an inscription identifying a man as the son of Publius and mentioning his wife, Pana, daughter of Gaius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100822.text_edition: \n Gaius Domitius Gai filius Galeria Proculus annorum XX hic situs est Gaius Domitius Galeria Proculus Aelia Luci filia Supersta Lucius Aelius Asiaticus Valeria Nysa vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-09100822.translation: Gaius Domitius Proculus, son of Gaius, aged 20, lies here. Gaius Domitius Proculus and Aelia Supersta, daughter of Lucius, and Lucius Aelius Asiaticus and Valeria Nysa made this while alive.
EDCS-09100822.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Domitius Proculus, aged 20, with the monument constructed by his family members, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100823.text_edition: \n Caio Caecilio Paterno annorum XXX Iulia Paterna filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09100823.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Paternus, aged 30. Iulia Paterna to her most dutiful son.
EDCS-09100823.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Caecilius Paternus, aged 30, dedicated by his mother Iulia Paterna, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100825.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Domitius Proculus annorum XVII hic situs est Marcus Domitius Seranus pater filio piissimo fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-09100825.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Shades. Marcus Domitius Proculus, aged 17, lies here. Marcus Domitius Seranus, his father, made this for his most dutiful son and for himself.
EDCS-09100825.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Domitius Proculus, aged 17, with a self-purchase clause for his father, Marcus Domitius Seranus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100826.text_edition: \n Have Procle Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Domitius Proculus annorum XVII hic situs est Marcus Domitius Seranus pater filio piissimo fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-09100826.translation: Hail, Proclus. Dedicated to the Divine Shades. Marcus Domitius Proculus, aged 17, lies here. Marcus Domitius Seranus, his father, made this for his most dutiful son and for himself.
EDCS-09100826.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Domitius Proculus, aged 17, with a self-purchase clause for his father, Marcus Domitius Seranus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100828.text_edition: \n Ccastori sic et Polluci Marcus Valerius Antus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-09100828.translation: To Castor and also to Pollux. Marcus Valerius Antus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09100828.summary: A votive offering by Marcus Valerius Antus to the divine twins Castor and Pollux, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09100830.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Publius Cornelius Frontinus sevir Augustalis pecunia sua posuit\n
EDCS-09100830.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Publius Cornelius Frontinus, Augustalis priest, erected at his own expense.
EDCS-09100830.summary: An Augustalis priest, Publius Cornelius Frontinus, dedicates this shrine to Mercury Augustus at his own expense, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100832.text_edition: \n Tutelae Augustae sacrum Caius Terentius Onesimus ob honorem seviratus sui et in honorem Cai Terenti Ursi fili\n
EDCS-09100832.translation: Sacred to Tutela Augusta, Caius Terentius Onesimus, for the honor of his office as Augustalis priest, and in honor of his son, Caius Terentius Ursus.
EDCS-09100832.summary: Caius Terentius Onesimus dedicates this shrine to Tutela Augusta, honoring both his own office as an Augustalis priest and his son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100833.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio tribunicia potestate consuli designato II res publica Dertosanorum curante legato Marco Baebio Crasso functo legatione gratuita\n
EDCS-09100833.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, holding tribunician power, consul-designate for the second time, by the public community of the Dertosans, with Marcus Baebius Crassus, legate, having discharged his duty without payment.
EDCS-09100833.summary: The city of Dertosanorum dedicates this monument to Emperor Antoninus Pius, recognizing his titles and holding power, with a legate overseeing the project, dating to his consulship-designate, likely mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09100835.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio nobilissimo Caesari filio domini nostri sanctissimi Traiani Deci Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti ordo decurionum c Dertosanae dicatissimus numini eius\n
EDCS-09100835.translation: The council of decurions of Dertosanorum, most devoted to his divine will, dedicates this to Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, son of our most sacred lord Trajanus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-09100835.summary: The city council of Dertosanorum dedicates this monument to Caesar Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, son of Emperor Trajan Decius, showing their devotion, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100838.text_edition: \n Caio Cassio Cai filio Galeria Nigro Caius Cassius Avitus patri omnibus honoribus functo de sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-09100838.translation: Caius Cassius Avitus erected this to his father, Caius Cassius Niger, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, who had held all offices, from his own money.
EDCS-09100838.summary: Caius Cassius Avitus erected this monument at his own expense in honor of his father, Caius Cassius Niger, who had served in public office, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100839.text_edition: \n Lucio Munnio Luci filio Galeria Placido II viro flamini Romae et Augusti Lucius Munnius Placidus ex testamento patris\n
EDCS-09100839.translation: Lucius Munnius Placidus, from his father's will, erected this to Lucius Munnius Placidus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, former duovir and priest of Rome and Augustus.
EDCS-09100839.summary: Lucius Munnius Placidus erected this monument from his father's will to honor Lucius Munnius Placidus, who had served as duovir and priest, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100840.text_edition: \n Lucio Munnio Luci filio Galeria Placido II viro flamini Romae et Augusti ex testamento Porciae Placidae\n
EDCS-09100840.translation: From the will of Porcia Placidus, erected to Lucius Munnius Placidus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, former duovir and priest of Rome and Augustus.
EDCS-09100840.summary: This monument was erected from the will of Porcia Placidus in honor of Lucius Munnius Placidus, who had served as duovir and priest, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100843.text_edition: \n Publio Valerio Dionysio VI viro Augustali cui ordo Dertosanorum ob merita eius aedilicios honores decrevit Publius Valerius Pardus libertus VI vir Augustalis patrono optimo\n
EDCS-09100843.translation: To Publius Valerius Dionysius, VI vir Augustalis, to whom the council of Dertosanorum decreed the aedilician honors for his services; erected by Publius Valerius Pardus, freedman, VI vir Augustalis, to his most excellent patron.
EDCS-09100843.summary: Publius Valerius Pardus, a freedman and Augustalis, erected this monument to his patron Publius Valerius Dionysius, honoring his achievements and aedilician office, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100844.text_edition: \n Pomponiae Gai filiae S PRO Lucius Munnius \n
EDCS-09100844.translation: To Pomponia, daughter of Gaius, daughter of S. PRO; Lucius Munnius.
EDCS-09100844.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to Pomponia, daughter of Gaius, with Lucius Munnius mentioned, the purpose and date are unclear.

EDCS-09100845.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Numisius Liberalis miles classis praetoriae nnatis III Marte stipendiorum XIII natione Cursicanus Marcus Didius Polio heres et commanipularis et Gellia Excitata \n
EDCS-09100845.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Numisius Liberalis, soldier of the praetorian fleet, completed 3 years of service, of Cursican nationality. Marcus Didius Polio, his comrade and heir, and Gellia Excitata [erected this].
EDCS-09100845.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Numisius Liberalis, a soldier in the praetorian fleet with 13 years of service and Cursican origin, honored by his comrade and heir, Marcus Didius Polio, and Gellia Excitata, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100846.text_edition: \n Marco Sallustio Felici peregre defuncto sodales Herculanei\n
EDCS-09100846.translation: To Marcus Sallustius Felix, who died abroad, the associates of Herculaneum [erected this].
EDCS-09100846.summary: A funerary monument erected by fellow associates for Marcus Sallustius Felix, who died while away from home.

EDCS-09100847.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Auli Caecili Cubiculari peregre defuncti Porcia Euphrosyne marito optimo posuit\n
EDCS-09100847.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Porcia Euphrosyne placed this for her most excellent husband, Aulus Caecilius Cubicularius, who died abroad.
EDCS-09100847.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Porcia Euphrosyne for her deceased husband, Aulus Caecilius Cubicularius, who died abroad.

EDCS-09100848.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Flaviano patrono et marito pientissimo et Quinto Iulio Gaetulico patri Flaviani Iulia Marcia impensa sua fecit\n
EDCS-09100848.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Marcia, at her own expense, made this for Quintus Iulius Flavianus, most dutiful patron and husband, and for Quintus Iulius Gaetulicus, Flavianus' father.
EDCS-09100848.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia Marcia for her husband Quintus Iulius Flavianus and his father Quintus Iulius Gaetulicus.

EDCS-09100849.text_edition: \n Lucio Sempronio Luci filio Quaesito Lucius Sempronius Hiberus et Caecilia Quinti filia Quieta \n
EDCS-09100849.translation: To Lucius Sempronius Quaestus, son of Lucius. Lucius Sempronius Hiberus and Caecilia Quieta, daughter of Quintus, [erected this].
EDCS-09100849.summary: A monument set up by Lucius Sempronius Hiberus and Caecilia Quieta for Lucius Sempronius Quaestus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-09100851.text_edition: \n io Marci filio Serano annorum XIIII us Paludinus meria Cai filia Serena mater\n
EDCS-09100851.translation: To Marcius Seranus, son of Marcius, 14 years old. His mother, Paludinus Maria, daughter of Caius, [set this up].
EDCS-09100851.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcius Seranus, aged 14, set up by his mother.

EDCS-09100856.text_edition: \n Caius Porcius Seranus Edebensis nnorum XXXVI hic situs est\n
EDCS-09100856.translation: Gaius Porcius Seranus of Edeba, 36 years old, lies here.
EDCS-09100856.summary: A tombstone marking the burial place of Gaius Porcius Seranus of Edeba, who died at the age of 36.

EDCS-09100857.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Lo Syntucheti et Furio Menomacho Lucio Veranio Furia Nectaris Cn mater IEO--YS MSDMA-- ET\n
EDCS-09100857.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [Erected by] Lucius Veranius [and] Furia Nectaris, mother of Quintus Lo Syntucheti and Furio Menomachus... [text is damaged].
EDCS-09100857.summary: A damaged funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, mentioning several individuals including Quintus Lo Syntucheti and Furio Menomachus.

EDCS-09100861.text_edition: \n Caius Aebutius Tardus Caius Aebutius Verecundus filius miles legionis VI in Suria Iamque XVIII aetas mea nunc conpleverat annos ruperunt misero fata repente viam lectus miles eras legio quae sexta tenebat qui pius in Suria fortiter arma tulit et fortuna nimis saevas exercuit eiras sic quam sic ut torrens ante diem rapuit nemo ne votisque meis adeo usque parentes militiae referat splendida facta suae o miseros quei sic me talem genuere parentes non licuit patrios continuisse sinus te miseram mater quae sic deserta quereris nam patris interitu lapsa ruina iacet nec te praetereor sic cor et solacia nostri indignor miseras non licuisse frui dulces anplexus morientis et oscula data nec tenuit moriens deficiente manu in supero it si qua viast aditusque sepulcro instituto casus pareat usque meus inritus infelix peregrinas possidet auras neu quisquam pressit lumina nemo manus hospes praeteriens titulum ne desere nostrum et precor ut deicas sit tibi terra levis Valeria Laura dilargitur\n
EDCS-09100861.translation: Gaius Aebutius Tardus, son of Gaius Aebutius Verecundus, a soldier of the Sixth Legion in Syria. My eighteenth year I had now completed when cruel fate suddenly broke my path; I was a soldier in the Sixth Legion which bravely bore arms in Syria, and fortune exercised too harsh a wrath. Thus it happened that the torrent snatched me away before my day. Did no one heed my wishes, or bring back my glorious deeds of military service to my parents? Alas, wretched parents who bore me such! I was not permitted to remain in my father's embrace, nor you, wretched mother, who weep so abandoned, for she lies ruined by the death of her father. Nor do I pass you by, my dear one, sharing our solace. I am indignant that we were not allowed to enjoy the sweet embrace and kisses of a dying man. Holding me with a weakening hand, he gave kisses. If there is a path and access to the tomb, may my fate submit to the established order. Unhappy, I possess foreign airs, and no stranger pressed my eyes or hands. Passing stranger, do not abandon our inscription, and I beg you, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' Valeria Laura bestows.
EDCS-09100861.summary: A lengthy epitaph, partly in verse, from a soldier of the Sixth Legion in Syria named Gaius Aebutius Tardus, lamenting his premature death and dedicated by Valeria Laura.

EDCS-09200319.translation: To Hercules, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09200319.summary: A dedication to the god Hercules, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09200321.text_edition: \n Iuniae Luci filiae Antiquae Publius Valerius Antiquos sic matri piissumae sic annorum XXXXV\n
EDCS-09200321.translation: To Iunia Antiqua, daughter of Lucius. Publius Valerius Antiquos [set this up] for his most dutiful mother, 45 years old.
EDCS-09200321.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Valerius Antiquos for his 45-year-old mother, Iunia Antiqua.

EDCS-09300278.text_edition: \n Vurovio Lucius Cassius Flaccus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300278.translation: Lucius Cassius Flaccus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300278.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Lucius Cassius Flaccus, likely to a local deity.

EDCS-09300279.text_edition: \n Vurovio Turaius Eburenius Calaeti filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300279.translation: Turaius Eburenius, son of Calaetus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300279.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Turaius Eburenius, son of Calaetus, likely to a local deity.

EDCS-09300282.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed: Accannius.
EDCS-09300282.summary: A funerary dedication to the Manes, honoring Accannius.

EDCS-09300283.translation: To Ammia, daughter of denae Ele, wife of Caerri...
EDCS-09300283.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Ammia, daughter of denae Ele and wife of Caerri.

EDCS-09300293.text_edition: \n Pompeiae Flavinae Flavi filiae annorum XX parentes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09300293.translation: Her parents arranged for this to be set up for Pompeia Flavia, daughter of Flavius, aged 20.
EDCS-09300293.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeia Flavia, erected by her parents.

EDCS-09300294.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Coeliae Maternae Quoeli sic filiae annorum XXX liberti Coelius Sextanus et Coelia Hospita et Coeli\n
EDCS-09300294.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: For Coelia Materna, daughter of Quoelius, aged 30. Her freedmen Coelius Sextanus and Coelia Hospita and Coelius [arranged for this].
EDCS-09300294.summary: A funerary inscription for Coelia Materna, set up by her freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300295.text_edition: \n Genio loci Ati Fr votum posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300295.translation: Ati Fr gladly and deservedly placed this vow to the genius of the place.
EDCS-09300295.summary: A dedication to the local genius by Ati Fr, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09300298.text_edition: \n Pompeio annorum XXIIII Pompeius Caccus et Clodia Elena parentes ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09300298.translation: Pompeius Caccus and Clodia Elena, parents, arranged for this to be set up for Pompeius, aged 24.
EDCS-09300298.summary: A funerary monument for Pompeius, aged 24, erected by his parents.

EDCS-09300301.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus pontifex maxsumus sic consul XI designatus XII tribunicia potestate XVIII imperator XIIII a Segisamone milia passuum II\n
EDCS-09300301.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine [Augustus], chief priest, consul designate for the 11th time, consul for the 12th time, with tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 14th time, 2 miles from Segisamon.
EDCS-09300301.summary: A milestone inscription from the reign of Augustus, likely dating to between 8 BCE and 5 CE, indicating distance.

EDCS-09300305.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Severi Pertinacis filio divi Marci Aureli nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Brittannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori III consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli\n
EDCS-09300305.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine Severus Pertinax, grandson of the Divine Marcus Aurelius, great-grandson of the Divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the Divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the Divine Trajan Parthicus and the Divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, greatest conqueror of Germany, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 19th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-09300305.summary: A lengthy imperial titulature inscription for Caracalla, dating to his reign (c. 212-213 CE) and listing his numerous titles and honors.

EDCS-09300306.text_edition: \n Cornelia mulieris liberta Bucca hic sita est nnorum XXVII Memmius Clodus contubernalis\n
EDCS-09300306.translation: Cornelia Bucca, freedwoman of Cornelia, is buried here, aged 27. Memmius Clodus, her partner, [erected this].
EDCS-09300306.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Bucca, a freedwoman, erected by her partner Memmius Clodus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301030.text_edition: \n Flavo filio Flavia filio posuit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09301030.translation: Flavia placed this for her son Flavo; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09301030.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavo, set up by his mother Flavia, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-09301031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonio Paterno Paterni filio nnorum LIII\n
EDCS-09301031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonio Paterno, son of Paterni, aged 53.
EDCS-09301031.summary: A tombstone for Antonio Paterno, son of Paterni, aged 53, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-09301032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemilio Clementino Publi filio Publius Aemilius Severini posuit annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-09301032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aemilius Severini set up [this] for Aemilius Clementinus, son of Publius, aged 35.
EDCS-09301032.summary: A funerary inscription for Aemilius Clementinus, son of Publius, aged 35, erected by Publius Aemilius Severini.

EDCS-09301033.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Ursus pater Valerio Fortunatiano annorum XIII posuit\n
EDCS-09301033.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Ursus set up [this] for Valerius Fortunatiano, aged 13.
EDCS-09301033.summary: A tombstone for Valerius Fortunatiano, aged 13, erected by his father Valerius Ursus.

EDCS-09301034.text_edition: \n Caius Terentius Maternus Eturissensis annorum XVII hoc loco sepultus est\n
