EDCS-11800417.translation: For the safety of our lord, Optatus the slave, from his own money, (dedicated these items): 4 marble bases, a bronze censer, a tomb, a bronze lamp, a double-wicked lamp, 4 bronze coins, a shield, and a curtain.
EDCS-11800417.summary: This is a votive dedication by Optatus, a slave, offering various items for the emperor's well-being, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11800418.text_edition: \n Iullo Antonio Fabio Africano ante diem XVII Kalendas Apriles civitas Bocchoritana ex insula Baliarum maiorum patronum cooptavit Marcum Crassum Frugi leiberos sic posterosque eius Marcus Crassus Frugi consul in suam suorumque clientelam recepit egerunt Caius Coelius Cai filius et Caius Caecilius Titi filius legati\n
EDCS-11800418.translation: On the 17th day before the Kalends of April, the citizen body of Bocchoris, from the island of Majorca, elected Marcus Crassus Frugi, consul, as their patron, and he received him, his freedmen, and his descendants into his and his clients' protection. This was proposed by Gaius Coelius, son of Caius, and Gaius Caecilius, son of Titus, envoys.
EDCS-11800418.summary: This inscription records the adoption of Marcus Crassus Frugi as patron by the citizens of Bocchoris on the island of Majorca, with specific officials acting as intermediaries, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-11800420.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneli Attici e firmo signo Pancrati hic iacet infelix fato deceptus iniquo solitus assiduis durare membra palaestris arte quoque signi sui populo placuitque frequenter qui terra fruge creatus et igne ac fumo crematus iam nihil existis nisi quod superfuit igni ossa atque cinis iacent sub tegmine saxi\n
EDCS-11800420.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Here lies Cornelii Atticus, from Firmo Signo Pancratius, unhappy, deceived by cruel fate, accustomed to endure his limbs in constant wrestling, and pleasing to the people by the skill of his art. Now, nothing remains of him who was created from earth, fire, and smoke, except what the fire left behind: bones and ashes lie beneath the covering of this stone.
EDCS-11800420.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelii Atticus, who is eulogized for his athletic prowess and his demise, suggesting a date within the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800421.text_edition: \n Quintus Favonius Pusinnus vixit annis XXX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11800421.translation: Quintus Favonius Pusinnus lived 30 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11800421.summary: This is a tombstone recording the age of Quintus Favonius Pusinnus, typical of Roman funerary epitaphs.

EDCS-11800446.text_edition: \n Arguta fidelis in pace vixit nnos XL de posita II Idus Iulias\n
EDCS-11800446.translation: Arguta, faithful, lived 40 years in peace. Buried on the 13th day of July.
EDCS-11800446.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan and burial date of Arguta, indicating her faithful service and peaceful life.

EDCS-11800447.summary: This is a dedication or funerary epitaph to Gallus Asinius, with the name possibly indicating a Gallic origin or connection.

EDCS-11800449.translation: Lucius Flavius Apro made (this) 30 (years ago/for 30 years/etc.).
EDCS-11800449.summary: This inscription indicates that Lucius Flavius Apro was the maker of something, with the number XXX possibly referring to a duration or quantity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800455.text_edition: \n vixit nnos Sossius us filius patri carissimo\n
EDCS-11800455.translation: Sossius, son of Sossius, lived (X years) for his dearest father.
EDCS-11800455.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son, Sossius, to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800469.translation: To Ionius, to his deserving tent-mate.
EDCS-11800469.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph from one soldier to his fellow soldier ('contubernalis'), suggesting a military context.

EDCS-11800470.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Paditu ave vale Sergia Spuri filia Cuduniu hic sita est\n

EDCS-11800474.text_edition: \n Caius Antestius annos vixit XX ave vale\n

EDCS-11800480.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus CFP LCV IAILI fecit vixit annos LXX\n

EDCS-11800482.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Scaraotia avae annos XXX posuit Quartio\n

EDCS-11800489.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Severi Pii filio divi Marci Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Maximo Britannico Maximo Germanico Maximo Pontifici Maximo tribunicia potestate XVII imperatori III consuli III procos\n

EDCS-11800494.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pater patriae refecit\n

EDCS-11800504.text_edition: \n Marco Cavio Squilla Callicano Sexto Carminio Vetere consulibus XI Kalendas Maias hoc in ESAEDINE cognomine COBRARIA SCVTATIVM INO\n

EDCS-11800505.text_edition: \n Sexto Quintilio Condiano et Virio consulibus XVII Kalendas Maias saedine I Publio Rufino V Numerio oni Victore Sexto NVAS PHAL\n

EDCS-11800534.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Fabio Propinquo Lucius Fabius Propinquos pater filio piissimo vixit annis XXXV\n

EDCS-11800535.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caecilianus vixit annos LXXXX\n
EDCS-11800535.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilianus lived for 90 years.
EDCS-11800535.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caecilianus, who lived to be 90 years old.

EDCS-11800536.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoni Apollinaris frumentari bene merito coniux pia fecit\n
EDCS-11800536.translation: His devoted wife made this for Antoni Apollinaris, a deserving *frumentarius* (grain official), in honor of the departed spirits.
EDCS-11800536.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, Antoni Apollinaris, who was a *frumentarius*.

EDCS-11800543.text_edition: \n SI CE annos XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-11800543.translation: SI CE lived for 30 years; he lies here.
EDCS-11800543.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of an individual identified as SI CE, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-11800894.text_edition: \n Lucretius Sabinus Genio Viriocelensi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11800894.translation: Lucretius Sabinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the Genio Viriocelensi (Genius of Viriocela).
EDCS-11800894.summary: A votive inscription in which Lucretius Sabinus pays a vow to the local Genius of Viriocela.

EDCS-11800897.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Falernus Cotoninus miles legionis VII geminae piae felicis votum solvit Marti\n
EDCS-11800897.translation: Lucius Valerius Falernus Cotoninus, a soldier of the Seventh Twin Legion Pia Felix, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Mars.
EDCS-11800897.summary: A military votive inscription by Lucius Valerius Falernus Cotoninus, a soldier of the VII Gemina legion, to the god Mars.

EDCS-11800900.text_edition: \n Iovi Sacer dedit Ambadus Alius Negali filius\n
EDCS-11800900.translation: Ambadus, son of Alius Negali, dedicated this to Jupiter.
EDCS-11800900.summary: A dedication of an object or place to Jupiter by Ambadus, son of Alius Negali.

EDCS-11800903.text_edition: \n ------ pr]o salute et reditu Aciliorum Cai et Severi Calpurnius Flaccus Proculi filius Suestatiensis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11800903.translation: Calpurnius Flaccus, son of Proculus, from Suestatium, willingly and deservedly paid his vow for the health and safe return of Acilius Caius and Severus.
EDCS-11800903.summary: A votive inscription by Calpurnius Flaccus for the well-being and return of Acilius Caius and Severus.

EDCS-11800905.text_edition: \n Valerius Badan Abisunsonis posuit ex votu Itsacurrine pro saluote sic et reditu libente animo\n
EDCS-11800905.translation: Valerius Badan, son of Abisunis, set this up according to his vow to Itsacurris for health and safe return, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-11800905.summary: A votive inscription by Valerius Badan for the health and safe return of Itsacurris.

EDCS-11800910.translation: Gaius Annius Iuvenalis [dedicated this] to Silvanus.
EDCS-11800910.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus by Gaius Annius Iuvenalis.

EDCS-11800913.translation: Lucius Cussius [dedicated this] to Quintus Pa...
EDCS-11800913.summary: An incomplete inscription indicating a dedication by Lucius Cussius to someone named Quintus Pa.

EDCS-11800914.text_edition: \n Caeciliae Cai libertae Titidi nnorum XXXVIII mensium II dierum XXIII Fabius Messenius sorori piissimae\n
EDCS-11800914.translation: To Caecilia, freedwoman of Caius, aged thirty-eight years, two months, and twenty-three days, Fabius Messenius for his most devoted sister.
EDCS-11800914.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia, erected by her brother Fabius Messenius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800919.text_edition: \n Cornelius Iulianus annorum X hic situs est\n
EDCS-11800919.translation: Cornelius Iulianus, aged ten years, is laid here.
EDCS-11800919.summary: A simple funerary epitaph recording the death of a young boy named Cornelius Iulianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800920.text_edition: \n Gaius Grattius Servandus annorum L hic situs est\n
EDCS-11800920.translation: Gaius Grattius Servandus, aged fifty years, is laid here.
EDCS-11800920.summary: A straightforward funerary epitaph for Gaius Grattius Servandus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800921.summary: A funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Publius Herennius Florus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800929.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Fortunae Reduci et Genio legionis VII geminae piae felicis Quintus Caelius Felix aram quam speculator voverat accepta honesta missione ex commentariensi praesidis provinciae Hispaniae citerioris libens posuit\n
EDCS-11800929.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Fortune the Bringer-Back, and to the Genius of the Seventh Twin Legion, pious and fortunate, Quintus Caelius Felix willingly placed this altar, which as a messenger he had vowed, after receiving an honorable discharge, from the commentary of the governor of the province of Hispania Citerior.
EDCS-11800929.summary: A military dedication by Quintus Caelius Felix of the VII Gemina legion to Jupiter, Fortuna, and the legion's Genius, thanking them for an honorable discharge, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901105.summary: A brief votive inscription, possibly recording a vow made by M. Visugius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901107.translation: Iulia Tibura, daughter of Iulius Natraeus, aged thirteen years, is laid here. Iulius Natraeus saw to the making of this for himself and his daughter.
EDCS-11901107.summary: A funerary monument for Iulia Tibura, erected by her father Iulius Natraeus, who also arranged for his own burial there, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901108.translation: Oppia Meducena, daughter of Camalus, aged forty-five years, is laid here. Gaius Valerius Cirrus for his wife...
EDCS-11901108.summary: A funerary epitaph for Oppia Meducena, with her husband Gaius Valerius Cirrus also mentioned, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901109.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of a monument by Valeria Pica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901110.translation: To Iulia Severina, aged twenty years, in the colony of Caesar Augusta. Marcus Iulius Atticus made this for his wife and himself while alive. I ask you passing by to say: may the earth be light for you.
EDCS-11901110.summary: A funerary monument for Iulia Severina, erected by her husband Marcus Iulius Atticus for both of them, including a common epitaph request, from Colonia Caesar Augusta, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901112.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sempronia Paterni filia\n
EDCS-11901112.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Sempronia, daughter of Paternus.
EDCS-11901112.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Sempronia.

EDCS-11901113.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Voltinia Donatus miles le<g=C>(ionis) IIII Macedonicae nnum XXXIX hic situs est \n
EDCS-11901113.translation: Gaius Valerius Donatus, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, a soldier in the 4th Macedonian Legion, aged 39, is buried here.
EDCS-11901113.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Valerius Donatus, a soldier of the 4th Macedonian Legion, dated by his age.

EDCS-11901118.text_edition: \n Fortunae sacrum Valeria Sotera votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-11901118.translation: Sacred to Fortuna. Valeria Sotera gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11901118.summary: This is a votive offering to Fortuna by Valeria Sotera.

EDCS-11901121.text_edition: \n Cassio Aestivo Titi filio annorum XXXIX socero Cassiae Madigenae annorum XL uxori\n
EDCS-11901121.translation: To Cassius Aestivus, son of Titus, aged 39, and to Cassia Madigena, his wife, aged 40.
EDCS-11901121.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cassius Aestivus and his wife Cassia Madigena.

EDCS-11901122.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To the deserving and pious son Neo and Cornelia [faciendum curaverunt].
EDCS-11901122.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Cornelia to her son Neo, with restorations indicating the deceased was a son of Aquifilavius and 18 years old.

EDCS-11901123.text_edition: \n Segiusvirono Matieni filio annorum XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-11901123.translation: Segiusvironus, son of Matienus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-11901123.summary: This is a tombstone for Segiusvironus, dated by his age.

EDCS-11901125.text_edition: \n Simplicius et Primitiva Attiae LeucanI carissimae V hic est annorum XXX monumentum faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-11901125.translation: Simplicius and Primitiva had this monument made for Attia Leucana, most dear to them, aged 30.
EDCS-11901125.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Simplicius and Primitiva for their beloved Attia Leucana.

EDCS-11901127.text_edition: \n Silvano Titullus Calaedico Vim filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11901127.translation: To Silvanus. Titullus Calaedicus, son of Vim, gladly fulfilled his deserved vow.
EDCS-11901127.summary: This is a votive offering to Silvanus by Titullus Calaedicus.

EDCS-11901129.text_edition: \n Valeria Saturnina Licinia Paterna scum cusna annorum XV\n
EDCS-11901129.translation: Valeria Saturnina [and] Licinia Paterna [made this] for their daughter Cusna, aged 15.
EDCS-11901129.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cusna, erected by Valeria Saturnina and Licinia Paterna.

EDCS-11901131.translation: This monument was set up to the spirits of the departed for [---], son of [---], father-in-law of [---], and Lu[---] and Iulia dedicated it to them as the best, according to the will.
EDCS-11901131.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated by Iulia and Lu[---] as per a will, honors the deceased Flavus, his father-in-law, and another individual.

EDCS-11901139.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augusti Licinia Paterna Aureliani filia in memoriam sibi et suuis sic\n
EDCS-11901139.translation: To Fortuna Augusti, Licinia Paterna, daughter of Aurelianus, in memory for herself and her kin.
EDCS-11901139.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Augusti by Licinia Paterna, likely on a personal monument for family remembrance.

EDCS-11901144.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sergia Materna placed this for her granddaughter.
EDCS-11901144.summary: A funerary inscription recording that Sergia Materna dedicated a monument to her granddaughter, Sergia Materna.

EDCS-11901691.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Leontius, a citizen of Asturica, aged 27, from the archives office, was placed here by his father Rufonius Rufinus and his mother Rufia Paterna, to their most dutiful son.
EDCS-11901691.summary: A tombstone for Leontius, a 27-year-old man from Asturica who worked in the archives, set up by his parents.

EDCS-11901694.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Marcus Aurelius Severus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11901694.translation: Marcus Aurelius Severus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.
EDCS-11901694.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Marcus Aurelius Severus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-11901700.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To the spirits of the departed. The father placed this inscription for his two most dutiful sons who were killed on March 30th; [one son was] Paterno, aged 26.
EDCS-11901700.summary: A funerary inscription where a father dedicates a monument to his two sons, one of whom is named Paterno and died at age 26.

EDCS-11901732.translation: Gaius Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the divine Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-11901732.summary: This inscription lists the titles and offices held by Gaius Caesar (Caligula), emphasizing his connection to Augustus and his high status.

EDCS-11901750.text_edition: \n Fidei publicae Caius Publicius Melissus ob honorem IIIIIIviratus l(ocus) datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901750.translation: To Public Faith. Gaius Publicius Melissus was granted this place by decree of the decurions on account of the honor of holding the office of IIIIvir.
EDCS-11901750.summary: This inscription records the granting of a public space to Gaius Publicius Melissus by the town council in recognition of his magisterial office.

EDCS-11901751.summary: A simple dedication to the goddess Juno Regina.

EDCS-11901752.text_edition: \n Kauti Deo Lucius Valerius Monteius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11901752.translation: Lucius Valerius Monteius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Kautus.
EDCS-11901752.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Valerius Monteius fulfilling a vow to the deity Kautus.

EDCS-11901753.text_edition: \n Minervae Augustae Marcus Aufustius Homuncio IIIIIIvir Augustalis collegio fabrum dono posuit\n
EDCS-11901753.translation: To Minerva Augusta. Marcus Aufustius Homuncio, a IIIIvir Augustalis of the guild of craftsmen, dedicated this gift.
EDCS-11901753.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Augusta by Marcus Aufustius Homuncio, an official of the craftsmen's guild.

EDCS-11901755.text_edition: \n Dis deabus Silvanis Marcus Antonius Chrysogonus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11901755.translation: Marcus Antonius Chrysogonus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the gods Silvanus.
EDCS-11901755.summary: This inscription records a personal votive offering to the gods Silvanus by Marcus Antonius Chrysogonus.

EDCS-11901756.text_edition: \n Veneri Augustae Marcus Porcius Martialis ob honorrem seviratus \n
EDCS-11901756.translation: Marcus Porcius Martialis, for the honor of his office as *sevir*, to Venus Augusta.
EDCS-11901756.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Venus Augusta by Marcus Porcius Martialis in recognition of his status as a *sevir*.

EDCS-11901757.text_edition: \n Pharnaces ob libertatem Fuscae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11901757.translation: Pharnaces willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the freedom of Fusca.
EDCS-11901757.summary: This inscription records a personal votive offering by Pharnaces for the liberation of Fusca.

EDCS-11901760.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901760.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-11901760.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, enacted by the local town council.

EDCS-11901761.text_edition: \n Faustinae Augustae Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901761.translation: To Faustina Augusta, by decree of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and the town council.
EDCS-11901761.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Faustina Augusta, decreed by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and the local council.

EDCS-11901762.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurellio sic Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Brittannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII imperatori III consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli colonia Iulia Faventia Paterna Barcino\n
EDCS-11901762.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, High Priest, with tribunician power for the 19th year, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, by the Colonia Iulia Faventia Paterna Barcino.
EDCS-11901762.summary: This inscription is a lengthy dedication by the colony of Barcino to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, listing his extensive titles and honors.

EDCS-11901763.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli II proconsuli patri patriae maximoque principi nostro ordo Barcinonensium devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-11901763.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Augustus, High Priest, with tribunician power, Consul for the 2nd time, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland, and our Greatest Prince, the town council of Barcino, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-11901763.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the town council of Barcino to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Claudius, detailing his titles.

EDCS-11901764.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Domitio Aureliano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Arabico maximo Gothico maximo Carpico maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli III proconsuli III principi nostro ordo Barcinonensium numini maiestatique eius Caio Iulio Cai filio Aniensi Senecae Liciniano flamini provinciae Hispaniae citerioris aedili IIvirro flamini praefecto fabrum tribuno militum legionis VI victricis piae fidelis et tribuno militum legionis XV Apollinaris decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901764.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Arabicus Maximus, Gothicus Maximus, Carpicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Consul for the 3rd time, Proconsul for the 3rd time, our Prince, by the town council of Barcino, devoted to his divine will and majesty; and to Gaius Julius, son of Caius, of the Aniensis tribe, Seneca Licinianus, *flamen* of the province of Hispania Citerior, *aedile* for the 2nd time, *duovir*, *flamen*, prefect of craftsmen, military tribune of the 6th Legion Victrix, pious and faithful, and military tribune of the 15th Legion Apollinaris.
EDCS-11901764.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the town council of Barcino to Emperor Aurelian and honors two local officials, Gaius Julius Seneca Licinianus and a tribune of the 15th Legion Apollinaris.

EDCS-11901765.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici Augusto po<n=M>tifici maximo tribunicia potestat<e=I> patri patriae principi nostro devotus numini aeius ordo Barcinonensium \n
EDCS-11901765.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Caro Pius Felix Augustus, High Priest, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, our Prince, devoted to his divine will, the town council of Barcino.
EDCS-11901765.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the town council of Barcino to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Caro, listing his titles.

EDCS-11901766.text_edition: \n [Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio?] Valerio Maximiano Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae proconsuli ordo Barcinonensium devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-11901766.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Invictus Augustus, High Priest, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, the town council of Barcino, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-11901766.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the town council of Barcino to Emperor Maximian, listing his imperial titles.

EDCS-11901776.text_edition: \n Lucio Pedanio Luci filio Secundo Iulio Persico\n
EDCS-11901776.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Secundus, son of Lucius, son of Persicus.
EDCS-11901776.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Pedanius Secundus, likely a dedication on a tombstone.

EDCS-11901779.text_edition: \n Lucio Gavio Romano Tromentina Vibio Secundo tribuno militum legionis III Augustae flamini provinciae Hispaniae citerioris decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901779.translation: To Lucius Gavius Romanus, of the Tromentina tribe, Vibius Secundus, military tribune of the Legio III Augusta, flamen of the province of Hispania Citerior, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11901779.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Vibius Secundus to Lucius Gavius Romanus, honoring his public offices as military tribune and provincial flamen, commissioned by the local council.

EDCS-11901790.text_edition: \n Lucius Licinius A Caius Iulius A Assedillus\n
EDCS-11901790.translation: Lucius Licinius and Caius Iulius Assedillus.
EDCS-11901790.summary: This inscription simply lists the names Lucius Licinius and Caius Iulius Assedillus, possibly on a shared monument or boundary marker.

EDCS-11901795.text_edition: \n ius Quinti filius Galeria Secundus aedilis? IIvir flamini sic ia Sexti filia Sexta soror Secundilla \n
EDCS-11901795.translation: Secundus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile? and duovir, and flamen; Sexta, daughter of Sextus, sister of Secundilla.
EDCS-11901795.summary: This inscription names several individuals, including Secundus who held magisterial and priestly offices, and Sexta and Sextilla, possibly related to Secundus or another figure.

EDCS-11901805.text_edition: \n Lucio Pedanio Luci liberto Euphroni IIIIIIviro Augustali Primus et Agathopus liberti Lucius Pedanius Clemens in memoriam Luci Pedani Euphronis cuius basis lapidea aere clusa vetustate erat corrupta statuam eius marmoreae superposuit permittente ordine Barcinonensium\n
EDCS-11901805.translation: To Lucius Pedanius Euphron, freedman of Lucius Pedanius, freedman of Lucius, formerly a freedman holding the office of IVvir Augustalis. Primus and Agathopus, freedmen, and Lucius Pedanius Clemens, his freedmen, have placed this marble statue on the stone base, which had been corrupted by age and was closed with bronze, with the permission of the magistrates of the citizens of Barcino.
EDCS-11901805.summary: This inscription is a dedication by freedmen and a patron to Lucius Pedanius Euphron, recording the replacement of a damaged stone base with a marble statue, commissioned by the officials of Barcino.

EDCS-11901806.text_edition: \n Caio Trocinae Cai liberto Onesimo IIIIIIviro Augustali Philetus libertus heres ex testamento\n
EDCS-11901806.translation: To Onesimus, freedman of Caius Trocina, freedman of Caius, IVvir Augustalis. Philetus, freedman and heir by will.
EDCS-11901806.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Onesimus, a freedman and IVvir Augustalis, by Philetus, his freedman and heir.

EDCS-11901812.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus I IIIIIIviri Augustalis sepulchrum I NICRAE heres ex testamento\n
EDCS-11901812.translation: To the departed spirits. Tomb of NICRA, IVvir Augustalis. Heres ex testamento.
EDCS-11901812.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Nicra, who held the office of IVvir Augustalis, erected by her heir.

EDCS-11901819.text_edition: \n Lucio Iul(io) HermetIano Aulus Aemilius Alexander amico optimo\n
EDCS-11901819.translation: To Lucius Iulius Hermetianus, Aulus Aemilius Alexander, his best friend.
EDCS-11901819.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aulus Aemilius Alexander to his friend Lucius Iulius Hermetianus.

EDCS-11901828.translation: Gemell, son of Cornelius, to Caius Cassius Cornelius.
EDCS-11901828.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Gemell, son of Cornelius, to Caius Cassius Cornelius, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-11901831.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Cosme <m=A>atri bene merenti \n
EDCS-11901831.translation: To Cornelia Cosme, who deserved well of her husband.
EDCS-11901831.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Cornelia Cosme, erected by her grieving husband.

EDCS-11901837.text_edition: \n Flaviae Manliae Flavius FilATIS DEAETAS hic sita est\n

EDCS-11901839.text_edition: \n to annorum XV Caius Granius et Aemilia Su hic situs est\n

EDCS-11901844.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Iulius Repostus Quinto Iulio Primigenio Reposto fratri bene merenti fecit quo vivus postulavit locum\n

EDCS-11901845.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Paternae Plutianus uxori bene merenti\n

EDCS-11901846.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iunius Vitalis Cleraniae Beroniceni uxxori sic bene merenti fecit\n

EDCS-11901850.text_edition: \n Lucio Minicio Diadumeno marito carissimo uxor\n

EDCS-11901852.text_edition: \n Quinto Nonio Felici heres ex testamento fecit\n

EDCS-11901858.text_edition: \n Viva Quintia mulieris liberta Privata sibi et @(obito) Quinto Marcio Quinti liberto Modesto contubernali\n

EDCS-11901862.text_edition: \n Caius Trocina Parthicus hic situs est annorum VIIII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11901862.translation: Gaius Trocina Parthicus is buried here, aged 9. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901862.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of a young boy named Gaius Trocina Parthicus, with a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-11901865.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Aniensis Rufinus sibi et Corneliae Spuri filiae Homullae contubernali Cornelio Spuri filio Hispano Cornelio Spuri filio Marcello Hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur nec locus sepulturae\n
EDCS-11901865.translation: Lucius Valerius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, for himself and for Cornelia Homulla, daughter of Spurius, his companion, and for Cornelius Hispanus, son of Spurius, and Cornelius Marcellus, son of Spurius. This monument shall not pass to an heir, nor shall the burial plot.
EDCS-11901865.summary: A funerary monument established by Lucius Valerius Rufinus for himself, his wife Cornelia Homulla, and two others, stipulating that the plot cannot be inherited.

EDCS-11901868.text_edition: \n Vassia Luci filia Sabina hic sita est Valeria Marci filia mater Cnaeus Ratumedius Cai filius Vatia de suo fecerunt\n
EDCS-11901868.translation: Vassia Sabina, daughter of Lucius, is buried here. Valeria, daughter of Marcus, and Gnaeus Ratumedius, son of Caius, made this from their own resources.
EDCS-11901868.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Vassia Sabina, erected by her mother Valeria and stepfather Gnaeus Ratumedius.

EDCS-11901869.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely part of a funerary dedication for a son from the Galeria tribe.

EDCS-11901875.summary: An incomplete inscription, possibly recording a dedication to or by a freedman of someone named Titus.

EDCS-11901876.summary: A fragmentary inscription with illegible text, possibly part of a name or title.

EDCS-11901877.text_edition: \n us Luci libertus annorum XLI sia \n
EDCS-11901877.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for a freedman of Lucius, mentioning his age.

EDCS-11901881.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Euhodus.
EDCS-11901881.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manes' (spirits of the underworld) for someone named Euhodus.

EDCS-11901882.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Feli AMI
EDCS-11901882.summary: A dedication to the 'Dis Manes' for someone named Feli, with an incomplete word following.

EDCS-11901883.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Geminiano Gemini filio nnorum VII\n
EDCS-11901883.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Geminianus, son of Gemini, aged 7.
EDCS-11901883.summary: A funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manes' for a boy named Geminianus, son of Gemini, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-11901884.translation: To the divine spirits of the departed. Hyathmat.
EDCS-11901884.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Hyathmat, likely a deceased individual, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901885.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be the end of a name, likely a personal name like Julius, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901889.translation: To the divine spirits of the departed. Trophimus, a man of importance.
EDCS-11901889.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Trophimus, described as a man of importance (VBIV), dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901928.text_edition: \n Botrocalis Iscolasticus sic Regnator Famosus Pyiripinus sic Harpastus Eufrata Eustolus Concordi Concordi Concordi isus ius us vor Niceti Concordi Concordi Eridanus Ispumeus sic Pelops Lucxuriosus sic Niceti Eridanus\n
EDCS-11901928.translation: Botrocalis, learned one; Regnator the famous; Pyiripinus; Harpastus; Eufrata; Eustolus; Concordius; Concordius; Concordius; Isus; Ius; vor; Nicetius; Concordius; Concordius; Eridanus; Ispumeus; Pelops; Lucxuriosus; Nicetius; Eridanus.
EDCS-11901928.summary: A very fragmented inscription listing numerous names, possibly a roster, a dedicatory list, or a list of freedmen, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12100676.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Victrici Publius Aelius Publi filius Hilarianus procurator Augusti cum liberis pro salute Commodi Augusti Pii Fel[icis]\n
EDCS-12100676.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, and Minerva Victrix. Publius Aelius Hilarianus, son of Publius, procurator of Augustus, with his children, for the health of Commodus Augustus Pius Felix.
EDCS-12100676.summary: A dedication to the Capitoline Triad by Publius Aelius Hilarianus, an imperial procurator, for the well-being of Emperor Commodus, dating to his reign (AD 177-192).

EDCS-12100678.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Vegetus et Aelius Dento magistri collegi de suo dignissimo curaverunt collegio\n
EDCS-12100678.translation: Sacred to the divine spirits of the departed. Aurelius Vegetus and Aelius Dento, masters of the college, have lovingly seen to it at their own expense for the college.
EDCS-12100678.summary: A funerary inscription recording a refurbishment of a college's property or monument by its masters, Aurelius Vegetus and Aelius Dento, during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12100683.text_edition: \n Lucius Octavius Luci Pupinia Baeter(ensis) Magius nnorum XXXVII aerorum sic XIX tubicen miles legionis X geminae centuria Titi Numisi heres exs sic testamento suo fecit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-12100683.translation: Lucius Octavius, son of Lucius, of the Pupinia tribe, from Baeterrae, aged 37 years, serving for 19 years, a trumpeter, soldier of the Legio X Gemina, in the century of Titus Numisius, his heir made this according to his will. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-12100683.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Octavius, a trumpeter in Legio X Gemina, who died at 37 after 19 years of service, with his heir setting up the monument, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12100765.translation: Gaius Caesar, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the third time, father of the country.
EDCS-12100765.summary: A titulary inscription listing the honors and offices held by Emperor Augustus, including his third consulship and third grant of tribunician power, likely dating to 27 BC.

EDCS-12700162.text_edition: \n Arganto Medutica Melmaniqum annorum L uxsor sic hic sit-- est\n
EDCS-12700162.translation: Arganto Medutica, aged 50 years. Here lies...
EDCS-12700162.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Arganto Medutica at the age of 50, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13301302.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Caeno TAMC centuria Nem decurio alae I Claudiae Gallorum COMP torquibus phaleris donatus Iovi Optimo Maximo votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-13301302.translation: Lucius Cassius Caeno, decurion of the 1st cavalry wing of Gauls, decorated with torques and phalerae, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-13301302.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Cassius Caeno, a decurion in the ala I Gallorum, honoring Jupiter Optimus Maximus after receiving military decorations.

EDCS-13301518.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius Caesar.
EDCS-13301518.summary: This is a dedication to Germanicus Caesar, son of Emperor Tiberius.

EDCS-13301519.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Luci filio Quirina Probo Aquicaldensi nnorum XXVII aedili IIviro Lucius Aemilius Celatus et Porcia Proba filio\n
EDCS-13301519.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Probus Aquicaldensis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, aged 27, aedile and duovir, Lucius Aemilius Celatus and Porcia Proba [dedicated this] to their son.
EDCS-13301519.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents for their son, Lucius Aemilius Probus, who held local magistracies and died at age 27.

EDCS-13301520.text_edition: \n Deo Seitundo aram votivam Campanus et Maximus\n
EDCS-13301520.translation: Campanus and Maximus dedicated this votive altar to the god Seitunus.
EDCS-13301520.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the god Seitunus by Campanus and Maximus.

EDCS-13301527.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Cai filius Galeria Montanus aedilis IIvir aedem et signum Tutelae sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-13301527.translation: Gaius Aemilius Montanus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile and duovir, built this temple and statue at his own expense.
EDCS-13301527.summary: This is a building inscription by Gaius Aemilius Montanus, who served as aedile and duovir, stating he funded the construction of a temple and statue.

EDCS-13301538.text_edition: \n Paullo Luci filio Aemilio Lepido patrono\n
EDCS-13301538.translation: To Paulus Aemilius Lepidus, son of Lucius, patron.
EDCS-13301538.summary: This is a dedication to Paulus Aemilius Lepidus, honored as a patron.

EDCS-13301542.text_edition: \n Lucius? Caecilius Luci filius Galeria Macer aedilis IIvir campum de sua pecunia faciundum coeravit idemque probavit\n
EDCS-13301542.translation: Lucius? Caecilius Macer, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile and duovir, arranged for the field to be made at his own expense and approved it himself.
EDCS-13301542.summary: This is a building inscription by Lucius Caecilius Macer, who served as aedile and duovir, concerning the construction and approval of a public field.

EDCS-13301547.text_edition: \n Manio Cornelio Mani filio Galeria Saturnino patri aedili IIviro flamini cultores Larum\n
EDCS-13301547.translation: To Manius Cornelius Saturninus, son of Manius, of the Galeria tribe, father, aedile, duovir, and flamen, [dedicated by] the cultores of the Lares.
EDCS-13301547.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated to Manius Cornelius Saturninus, who held multiple local civic and religious offices, by the 'cultores Larum' (worshippers of the household gods).

EDCS-13301550.text_edition: \n Lucio Minicio Luci filio Rufo aedili IIviro quaestori flamini Romae et Augusti Lucius Minicius Rufus \n
EDCS-13301550.translation: Lucius Minicius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the...? Rufus, aedile, duovir, quaestor, and flamen of Rome and Augustus, [dedicated this to] Lucius Minicius Rufus.
EDCS-13301550.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication set up by Lucius Minicius Rufus, who held several municipal offices including aedile, duovir, and quaestor, possibly to himself or a relative with the same name.

EDCS-13301552.text_edition: \n Lucio Rosio Luci filio Sergia Rufo aedili IIviro quaestori Lucio Rosio patri Rosiae matri\n
EDCS-13301552.translation: To Lucius Rosius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, aedile, quaestor, [dedicated this to] Lucius Rosius, father, and Rosia, mother.
EDCS-13301552.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Rosius Rufus, who held municipal offices, for his parents Lucius Rosius and Rosia.

EDCS-13301568.text_edition: \n Fulvia trix Fulvio Fausto fil(io) et Marco Loreio Celeri adfini\n

EDCS-13301571.text_edition: \n Iulia Sexti liberta Aucta hic est sita\n

EDCS-13301580.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Sergii Pauli Iunioris ex testamento Cai Sergii Calendionis liberti\n

EDCS-13301589.text_edition: \n Publionio Publi liberto Hilaro Publioinio Publi filio N ia Marci filia Sabina sibi et viro et filio\n

EDCS-13301670.text_edition: \n Consilium Fulvi legati Olossitani Campanus Fidentinus Au[gusti ------ Fulvus legatus Augusti Rufus legatus Augusti Maturus proqurator sic Augusti legati atvocati sic Indicetanorum\n
EDCS-13301670.translation: The council of Fulvus, legate of the Augusti, and Campanus Fidentinus, adviser of the Augusti, with Fulvus, legate of the Augusti, Rufus, legate of the Augusti, and Maturus, procurator of the Augusti, the council of the legates of the Augusti, advocates of the Indicetani.
EDCS-13301670.summary: This inscription records a legal council or advisory body related to Roman officials, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-13301673.text_edition: \n Maturus proqurator sic Augusti consilium legati legati Indicetanorum Indicet Olossitani Titus Aurelius Fulvus legatus Augusti Rufus legatus Augusti\n
EDCS-13301673.translation: Maturus, procurator of the Augusti, the council of the legates of the Augusti, the legates of the Indicetani, the Olossitani, Titus Aurelius Fulvus, legate of the Augusti, Rufus, legate of the Augusti.
EDCS-13301673.summary: This inscription lists Roman officials, including a procurator and legates, possibly related to the Indicetani and Olossitani communities.

EDCS-13301675.text_edition: \n Olossitani Sempronius Campanus Fidentinus atveversari sic mei necui NI N Fulvus legatus Augusti Rufus legatus Augusti Maturus proqurator Augusti consilium legati atvocati sic Indicetanorum\n
EDCS-13301675.translation: The Olossitani, Sempronius Campanus Fidentinus, against me, Fulvus, legate of the Augusti, Rufus, legate of the Augusti, Maturus, procurator of the Augusti, the council of the legates, advocates of the Indicetani.
EDCS-13301675.summary: This inscription appears to be a legal or administrative record involving Roman officials and the Olossitani and Indicetani, possibly related to a dispute or legal proceeding.

EDCS-13301676.text_edition: \n Veranio Pupilius Stabilionis Polinidorus Philargurus Scapi Surisca Alexae Papus Amphio Parnacis Zodiana omnes quei inimeici Senecae\n
EDCS-13301676.translation: To Veranius Pupilius, Polinidorus, Philargurus, Scapi, Surisca, Alexae, Papus, Amphio, Parnacis, Zodiana, all enemies of Seneca.
EDCS-13301676.summary: This inscription names individuals who are declared enemies of someone named Seneca, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13301681.text_edition: \n L S C Luci Sextili Callisti\n
EDCS-13301681.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Sextilius Callistus, likely part of a funerary or honorary monument.

EDCS-13301682.summary: This inscription is a simple name, Sextus Rulius Neos, possibly a tombstone or an owner's mark.

EDCS-13301692.text_edition: \n Domino? nostro? Flavio Claudio Constantino Augusto Domino nostro Flavio Theodosio Pio Felici Augusto bono rei publicae nato\n
EDCS-13301692.translation: To our Lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus Augustus, our Lord Flavius Theodosius, the Pious, the Fortunate Augustus, born for the good of the Republic.
EDCS-13301692.summary: This inscription honors two Roman emperors, Constantine and Theodosius, likely dating to the 4th or 5th century CE.

EDCS-13400258.translation: To the great god Peremustae, Marcella gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for the safety of herself and her family.
EDCS-13400258.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Peremustae, dedicated by Marcella for her well-being and that of her family, likely from a provincial area.

EDCS-13600136.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus da miserissimi parentes aliam in te in te ispe<m=N> havevamus sic aliut sic fata dederunt quae te nobis abistulerunt reliquisti nobis aeternas lacrimas luctosque per annos pietatem tuam requirimus et nusquam te fili dulcissime inveniemus Fortunata et Heliodorus parentes carissimi Pomponio filio carissimo et incompara<b=D>ili monumentum posuerunt annos situs sepultus XXV et ego Heliodorus posui me cum filio meo annorum situs sepultus LXXXXV et Acarifaciae nurae pientissimae annorum XXV \n
EDCS-13600136.translation: To the departed spirits. Most wretched parents, we had another in you, but fate decreed otherwise and took you from us, leaving us eternal tears and grief for years. We miss your piety, and we will never find you, dearest son. Fortunata and Heliodorus, dearest parents, placed this monument to their dearest and incomparable son Pomponius, who died at 25 years of age. And I, Heliodorus, have placed [this for] myself, who died at 95 years of age, and for my most pious mother Acarifacia, aged 25.
EDCS-13600136.summary: This is a deeply personal funerary inscription from parents mourning their son Pomponius and also commemorating Heliodorus and his mother, dating likely to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13900143.text_edition: \n Dercinoassedenses vicanii Cluniensium adit cum suis ornamentis populo Termestino de sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt Dercinoassedensibus vicanis Cluniensium liberis posterisque eorum senatus populusque Termestinus concessit ut eodem iure essent Term<i=E>s quo cives Termestini IIIIviris Lucio Licinio Pilo Marco Terentio Celso Lucio Pompeio Vitulo Tito Pompeio Raro \n
EDCS-13900143.translation: The villagers of Dercinoassedenses, from the Cluniensians, with their ornaments, took care to have this made for the people of Termes from their own money. The Senate and People of Termes granted to the free villagers of Dercinoassedenses from the Cluniensians, for their children and descendants, that they might be under the same law as the citizens of Termes, under the IIIIviri Lucius Licinius Pilo, Marcus Terentius Celsus, Lucius Pompeius Vitulus, Titus Pompeius Rarus.
EDCS-13900143.summary: This inscription records a grant of citizenship rights by the Termes community to the villagers of Dercinoassedenses, under the authority of local magistrates.

EDCS-14100198.text_edition: \n Cornelius vicanus Aunigainum Cesti filius aram possuit sic deo Erudino X Kalendis Augustis Manlio Eutropio consulibus\n
EDCS-14100198.translation: Cornelius, a villager of Aunigainum, son of Cestus, erected this altar to the god Erudinus on the tenth day before the Kalends of August, when Manlius Eutropius was consul.
EDCS-14100198.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the god Erudinus by a local villager, dated to the consulship of Manlius Eutropius.

EDCS-14700045.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus tribunicia potestate VIIII et pro consule dicit castellanos Paemeiobrigenses ex gente Susarrorum desciscentibus ceteris permansisse in officio cognovi ex omnibus legatis meis qui Transdurianae provinciae praefuerunt itaque eos universos immunitate perpetua dono quosque agros et quibus finibus possederunt Lucio Sestio Quirinale legato meo eam provinciam optinentem eos agros sine controversia possidere iubeo castellanis Paemeiobrigensibus ex gente Susarrorum quibus ante ea immunitatem omnium rerum dederam eorum loco restituo castellanos Aiiobrigiaecinos ex gente Gigurrorum volente ipsa civitate eosque castellanos Aiiobrigiaecinos omni munere fungi iubeo cum Susarris actum Narbone Martio XVI et XV Kalendas Martias Marco Druso Libone Lucio Calpurnio Pisone consulibus\n
EDCS-14700045.translation: I, Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, in my ninth tribunician power and as proconsul, have learned that the castellans of Paemeiobriga, from the Susarri tribe, remained loyal to their duty while others defected, as reported by all my legates who governed the Transdurian province. Therefore, I grant them perpetual immunity for all their lands and the boundaries they have possessed. While my legate Lucius Sestius Quirinalis held that province, I ordered them to possess those lands without dispute. I restore to their place the castellans of Aiiobrigiaecinos, from the Gigurri tribe, at their own request, and I order these castellans of Aiiobrigiaecinos to perform all public duties along with the Susarri. This business was concluded at Narbo Martius on the sixteenth and fifteenth days before the Kalends of March, when Marcus Drusus Libo and Lucius Calpurnius Piso were consuls.
EDCS-14700045.summary: This is an imperial rescript by Augustus granting perpetual immunity and land rights to loyal castellans of Paemeiobriga and Aiiobrigiaecinos, dated to the consulship of Drusus Libo and Calpurnius Piso.

EDCS-14700049.text_edition: \n Ambato Virovarco Munoimo Elanoso Lubos Cairo Ant MNNIMV RI Amitimum Virovacom\n
EDCS-14700049.translation: Ambatus Virovarcus Munoimos Elanosos Lubos Cairo Ant MNNIMV RI Amitimum Virovacom.
EDCS-14700049.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to a local community or group, with no clear indication of date or purpose beyond identifying individuals.

EDCS-14700050.translation: Aemilius Quintilianus, to the Apillarian Mothers, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14700050.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aemilius Quintilianus to the Matres Apillares.

EDCS-14700051.text_edition: \n Valerius Atto Saigleiniqum Acconis filius annorum LXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-14700051.translation: Valerius Atto, son of Saigleiniqum, aged 62, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Atto, son of Saigleiniqum, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14700053.text_edition: \n Maternus Culericon Aionis filius Silvano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14700053.translation: Maternus Culericon, son of Aionis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-14700053.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus by Maternus Culericon, son of Aionis.

EDCS-14700054.text_edition: \n Antonius Addio Eburanco Aemili filius annorum XXV hic situs est Aemilius Blanda mater Aquilus et Addo fratres de suo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-14700054.translation: Antonius Addio Eburancus, son of Aemilius, aged 25, is buried here. His mother Aemilia Blanda and his brothers Aquilus and Addo arranged for this to be made from their own resources.
EDCS-14700054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Addio Eburancus, aged 25, funded by his mother and brothers.

EDCS-14700057.text_edition: \n Pompei Fusci annorum LX Antonia Maellusa marito posuit\n
EDCS-14700057.translation: Antonia Maellusa placed this for her husband Pompei Fuscus, aged 60.
EDCS-14700057.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Antonia Maellusa for her husband Pompei Fuscus.

EDCS-14700059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabio Urbanillo uxxor sic merenti nnum vixit XXXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-14700059.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His wife placed this for Urbanillus, son of Fabius, who deserved it. He lived 50 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabio Urbanillus, erected by his wife and indicating his age and burial.

EDCS-14700060.text_edition: \n Caius Vandius Aemilius hic situs est\n
EDCS-14700060.translation: Caius Vandius Aemilius is buried here.
EDCS-14700060.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating the burial of Caius Vandius Aemilius.

EDCS-14700061.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Pentius hic situs est\n
EDCS-14700061.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Marcus Fabius Pentius.

EDCS-14700062.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Minicius Germanus annorum XXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-14700062.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Minicius Germanus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700062.summary: A tombstone for Minicius Germanus, who died at 25, with a standard funerary invocation.

EDCS-14700066.text_edition: \n Tertiae Primigenio Festae Potito Aegle Annius Fuscus Recti libertus sibi et suis\n
EDCS-14700066.translation: Aegle, freedwoman of Annius Fuscus, for Tertia, Primigenius, Festa, Potitus, and his own family.
EDCS-14700066.summary: A funerary dedication by Aegle, a freedwoman, for herself and several others.

EDCS-14700067.text_edition: \n Caio Aurelio SVIA Quintus Munius stus Toletanus cipum et sepulturam de suo dedit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-14700067.translation: To Gaius Aurelius SVIA, Quintus Munius of Toledo gave this monument and burial at his own expense. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700067.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Munius of Toledo for Gaius Aurelius SVIA.

EDCS-14700068.text_edition: \n ------ I]ulius servae optumae sic et bene merenti\n
EDCS-14700068.translation: [To his] devoted servant, who served well and faithfully.
EDCS-14700068.summary: A dedication likely for a loyal freedwoman or slave.

EDCS-14700069.summary: An incomplete inscription that begins with the name C. Sempronius.

EDCS-14700074.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo M(aximo) Lucius Sempronius libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-14700074.translation: Lucius Sempronius willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-14700074.summary: A votive inscription recording Lucius Sempronius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-14700076.translation: son, Galeria Quadratus [dedicated this] to his wife...
EDCS-14700076.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription likely by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-14700077.summary: An incomplete tombstone that begins with the name Publilius and indicates an age.

EDCS-14700148.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici libens animo ex visu aram posuit\n

EDCS-14700155.text_edition: \n Optimo Iovi Maximo Geio Gaius Iulius Severinus ex voto\n
EDCS-14700155.translation: To the best and greatest Jupiter, Gaius Julius Severinus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14700155.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter fulfilled by Gaius Julius Severinus, likely on a private altar or shrine.

EDCS-14700241.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Niger, son of Camali.

EDCS-14700244.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Fortunata, his daughter, placed this for her mother Aunnia, and for her father Semnus, aged...
EDCS-14700244.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a daughter for her mother and father.

EDCS-14700285.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Albina, daughter of Avitus, placed this for her dearest and dutiful husband Avitus, aged 40, and for her dearest and dutiful daughter Avita, aged 12.
EDCS-14700285.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Albina for her husband Avitus and daughter Avita.

EDCS-14700393.text_edition: \n Imperatore Tito Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Vespasiano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatore XV patre patriae consule VIII Imperatore Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano consule VII \n
EDCS-14700393.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 15th time, father of the country, consul for the 8th time; Under Emperor Caesar Domitian, son of the deified Vespasian, consul for the 7th time.
EDCS-14700393.summary: This text is a consular dating formula, listing the titles and offices of Emperors Vespasian and Domitian.

EDCS-14700404.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Augustus...
EDCS-14700404.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honoring Emperor Probus.

EDCS-15800329.text_edition: \n Gaio Sulpicio Ursulo praefecto Symmachiariorum Asturum belli Dacici |(centurioni) legionis I Minerviae piae fidelis |(centurioni) cohortis XII urbanae |(centurioni) cohortis IIII praetoriae primo pilo legionis XIIX praefecto legionis III Augustae Gaius Sulpicius Africanus posuit\n
EDCS-15800329.translation: Gaius Sulpicius Africanus placed this for Gaius Sulpicius Ursulus, prefect of the Symmachiarii of Asturica, a centurion of the 1st Minervia legion, pious and faithful, a centurion of the 4th Praetorian cohort, first file centurion of the 18th legion, prefect of the 3rd Augusta legion.
EDCS-15800329.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Sulpicius Africanus for Gaius Sulpicius Ursulus, a soldier with a distinguished career.

EDCS-16200660.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Mucius Ianuarius pro salute filii Muci Calistiani votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-16200660.translation: Mucius Ianuarius willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of his son Mucius Calistianus.
EDCS-16200660.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Mucius Ianuarius for his son's well-being.

EDCS-16200662.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Capitolino Gaius Octavius ex (voto) posuit\n
EDCS-16200662.translation: Gaius Octavius placed this as a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus.
EDCS-16200662.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus by Gaius Octavius.

EDCS-16200664.text_edition: \n Domitio O equiti alae Flaviae II civium Romanorum domo Tabalaca annorum XXXVII aerarum XVII Caius Cornelius Serenus eques alae eiusdem municipi heres exs sic voluntate faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200664.translation: Caius Cornelius Serenus, heir from the same municipality, took care to have this made according to the will of Domitius O, a cavalryman of the Flavian II ala of Roman citizens, from Tabalaca, aged 37, of 17 years' service.
EDCS-16200664.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitius O, a cavalryman, with his heir Caius Cornelius Serenus arranging its construction.

EDCS-16200772.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius divi Iuli nepos Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXXV imperator VIIII sic consul V Turiasone milia XXII\n

EDCS-16200953.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Barbarus Medutticorum Cai filius hic situs est Aemilia Acca Medutticorum Barbari mater hic sita est Caius Iulius Labeo Crastunonis filius Medutticorum hic situs est\n

EDCS-16201544.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Scribonius Fuscinus Suestatiensis annorum LXXV hic situs est Aemilia Materna Suestatiensis annorum XXX hic sita est Scribonia Frontina annorum V hic sita est Scribonius Fronto patri uxsori sic vivus faciendum curavit\n

EDCS-16201631.text_edition: \n Tutelae sacrum Marcus Caecilius Montanus pro salute Fusci fili sui posuit QVNO fecit\n

EDCS-16300116.text_edition: \n Dobiterus Caburoniqum Equaesi filius sit tibi terra levis Arena Mentoviequm Aergui filia sit tibi terra levis\n

EDCS-16700168.text_edition: \n Persius Marci filius Pollia esus domo Hasta miles legionis X geminae centuria Sils nnorum L aerorum sic XXVI hic situs est\n

EDCS-16700299.text_edition: \n Baebius Latro Nigri filius Uxama Ibarcensis annorum XIII hic situs est\n

EDCS-16900276.text_edition: \n Valeriae Cai filiae Faustae Caio Marcio Cai filio Senecae hoc monumentum heredi non cedit Marcus Aemilius Avitus Cornelia Publi filia Primula mater Cornelia Quieta\n

EDCS-16900285.text_edition: \n filius Festus Turpilla Publi filius filius \n
EDCS-16900285.translation: Festus, son of Turpilla, son of Publius.
EDCS-16900285.summary: This inscription names Festus, son of Turpilla, and grandson of Publius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-16900287.translation: Crescens and Piganco[n?], son of [3]ia, and his wife [3]ri. [Here lies]... had this made.
EDCS-16900287.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription recording the burial of Crescens and his wife, with an indication that someone paid for the monument.

EDCS-16900288.translation: Severus of the VIIth Legion Gemina Felix, Crescens, set this up.
EDCS-16900288.summary: This inscription records Severus, a soldier of the VII Legion Gemina Felix, and indicates that Crescens erected the monument.

EDCS-16900289.translation: To Rustica, daughter of Meduttus, Avia.
EDCS-16900289.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription to Rustica, daughter of Meduttus, by Avia.

EDCS-16900332.text_edition: \n Atta Turocom Gai uxsor sic hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Gaius Turocom Pacati filius vivus sibi monumentum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16900332.translation: Atta Turocom, wife of Gaius, lies here. May the earth be light on you. Gaius Turocom, son of Pacatus, arranged for this monument to be made during his lifetime for himself.
EDCS-16900332.summary: This is a self-funded funerary inscription set up by Gaius Turocom for himself and his wife Atta, with a wish for her soul.

EDCS-16900408.summary: This brief inscription likely records the name of Marcus Statorius Amphio, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-17100189.text_edition: \n Marti Caius Petronius Maternus Quinti filius missicius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17100189.translation: To Mars, Caius Petronius Maternus, son of Quintus, a discharged soldier, willingly paid his vow as deserved.
EDCS-17100189.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Petronius Maternus, a retired soldier, to the god Mars.

EDCS-17800381.translation: To the holy god Larius, Breo Bro.
EDCS-17800381.summary: This is a dedication to the god Larius, possibly by Breo Bro.

EDCS-18100083.text_edition: \n Gaio Voconio Venusto annorum X Gaio Voconio Primigenio patri annorum LV\n
EDCS-18100083.translation: To Gaius Voconius Venustus, aged 10, and to Gaius Voconius Primigenius, his father, aged 55.
EDCS-18100083.summary: This inscription records the names and ages of Gaius Voconius Venustus and his father Gaius Voconius Primigenius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-18100089.translation: You break knees in the rough joint with your shins. As much as my old age indicates in this way, as it is the height of war. Give me a little kiss, lord. Playful girls always laugh with pleasure. If only... Caius Valerius Verdullus paints.
EDCS-18100089.summary: This fragmented inscription contains a mix of themes, possibly a witty epitaph or poem, and ends with the name of the painter Caius Valerius Verdullus.

EDCS-18100245.translation: By order of our lords Avitus and Faustina, a cistern was built, 13 feet wide, 17 feet long, 10 feet high, with a capacity of 125 (cubic) moduli.
EDCS-18100245.summary: This inscription records the construction of a public cistern, likely during the reign of emperors Avitus and Faustina.

EDCS-18100251.translation: By the favor of Hadrian, I have recorded here that three streams of water are brought across the valley, which will be recovered as private property.
EDCS-18100251.summary: This inscription details the management of water resources, referencing a benefit from Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-18100256.translation: Nemadora, wife of Lucius Lepidus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nemadora, wife of Lucius Lepidus, with a common wish for a light burial.

EDCS-18100258.translation: Contaeca, servant of Moeniccus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Contaeca, a servant of Moeniccus.

EDCS-18100261.translation: Turamus, son of Coerobrius, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-18100261.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Turamus, son of Coerobrius.

EDCS-18100268.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Petracio Flavo, aged 50. To the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Cloutus, aged [?].
EDCS-18100268.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment containing two funerary dedications to the De Manibus for individuals named Petracio Flavo and Flavius Cloutus.

EDCS-18100280.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Macilo, a freedman.

EDCS-18100327.text_edition: \n Accae Metelli servae annorum XXV Epaphroditus \n
EDCS-18100327.summary: This inscription commemorates Acca Metella, a servant aged 25, and possibly records the name of the dedicator, Epaphroditus.

EDCS-19000353.text_edition: \n Caio Publicio Melisso IIIIIIviro ob causas utilitatesque publicas fideliter et constanter defensas ordo Barcinonensium pecunia publica\n
EDCS-19000353.translation: The order of the people of Barcino, with public funds, for Gaius Publicius Melissus, as a IIIIvir for the public causes and advantages faithfully and steadfastly defended.
EDCS-19000353.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Barcino to Gaius Publicius Melissus, likely for his service as a magistrate.

EDCS-19100805.text_edition: \n ------ Sempr]onia Arganta Marcus Iulius Cephalo de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-19100805.translation: Sempronia Arganta, Marcus Julius Cephalo, from their own money.
EDCS-19100805.summary: This inscription records a dedication made at private expense by Sempronia Arganta and Marcus Julius Cephalo.

EDCS-19100841.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-19100841.summary: This is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Diocletian, likely on a public monument, dating to his reign (284-305 AD).

EDCS-19100923.text_edition: \n Thaumastus Cai S C servus annorum XIII hic situs est Acceptus posuit\n
EDCS-19100923.translation: Thaumastus, a slave of Caius S.C., aged thirteen years, lies here. Acceptus set this up.
EDCS-19100923.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Thaumastus, a slave boy thirteen years old, who was commemorated by Acceptus.

EDCS-20300494.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a primicius, a chief servant or official, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-20300495.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a primicius, a chief servant or official, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-20300982.summary: This inscription records ownership, identifying Quintus Atilius as a slave.

EDCS-20301052.summary: This inscription likely marks a tomb or property belonging to Gaius Titedius Moderatus.

EDCS-20301091.summary: This inscription indicates the relationship of a freedman named Trigetus to his former master, Lucius.

EDCS-20301770.summary: This inscription identifies a freedman named Caelius and his former master, Marcius.

EDCS-20301794.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name of Aulus Vibius, possibly for a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-20400039.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus QVSCOIECIVS annorum L hic situs est EVTNRA uxor marito bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-20400039.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quscoiejcius, aged fifty years, lies here. Eutnra, his wife, had this made for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20400039.summary: This epitaph records the death of Quscoiejcius, aged fifty, and commemorates his wife Eutnra's initiative in erecting the tombstone.

EDCS-20400097.summary: This very fragmentary inscription, likely a boundary marker or a simple direction, dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400118.text_edition: \n Caio Trocinae Cai liberto Synecdemo IIIIIIviro Augustali Valeria Haline marito optimo\n
EDCS-20400118.translation: To Gaius Synecdemus, freedman of Gaius, a municipal priest, husband of Valeria Haline, the best of husbands.
EDCS-20400118.summary: This epitaph honors Gaius Synecdemus, a freedman and municipal priest, by his wife Valeria Haline, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400120.text_edition: \n Semproniae Modestae Luci filiae annorum XL Modestus\n
EDCS-20400120.translation: To Sempronia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, aged 40. Modestus [erected this].
EDCS-20400120.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sempronia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, aged 40, and was likely set up by Modestus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400140.translation: To the departed spirits. To Quintia, mother of Segges(?), most dutiful, aged 35. Terentia [erected this].
EDCS-20400140.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Quintia, the most dutiful mother of Segges(?), who died at age 35, erected by Terentia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400142.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul III refecit a Clunia milia passuum IIII\n
EDCS-20400142.translation: Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, supreme pontiff, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, rebuilt [this road] from Clunia for four miles.
EDCS-20400142.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian's rebuilding of a road segment near Clunia, dating to his third consulship, likely around 119-121 AD.

EDCS-20400144.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Sertorius Paternus, aged 60. Sertoria Septimina [erected this] for her most dutiful husband, and for herself, aged 40.
EDCS-20400144.summary: This funerary epitaph records Sertoria Septimina dedicating a tomb to her husband Lucius Sertorius Paternus and reserving a space for herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400206.summary: This is a simple dedication naming Aurolus, son of Veniatioq., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400210.text_edition: \n Euodus Marcelli Parthenis Marcelli Rustica Marcelli hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Euodus sibi uxori socrae curavit\n
EDCS-20400210.translation: Euodus, Marcellus, Parthenis, Marcellus, Rustica, Marcellus are buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Euodus arranged [this] for himself, his wife, and his mother-in-law.
EDCS-20400210.summary: This epitaph records the burial of several individuals, including Euodus who arranged the burial for himself, his wife, and mother-in-law, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400211.translation: Valeria Afra [dedicated this] to Hercules, according to her vow.
EDCS-20400211.summary: This is a votive dedication by Valeria Afra to the god Hercules, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400216.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Retucenus Telassicus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400220.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Annius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Juno, aged 60. Iulia [his wife/daughter], aged 50. Gaius Annius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe. Marcus Annius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, priest of Hercules, aged 24. Lucius Annius [son of...], Sextus Annius [son of...]. Here they lie. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for several members of the Annius family, likely a father and his sons, and possibly a wife or daughter, who served as priests, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400221.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saturna made this for her Flaccus.
EDCS-20400221.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, set up by Saturna for her deceased husband, Flaccus.

EDCS-20400223.translation: [text illegible] aged 19, he lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400223.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, recording the age of the deceased and a standard closing sentiment.

EDCS-20400224.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus consul XI imperator XIIII tribunicia potestate XVI pontifex maximus \n
EDCS-20400224.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 14th time, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, high priest.
EDCS-20400224.summary: This text is an official titulature of Emperor Augustus, listing his numerous honors and magistracies, likely from a public inscription during his reign (27 BCE - 14 CE).

EDCS-20400225.text_edition: \n Marco Clodio Marci filio Galeria Flacco IIviro bis flamini tribuno militum legionis IIII Flaviae viro praestantissimo et civi optimo ob plurima erga rem publicam suam merita decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-20400225.translation: To Marcus Clodius Flaccus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, twice chief magistrate, priest, military tribune of the IIII Flavia legion, a most outstanding man and an excellent citizen, for his very many services to his state, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400225.summary: A civic honorific inscription honoring Marcus Clodius Flaccus for his public service and dignities, likely from a Roman provincial town during the Imperial period.

EDCS-20400238.translation: Caius Publicius [name illegible]ianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400238.summary: A votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Caius Publicius [name illegible]ianus.

EDCS-20400239.translation: Gaius C. Senecianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400239.summary: A votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius C. Senecianus.

EDCS-20400274.text_edition: \n Sei quaeris nomen consiste et percipe pauca dum vixei et potuei Pontiliena et Pontilieni liberta et filia eadem moriens et fui et sum epistolium vale\n
EDCS-20400274.translation: If you ask my name, stand still and perceive a few things: while I lived and could, I was Pontiliena, freedwoman and daughter of Pontilienus; dying, I was and am both. Farewell.
EDCS-20400274.summary: A self-penned epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, attributed to Pontiliena, who describes her identity as a freedwoman and daughter, and bids farewell.

EDCS-20400286.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Severo Pio F(elici) Invicto Nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-20400286.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Severus Pius Felix Invictus Nobilissimus Caesar.
EDCS-20400286.summary: An acclamatory inscription from the late Roman Empire, addressing a Caesar, likely honoring the emperor or a prominent member of the imperial family.

EDCS-20400296.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Iunia Materna Cai filia Aemiliano marito annorum LXX et Aemilio Materno filio annorum XXV\n
EDCS-20400296.translation: To the Infernal Divine Shades. Iunia Materna, daughter of Caius, [sets this up] for her husband Aemilianus, aged 70, and for her son Aemilius Maternus, aged 25.
EDCS-20400296.summary: A funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, set up by Iunia Materna for her deceased husband Aemilianus and son Aemilius Maternus.

EDCS-20400299.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli \n
EDCS-20400299.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-20400299.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Constantine I, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400369.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maxsimiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V proconsuli\n
EDCS-20400369.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul five times, proconsul.
EDCS-20400369.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400374.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maxsimiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V proconsuli\n
EDCS-20400374.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul five times, proconsul.
EDCS-20400374.summary: This is a formal imperial titulature for Emperor Galerius Valerius Maxsimianus, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-20400376.translation: To the departed spirits. Antestia Titulla Sempronia, 20 years old, who is buried here, erected this monument to Titulla Sempronia. Minicia Felicisima placed [it].
EDCS-20400376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Minicia Felicisima erected a tomb for the 20-year-old Antestia Titulla Sempronia.

EDCS-20400377.translation: Pergamis, slave of Lucius O() C(), is buried here, 30 years old. Helius and his daughter Musice made [this monument] for you, companion.
EDCS-20400377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pergamis, a slave, erected by her companion Helius and daughter Musice.

EDCS-20400413.summary: This is a very abbreviated funerary inscription recording the name Iulius and his age of 18.

EDCS-20400419.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Imperatori Caesari Valerio][3 [] [] [] [] [] [] bono rei publicae nato\n
EDCS-20400419.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar Valerius [---], born for the good of the state.
EDCS-20400419.summary: This is an incomplete imperial dedication, likely to Emperor Valerius, celebrating his birth for the state's benefit.

EDCS-20400423.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or a tombstone, mentioning the names Baebia and Claudia.

EDCS-20400443.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription recording the name Efrem and his age of 50.

EDCS-20400463.translation: To Virnon, son of Turonus, 60 years old.
EDCS-20400463.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Virnon, son of Turonus, at the age of 60.

EDCS-20400467.translation: Vibius Turinus set this up by Jupiter's command.
EDCS-20400467.summary: A private votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Vibius Turinus, likely a small shrine or offering.

EDCS-20400469.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-20400469.summary: A dedication to Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, likely erected during his lifetime or in his memory.

EDCS-20400472.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesari Gaio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III patri patriae consuli II proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Afenio Gallo Veldumiano Volusiano Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli \n
EDCS-20400472.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afenius Gallus Veldumianus Volusianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul.
EDCS-20400472.summary: A dedication to co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, recording their titles and offices, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-20400478.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Petronia Lupina, his wife, had this made for Leopardo, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-20400478.summary: A funerary epitaph for Leopardo, who died at age 25, erected by his wife Petronia Lupina.

EDCS-20400479.translation: To the departed spirits. Nardenus set up this monument for his wife Serenus and himself.
EDCS-20400479.summary: A funerary monument set up by Nardenus for his wife Serenus and himself.

EDCS-20400495.translation: Under Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 15th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 8th time, and Caesar Domitian, son of the deified Vespasian, consul for the 7th time. The Via Nova was built, 14 miles from Bracara, by Gaius Calpetanus Rantius Quirinalis Valerius Festus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power.
